source.subject: Coordinating CIO/VP presence for NovaPower demo — availability check
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira@novapower.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: IT-Provisioning <it-provisioning@novapower.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the great call yesterday. As we finalize the agenda with our execs, our CIO Mark DeLong and VP of Ops want to attend a short sponsor-level demo to validate fit before we lock in a technical follow-up. Can you confirm whether Redwood can have an exec-appropriate overview (30–45 min) that covers SLA/hosting options, data controls, and a high-level cost/throughput slide?

Preferred windows from our side:
- Tue Jul 20: 9:00–10:00 AM PT
- Wed Jul 21: 2:00–3:00 PM PT

I attached a one-page agenda for reference. If a deeper tech breakout is needed, we can schedule a separate 60-minute slot.

Would appreciate confirmation of who from Redwood would join (VP/Head level) so our execs know who to expect.

Best,
Samira Khan
Head of Platform Partnerships
NovaPower

[attachment: NovaPower_Demo_Agenda_v1.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@novapower.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 09:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Hi Samira — thanks for sending the agenda. We can absolutely run a concise sponsor-level session that covers hosting modes, SLAs, and the unit economics primer.

Proposed attendees from Redwood:
- Marcus Lin, VP of Engineering (sponsor-level coverage on runtime and resilience)
- Rachel Kim, Solutions Engineer (short walkthrough and Q&A)
- Myself (product & commercial questions)

I have a potential calendar conflict the morning of Jul 20 but Marcus is usually flexible. Would a compressed 30–45 minute window on Jul 20 from 9:30–10:15 AM PT work instead of the 9:00 slot? If Mark (CIO) prefers the later 2:00 PM slot on Jul 21, we can do that — either way we can prepare a 30-minute core demo + 15-minute optional Q&A.

Attached a slightly trimmed agenda (for the 30-min format). Please confirm the preferred slot and whether Mark wants any pre-read.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Director, Solutions
Redwood Inference

[attachment: Demo_1pager.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Ava — I can join the sponsor session. Quick note: I have a leadership sync that morning that often runs long. If we aim for 9:30–10:15 AM PT on Jul 20 that should work better for me (gives a 30–45 minute buffer). If NovaPower needs later in the day on Jul 21, I can make time then too.

Marcus
VP, Engineering
Redwood Inference

From: Samira Khan <samira@novapower.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark DeLong <mdelong@novapower.com>, IT-Provisioning <it-provisioning@novapower.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Thanks all — apologies for the back-and-forth. Mark confirms he can do Wed Jul 21 at 2:00 PM PT but he prefers the later slot to accommodate an upstream review. However, given Marcus's availability and Ava's suggestion, we'd like to propose this hybrid approach:

- Wed Jul 21 2:00–2:30 PM PT: sponsor-level session (Mark + VP-level attendees)
- Wed Jul 21 2:30–3:00 PM PT: optional tech Q&A with Marcus and Rachel if needed

If Redwood can commit Marcus for the optional Q&A immediately after the sponsor walkthrough, we can keep both the exec presence and the deeper technical conversation in one contiguous block. Please confirm and we will send a calendar invite (I will include a short mutual action plan).

Also attaching the mutual action plan that legal asked to see ahead of time — it's lightweight and includes the items we'll discuss in the meeting.

Confirming: Mark DeLong (CIO) will attend the sponsor slot.

Best,
Samira

[attachment: Mutual_Action_Plan.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

source.subject: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Team — kicking off the mid-quarter readout thread. Attached is the short deck for the stakeholder readout (slide notes included) and a spreadsheet that lists the proposed re-scopes + suggested dependency owners. High level asks here: 

1) Confirm which scope reductions we approve today (see slide 3: "non-critical infra experiments" & "incubation UX flow") by EOD Thu 7/16.
2) Sign up dependency owners in the spreadsheet for any item marked "needs owner" — owners should be internal (no external handoffs without a partner contact).
3) Vivek / Marcus: call out any infra or model-serving blockers that would prevent a safe QBR roll-forward.
4) Naomi: please confirm whether leadership wants this as a focused 15-min readout or a 30-min Q&A for execs.

Quick notes in the deck: proposed latency optimization sprint can be delayed one sprint if we reassign two infra FTEs to the routing work. The risk is higher per-token cost in short term; I called it out on slide 5.

Attachments: 20260714-midq-readout-deck-v1.pdf (deck), dependency-owners-2026Q3.xlsx (living owner matrix)

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Head of Product Operations | Redwood Inference
+aisha.rahman@redwood.com

-----

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Thanks Aisha — quick scan and a few immediate flags: 

- Routing work: the proposed reassign of two infra FTEs will unblock the routing proofs, but we need CloudOps partner sign-off for the private VPC tests (see row 7 in the spreadsheet). I'll ping CloudOps; without sign-off the earliest integration window is 8/2.
- KV cache experiment: this is low risk but depends on a model-regression eval that Research needs to run. Marcus, can you confirm if we have the eval harness ready?
- Capacity: if we shift the latency sprint out, we need to extend the dedicate-capacity burn rate mitigation from the Optimize playbook (I can author a short ops plan).

I will loop in partners@cloudops.co to clarify test windows. For owners: I'll take on "routing external fallbacks" and put a comment in the spreadsheet.

-Vivek
Director, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

-----On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 09:12 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — kicking off the mid-quarter readout thread. Attached is the short deck for the stakeholder readout (slide notes included) and a spreadsheet that lists the proposed re-scopes + suggested dependency owners. High level asks here: ...

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Brief update from infra/model side: 

- Eval harness: we have a baseline harness but it needs 2 days to run the new regression suite for the KV cache change. I can prioritize this to finish by 7/16; results to be posted in the Drive folder.
- Dedicated capacity: shifting latency work will increase short-term token cost ~6-8% for Q3 until we re-run the kernel selection pass. That matches Vivek's note.
- Resource ask: to keep QBR messaging clean, I recommend we mark the "latency sprint" as "deferred one sprint" and add a mitigation bullet (low-cost cache tweak + tighter batching). I updated the deck slide 5 and added a one-slide mitigation note.

I'll mark the "needs owner" rows for Eval and Capacity in the spreadsheet and add ETA comments. Marcus

-----On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 10:03 AM Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Aisha — quick scan and a few immediate flags: ...

From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@stellarpay.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 07:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Aisha — thanks for the deck and the heads-up. From StellarPay's side: 

- We can accept the deferred latency sprint as long as the routing fallback guarantee remains in place for our peak weekend tests (Aug 14-16). We need a confirmed fallback plan (owner + runbook) by 7/30.
- CloudOps sign-off: we'll need a named contact on the VPC tests; please let us know who that will be so we can schedule mutual testing windows.
- QBR: a 15-min focused readout is fine on our end if you include the mitigation bullets and the short contingency runbook.

Happy to jump on a 20-min sync next week to walk through the runbook.

—Sarah Nguyen
Head of Platform — StellarPay

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@stellarpay.com>, CloudOps Consulting <partners@cloudops.co>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Thanks everyone — consolidation and next steps below.

Decisions:
- Approve deferral of the Latency Sprint by one sprint. Public-facing QBR message: "Timing adjusted to prioritize routing resiliency and partner integration; mitigation plan in place."
- Owners assigned (spreadsheet updated): Vivek — routing fallbacks & cloud partner coordination; Marcus — eval harness & capacity ETA; Naomi — stakeholder readout cadence and exec invite list.

Action items (owners / due dates):
- Vivek: confirm CloudOps named contact and schedule VPC test window by 7/20. (Assigned)
- Marcus: run regression suite for KV cache and post results to Drive by 7/16. (Assigned)
- Naomi: confirm whether leadership prefers 15-min or 30-min slot by 7/18; draft invite language for execs. (Assigned)
- Aisha: final QBR deck update (slide 5 mitigation) and send final readout deck to StellarPay by 7/20. (Assigned)

StellarPay asks captured: fallback runbook owner + runbook by 7/30 for Aug stress window.

Attachments referenced: 20260714-midq-readout-deck-v1.pdf, dependency-owners-2026Q3.xlsx

I'll follow-up with a short calendar invite once Naomi confirms timing. Thanks for quick turnarounds on the eval and partner sign-off.

—Aisha
Head of Product Operations | Redwood Inference
+aisha.rahman@redwood.com

Quick notes in the deck: proposed latency optimization sprint can be delayed one sprint if we reassign two infra FTEs to the routing work. The risk is higher per-token cost in short term; I called it out on slide 5.

Attachments: 20260714-midq-readout-deck-v1.pdf (deck), dependency-owners-2026Q3.xlsx (living owner matrix)

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Head of Product Operations | Redwood Inference
+aisha.rahman@redwood.com

-----


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Thanks Aisha — quick scan and a few immediate flags: 

- Routing work: the proposed reassign of two infra FTEs will unblock the routing proofs, but we need CloudOps partner sign-off for the private VPC tests (see row 7 in the spreadsheet). I'll ping CloudOps; without sign-off the earliest integration window is 8/2.
- KV cache experiment: this is low risk but depends on a model-regression eval that Research needs to run. Marcus, can you confirm if we have the eval harness ready?
- Capacity: if we shift the latency sprint out, we need to extend the dedicate-capacity burn rate mitigation from the Optimize playbook (I can author a short ops plan).

I will loop in partners@cloudops.co to clarify test windows. For owners: I'll take on \"routing external fallbacks\" and put a comment in the spreadsheet.

-Vivek
Director, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

-----On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 09:12 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — kicking off the mid-quarter readout thread. Attached is the short deck for the stakeholder readout (slide notes included) and a spreadsheet that lists the proposed re-scopes + suggested dependency owners. High level asks here: ...

To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Brief update from infra/model side: 

- Eval harness: we have a baseline harness but it needs 2 days to run the new regression suite for the KV cache change. I can prioritize this to finish by 7/16; results to be posted in the Drive folder.
- Dedicated capacity: shifting latency work will increase short-term token cost ~6-8% for Q3 until we re-run the kernel selection pass. That matches Vivek's note.
- Resource ask: to keep QBR messaging clean, I recommend we mark the \"latency sprint\" as \"deferred one sprint\" and add a mitigation bullet (low-cost cache tweak + tighter batching). I updated the deck slide 5 and added a one-slide mitigation note.

I'll mark the \"needs owner\" rows for Eval and Capacity in the spreadsheet and add ETA comments. Marcus

-----On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 10:03 AM Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Aisha — quick scan and a few immediate flags: ...

Subject: Re: Mid-quarter stakeholder readout — scope shift proposals & dependency owners

Aisha — thanks for the deck and the heads-up. From StellarPay's side: 

- We can accept the deferred latency sprint as long as the routing fallback guarantee remains in place for our peak weekend tests (Aug 14-16). We need a confirmed fallback plan (owner + runbook) by 7/30.
- CloudOps sign-off: we'll need a named contact on the VPC tests; please let us know who that will be so we can schedule mutual testing windows.

Happy to jump on a 20-min sync next week to walk through the runbook.

—Sarah Nguyen
Head of Platform — StellarPay

- Owners assigned (spreadsheet updated): Vivek — routing fallbacks & cloud partner coordination; Marcus — eval harness & capacity ETA; Naomi — stakeholder readout cadence and exec invite list.

Action items (owners / due dates):

- Marcus: run regression suite for KV cache and post results to Drive by 7/16. (Assigned)
- Naomi: confirm whether leadership prefers 15-min or 30-min slot by 7/18; draft invite language for execs. (Assigned)

StellarPay asks captured: fallback runbook owner + runbook by 7/30 for Aug stress window.

Attachments referenced: 20260714-midq-readout-deck-v1.pdf, dependency-owners-2026Q3.xlsx

source.subject: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed
messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T08:14:00Z
Subject: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Team —

Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts. Key asks from Novus:
- 2x nominal RPS for first 48 hours (expected peak: 220 RPS, baseline 110)
- Temporary increase to token throughput for streaming requests (peak ~1.6M tokens/min)
- Warmstart KV cache retention for conversation sessions (they expect 10–15s between follow-up calls)

I need us to confirm feasibility and the operational checklist: entitlements from Billing, IP whitelist, route tag + canary rollout, and an emergency throttling rollback plan. Priya — what product toggles do we need to enable for warmstart retention and fallback model variant? Karthik — can infra support a 2x uplift for 48 hours with a safe drain plan? Marcus/Evelyn — any telemetry/alerting we should pre-configure in Console?

I've attached their forecast spreadsheet and the entitlement request they emailed. I'll loop in Novus after we agree on the internal playbook.

Thanks,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: novus_warmstart_forecast.xlsx (spreadsheet), entitlement_request_novus.pdf (PDF)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement:

1) Warmstart KV semantics: we can increase per-session KV retention to 60s for the pilot but it will impact memory usage on the serving pool. We should scope which routes actually need this — recommend enabling for the /chat/stream route only.

2) Fallback model variant: we can prepare a quantized 8-bit variant (same architecture) that reduces GPU token cost ~40%. We need ~24 hours to validate quality on their smoke prompts and to add the variant to the routing policy.

3) Throttle window & quotas: product can support a timebound ephemeral entitlement (48 hours max) that maps to a temp rate limit and a post-window automatic rollback. We should specify exact start/end UTC times in the entitlement doc.

Questions:
- Expected percent of requests that are streaming vs non-streaming? The forecast lists tokens but not streaming mix.
- Do they plan long-running streaming sessions (>30s)? That changes KV and connection resource planning.

If we get answers by EOD we can ship the fallback model and the entitlement change in time.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Manager, Inference

On 2026-07-13 08:14, Avery Johnson wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts.
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: 

- Pool capacity: current dedicated pool for pilot-grade tenants has ~30% spare headroom; pushing to 2x will require borrowing from the shared burst pool. That is feasible but we need an explicit borrow-and-return schedule to avoid impacting other pilots.

- KV cache retention: increasing retention to 60s is ok for the /chat/stream route only. We'll need to set per-route cache limits (max entries per session) — recommend max 256 tokens stored per session as a guardrail.

- Canary & rollback: We'll publish a route tag (novus-pilot-48h) and do a canary rollout at 10% for the first hour. If error rate or 95p latency increases beyond 2x baseline we'll auto-roll back. We need Product to sign off on the rollback SLA thresholds.

- Whitelisting: Novus must provide egress IP ranges for their client traffic (or we can accept a JWT assertion). Marcus — please coordinate with Network for the 2–3 CIDR blocks.

I'll push a runbook for ops and a short playbook for on-call to the shared drive once we confirm times.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineering / Infra

On 2026-07-13 09:02, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement...
From: Alice Park <alice@novus.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudOps <cloudops@novus.ai>, Procurement <procurement@novus.ai>
Date: 2026-07-13T13:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Hi Avery —

Thanks for coordinating. Quick answers from our side: 
- Streaming vs non-streaming: ~70% streaming (short bursts), 30% normal request/response. Median session ~12s, 95p ~45s.
- Ramp schedule: 2026-07-18 02:00–08:00 UTC (nights), 2026-07-19 scaled ramp 00:00–10:00 UTC. We'll provide a CSV with our expected IP egress ranges by EOD today.
- Procurement will review the entitlement doc; they asked whether we can get a one-time credit if we exceed the uptick during the pilot window (they want a safety net).

We appreciate the fallback variant offer — would like the 8-bit option on standby.

Best,
Alice Park
Head of Product, Novus AI

On Tue, Jul 13, 2026 at 08:14 AM Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday...
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-14T11:20:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Thanks all — summary and action items below (agree or correct):

1) Entitlement: Billing to issue ephemeral entitlement for 48 hours (start 2026-07-18T02:00Z, end 2026-07-20T02:00Z). Temporary limit: 2x RPS (baseline 110 -> 220) and token throughput cap + post-window auto-rollback. (owner: Sean Gallagher)

2) Warmstart & KV: Product to enable 60s KV retention for /chat/stream only, with per-session token guardrail (256 tokens). Also prepare 8-bit fallback variant and add to route policy (owner: Priya Natarajan).

3) Infra & Canary: Use route tag novus-pilot-48h; canary at 10% for first hour, rollback thresholds: error rate +95p latency > 2x baseline (owner: Karthik Iyer).

4) Network & Whitelist: Novus will provide CIDR list; Network to apply (owner: Marcus Lin).

5) Observability: Pre-create a Console dashboard with token-rate, 95/99 latency, error-rate by route and session-duration histogram (owner: Evelyn Hart).

6) Commercial: Procurement asked about a one-time credit if pilot overshoots the uplift window. Sean — can you scope an onetime safety credit or a short-term surcharge cap? (owner: Sean Gallagher)

7) Docs/Runbook: Karthik will publish ops runbook and on-call playbook; QA will run smoke checks with Novus prompts using the 8-bit variant (owner: Karthik Iyer).

I'll reply-all to Novus once we have confirmed the whitelist CIDRs and Sean confirms the entitlement doc.

Avery

On 2026-07-13 10:45, Karthik Iyer wrote:
> We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: ...
From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-14T16:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Avery — quick commercial note: deal id DEAL-7842. I can approve a one-time safety credit up to $2,000 for the pilot overshoot, or we can offer a surcharge cap of 15% above list during the entitlement window. I'll raise the entitlement ticket once Network confirms CIDRs and once we have the exact UTC window. I'm attaching the DocuSign link for the ephemeral entitlement addendum.

DocuSign: https://docusign.com/novus-entitlement-12345
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/7842

—Sean
Sean Gallagher
Sales Operations, Redwood Inference

If we get answers by EOD we can ship the fallback model and the entitlement change in time.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Manager, Inference

On 2026-07-13 08:14, Avery Johnson wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts.


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: 

- Pool capacity: current dedicated pool for pilot-grade tenants has ~30% spare headroom; pushing to 2x will require borrowing from the shared burst pool. That is feasible but we need an explicit borrow-and-return schedule to avoid impacting other pilots.

- KV cache retention: increasing retention to 60s is ok for the /chat/stream route only. We'll need to set per-route cache limits (max entries per session) — recommend max 256 tokens stored per session as a guardrail.

- Canary & rollback: We'll publish a route tag (novus-pilot-48h) and do a canary rollout at 10% for the first hour. If error rate or 95p latency increases beyond 2x baseline we'll auto-roll back. We need Product to sign off on the rollback SLA thresholds.

- Whitelisting: Novus must provide egress IP ranges for their client traffic (or we can accept a JWT assertion). Marcus — please coordinate with Network for the 2–3 CIDR blocks.

I'll push a runbook for ops and a short playbook for on-call to the shared drive once we confirm times.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineering / Infra

On 2026-07-13 09:02, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement...


From: Alice Park <alice@novus.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudOps <cloudops@novus.ai>, Procurement <procurement@novus.ai>
Date: 2026-07-13T13:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Hi Avery —

Thanks for coordinating. Quick answers from our side: 
- Streaming vs non-streaming: ~70% streaming (short bursts), 30% normal request/response. Median session ~12s, 95p ~45s.
- Ramp schedule: 2026-07-18 02:00–08:00 UTC (nights), 2026-07-19 scaled ramp 00:00–10:00 UTC. We'll provide a CSV with our expected IP egress ranges by EOD today.
- Procurement will review the entitlement doc; they asked whether we can get a one-time credit if we exceed the uptick during the pilot window (they want a safety net).

We appreciate the fallback variant offer — would like the 8-bit option on standby.

Best,
Alice Park
Head of Product, Novus AI

On Tue, Jul 13, 2026 at 08:14 AM Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday...


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-14T11:20:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Thanks all — summary and action items below (agree or correct):

1) Entitlement: Billing to issue ephemeral entitlement for 48 hours (start 2026-07-18T02:00Z, end 2026-07-20T02:00Z). Temporary limit: 2x RPS (baseline 110 -> 220) and token throughput cap + post-window auto-rollback. (owner: Sean Gallagher)

2) Warmstart & KV: Product to enable 60s KV retention for /chat/stream only, with per-session token guardrail (256 tokens). Also prepare 8-bit fallback variant and add to route policy (owner: Priya Natarajan).

3) Infra & Canary: Use route tag novus-pilot-48h; canary at 10% for first hour, rollback thresholds: error rate +95p latency > 2x baseline (owner: Karthik Iyer).

4) Network & Whitelist: Novus will provide CIDR list; Network to apply (owner: Marcus Lin).

5) Observability: Pre-create a Console dashboard with token-rate, 95/99 latency, error-rate by route and session-duration histogram (owner: Evelyn Hart).

6) Commercial: Procurement asked about a one-time credit if pilot overshoots the uplift window. Sean — can you scope an onetime safety credit or a short-term surcharge cap? (owner: Sean Gallagher)

7) Docs/Runbook: Karthik will publish ops runbook and on-call playbook; QA will run smoke checks with Novus prompts using the 8-bit variant (owner: Karthik Iyer).

I'll reply-all to Novus once we have confirmed the whitelist CIDRs and Sean confirms the entitlement doc.

Avery

On 2026-07-13 10:45, Karthik Iyer wrote:
> We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: ...


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>

Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-14T16:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Avery — quick commercial note: deal id DEAL-7842. I can approve a one-time safety credit up to $2,000 for the pilot overshoot, or we can offer a surcharge cap of 15% above list during the entitlement window. I'll raise the entitlement ticket once Network confirms CIDRs and once we have the exact UTC window. I'm attaching the DocuSign link for the ephemeral entitlement addendum.

DocuSign: https://docusign.com/novus-entitlement-12345
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/7842

—Sean

source.subject: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

Hi Kim / Marissa —

Quick summary: NimbusHealth is seeing a sustained p95 increase for inference on model_phi v3.14 that started after the 2026-07-03 canary push. Their production p95 climbed from ~220ms to ~560ms for a subset of long-sequence traffic (~5–6% of requests). They are requesting a temporary SLA tolerance while we investigate.

I attached the p95 export and perf graphs (taken across their prod region us-west-2). Key ask: they want a provisional 72-hour tolerance on p95 (to avoid automatic downstream throttles) and a traffic pin to keep them on the older variant if we push a fast rollback.

What I need from you:
- Kim: authorization to approve a provisional SLA tolerance window for NimbusHealth (72 hours) OR a shorter 48-hour conditional window if we can commit to a rollback plan within that time.
- Marissa: advice on customer credit implications if we approve the tolerance, and whether legal should be looped for a carveout addendum.
- Marcus: quick ops checklist for a safe rollback and rollback-if-unresolved trigger.

Attachments: 2026-07-04_nimbus_p95_report.csv, model_phi_v3.14_perf_graphs.pdf

Proposed immediate mitigation steps (I can coordinate with Nimbus SRE):
- Pin 20% of their traffic to model_phi v3.13 (previous variant) as a fallback path (traffic fencing).
- Activate prefix KV caching for their hot prefixes (estimated ~10–15% improvement on those requests).
- Stand up an elevated monitoring dashboard (15-minute cadence) and mobile alerting to on-call.

I'll be on call this morning — can hop on quick with SRE and Nimbus if helpful.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

--
Attachment(s): 2026-07-04_nimbus_p95_report.csv (csv), model_phi_v3.14_perf_graphs.pdf (pdf)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

Thanks for the heads-up, Ben. I reviewed the attached CSV and the PDF plots — the delta is non-trivial and concentrated on long-sequence requests, which aligns with our kernel/attenuation hypothesis for v3.14.

A few quick clarifying Qs before I can approve anything:
- Is the increase isolated to their region (us-west-2) or global? (the graphs look regional but I want confirmation)
- What's the customer impact in terms of revenue/critical workflows? Are they in a hard real-time path?
- Do we have an immediate rollback candidate that preserves semantic parity (v3.13)?

On tolerances: I'm open to a provisional 48-hour tolerance provided we get a written acceptance from Nimbus that they understand potential SLA credit consequences. If we can't produce a rollback plan that includes a tested canary within 24 hours, I won't sign a 72-hour blanket tolerance.

Marissa — can you size potential credits for a 48 vs 72-hour tolerance? If legal needs to draft a short-term carveout, tell me and I'll approve the emergency sign-off workflow.

Kim
Kimberly Park
Head of Sales Operations | Redwood Inference

On Sat, Jul 4, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick summary: NimbusHealth is seeing a sustained p95 increase for inference on model_phi v3.14...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 11:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

Kim / Ben —

Thanks for the detail. I agree with Kim's caution. From a finance/contracting perspective:
- A 48-hour tolerance with a documented rollback trigger is preferable. For a high-dollar account like Nimbus (NB-4832), a 72-hour blanket tolerance will likely require a short amendment and a recorded customer signoff.
- Estimated credit exposure if we grant a 48h tolerance: ~$3.2K (prorated per SLA terms). For 72h, ~$6.8K + potential churn risk if they lose confidence.

Conditions for my provisional approval (48 hours):
1) Marcus to publish a rollback playbook (30 mins) that includes a tested canary and an automated traffic pin.
2) Ben to secure a written acceptance from Katelyn (Nimbus SRE) confirming they agree to the provisional tolerance and understand credit implications.
3) Engineering (on-call infra) to commit to a 12-hour hotfix window or an explicit rollback if root cause not found.

Marcus — can you drop the playbook draft into the Drive link and confirm that the rollback is scripted (not manual) so we can avoid human delay?

Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Success & Renewals | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

On Sat, Jul 4, 2026 at 10:05 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks for the heads-up, Ben...

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>, ops@nimbushealth.com
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

All —

I pulled a quick ops runbook and dropped a draft rollback_playbook_draft.md into the shared Drive folder (link in related links). Summary of the actionable items I can complete within the next 6 hours:
- Scripted rollback to v3.13 with traffic fencing baked in (automated route changes + canary at 5% then 20%).
- Smoke tests against Nimbus's long-sequence workloads (10 test sequences) — results expected within 45 minutes.
- Rapid rollback trigger: if p95 for the canary >350ms for two consecutive 10-minute windows we auto-execute the rollback.

I recommend approving the provisional 48-hour tolerance with the three conditions Marissa listed. I will also tag infra on-call and assign an incident owner.

Ben — please ask Katelyn to reply here with explicit acceptance (one-liner is fine) so I can flip the automation to the canary mode. Kim / Marissa — if you each approve that path, I'll flip the switches and commence validation.

Marcus Lin
Sr. Infrastructure Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

On Sat, Jul 4, 2026 at 11:23 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Kim / Ben —
> 
> Thanks for the detail...

source.subject: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Team —

Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. The deal is at signature (GF-3382) and procurement has signaled a hard gate: the pilot must demonstrate per-tenant QoS (latency SLO enforcement) and a short-term model residency/pinning guarantee for a 10-day activation window. They want this validated before committing the first monthly payment.

Key asks (from GF):
- Pin a model variant and provide a tenant-level QoS policy that limits end-to-end p95 latency to 250ms for a capped RPS.
- A short-term residency/pinning guarantee for the pilot (10 days) with rollback policy.
- A telemetry snapshot and acceptance test plan they can run during the pilot.

Why this matters: If we can offer a clear, timeboxed pin + QoS approach we keep GF in the deal (ACV $560k ARR annualized) and open the account to Dedicated expansion. They are sensitive to noisy neighbors and want deterministic performance for an internal trading workflow.

Proposed commercial trade: short-term SLA addendum for the 10-day pilot in exchange for commit to a Dedicated trial and a 6-month minimum if pilot meets acceptance criteria. I captured the mutual action plan and a compact requirements sheet (attached).

Asks for Product/Eng:
- Can we:
  1) Define a minimal implementation for tenant QoS enforcement that is safe to ship as a pilot (configurable policy + documented rollback).
  2) Approve an engineering timebox (estimate) and a tentative ETA for a pilot-ready config (non-invasive, feature-flagged path).

Timeline: GF expects pilot readiness week of 7/12. That gives ~2 weeks for an implementation plan + prioritization decision.

Requesting a quick sync to triage tradeoffs and document the minimal acceptance criteria. If this is a no-go, I need to tee up a fallback conversation with GF about a staged performance validation (paid POC).

Attaching: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf, galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

On Mon, 29 Jun 2026 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. ...

Thanks for the clear summary, Ben. I reviewed the attached MAP and requirement sheet. A few clarifying points before I commit Eng budget/time: we need to be explicit about scope so we don't expand into a full multi-tenant QoS system for the pilot.

Suggested minimal scope (for pilot):
- Policy surface: route-level token that allows us to set a latency target and max concurrent requests per tenant (config only; enforcement via queueing + backpressure).
- Residency: a pinned worker pool assignment for the tenant for the 10-day window (best-effort on the first run; explicit fallback to shared pool if capacity drops).
- Observability: a telemetry snapshot + an automated acceptance test (RPS/latency runbook) that GF runs with our SRE.

Questions for Eng:
1) Marcus — rough estimate to implement a configurable route-level QoS token + basic backpressure enforcement (safe, feature-flagged path)?
2) Priyom — any existing infra that can support a short-term residency pin at the tenant layer without requiring a full tenancy isolation change?
3) Jordan — will the proposed approach require schema or SDK changes that client teams must pick up for the pilot?

If we can get rough estimates today, I can scope a short runbook and provide Ben an ETA to share with GF. If estimates look >2w, we should discuss the paid POC fallback.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Serving Platform

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>, procurement@galaxyfinance.com
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Thanks, Laura — really helpful framing. Short answers inline to help prioritization: 

- Business impact: GF moves to signature upon receiving a deterministic pilot plan. If we miss the 7/12 readiness window they will push the deal and consider alternative vendors. This is a one-time strategic account with clear expansion potential.
- Commercial: GF has agreed to the short-term SLA addendum language; procurement is ready to sign once we confirm the technical approach and ETA.

Marcus / Priyom / Jordan — we need very rough T-shirt sizing today if possible (S / M / L + estimated engineering days). Laura has suggested a conservative minimal scope: route-level latency token + best-effort pinned worker pool + acceptance telemetry. Please reply with sizing and any blockers. If any of these are L (>3w), flag it and we will propose the paid POC alternative to GF (they indicated willingness).

Next steps I will take after your replies:
- Draft a short internal prioritization note for Eng leadership with expected impact and tradeoffs.
- If approved, coordinate a canary/feature-flag rollout and schedule the GF acceptance window (7/12 preferred).

Oliver / GF team — appreciate patience; pushing internally now and will confirm by EOD tomorrow.

Ben
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment stubs: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf (application/pdf); galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Team —

Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. The deal is at signature (GF-3382) and procurement has signaled a hard gate: the pilot must demonstrate per-tenant QoS (latency SLO enforcement) and a short-term model residency/pinning guarantee for a 10-day activation window. They want this validated before committing the first monthly payment.

Key asks (from GF):
- Pin a model variant and provide a tenant-level QoS policy that limits end-to-end p95 latency to 250ms for a capped RPS.
- A short-term residency/pinning guarantee for the pilot (10 days) with rollback policy.
- A telemetry snapshot and acceptance test plan they can run during the pilot.

Why this matters: If we can offer a clear, timeboxed pin + QoS approach we keep GF in the deal (ACV $560k ARR annualized) and open the account to Dedicated expansion. They are sensitive to noisy neighbors and want deterministic performance for an internal trading workflow.

Proposed commercial trade: short-term SLA addendum for the 10-day pilot in exchange for commit to a Dedicated trial and a 6-month minimum if pilot meets acceptance criteria. I captured the mutual action plan and a compact requirements sheet (attached).

Asks for Product/Eng:
- Can we:
  1) Define a minimal implementation for tenant QoS enforcement that is safe to ship as a pilot (configurable policy + documented rollback).
  2) Approve an engineering timebox (estimate) and a tentative ETA for a pilot-ready config (non-invasive, feature-flagged path).

Timeline: GF expects pilot readiness week of 7/12. That gives ~2 weeks for an implementation plan + prioritization decision.

Requesting a quick sync to triage tradeoffs and document the minimal acceptance criteria. If this is a no-go, I need to tee up a fallback conversation with GF about a staged performance validation (paid POC).

Attaching: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf, galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot
messages:
From: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medtech.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Hi Alex / Priya,

Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed, the steering committee needs a short mutual action plan so we can lock a pilot decision by mid-July. Two things are blocking the contract: (1) clear hosting recommendation (Hosted API vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment) for our pilot dataset (PHI subset, US-only), and (2) a validated timeline with security gating (SOC 2 evidence + our questionnaire).

Attached is our draft MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx) and our security questionnaire (security_questionnaire.pdf). High level asks from our side:
- Deployment mode recommendation with tradeoffs (latency, cost, data residency, SLA)
- Estimated run costs for a 3-month pilot (token volumes attached in MAP)
- Evaluation gates and minimum success criteria (latency P95, throughput, false-positive rate on redaction)
- Region options — we prefer us-east for latency but need clarification on data residency and encryption in transit/at rest.

Our steering committee wants a firm commit or a clear go/no-go path by 2026-07-15 so procurement can start PO paperwork. Can you send a proposed MAP with milestones + dates and a recommended hosting mode by EOD 2026-06-30?

Thanks —
Brenda
Brenda Long
Head of Platform Partnerships, MedTech Inc.
brenda.long@medtech.com
+1 (415) 555-0129

Attachments: MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), security_questionnaire.pdf (PDF, 12 pages)
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now. High-level response below; I will attach an updated MAP workbook (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) by EOD Tuesday.

Short recommendation (pilot scope):
- Recommended mode: Hosted API (us-east-1) for fastest time-to-value and to avoid initial procurement friction.
  - Pros: no infra procurement, Redwood manages SLAs and autoscaling, fastest onboarding (1–2 weeks).
  - Cons: shared tenancy (we provide strict data isolation controls), possibly higher unit costs vs Dedicated.
- Dedicated capacity: attractive if you need predictable SLOs + cost controls for sustained high QPS; requires reserved capacity contract and ~4–6 week lead time.
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): recommended only if legal requires customer-controlled keys or air-gap. Longer setup (6–10+ weeks) and heavier Ops.

Security gating: we can provide SOC 2 artifacts and a completed questionnaire. We will also propose an encrypted-in-transit and encrypted-at-rest default for the pilot, and offer customer-managed KMS if you need key separation (this pulls the thread toward Dedicated/Private).

Proposed MAP milestones (short):
1) Security review complete (Redwood provides SOC 2 + questionnaire answers) — target: 2026-07-06
2) Technical eval (integration + smoke tests, 7 days) — target window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13
3) Performance gate (latency P95 < 150ms for typical prompt mix OR documented fallback to smaller model) — by 2026-07-13
4) Contract & PO signoff — target: 2026-07-15

To hit these dates we need:
- Rishi / infra team: quick costed estimate for Dedicated reserved pool (3-month pilot profile) and delta vs hosted (units).
- CS (Priya): success criteria & canary plan for integration and data loading.
- MedTech: confirmation on whether customer-managed KMS or standard Redwood keys are acceptable for the pilot.

Looping in Rishi for the cost numbers. Priya — can you draft the canary plan? I will prepare the updated MAP and call out the decisions that shift us between Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

On Mon, Jun 28, 2026 at 3:02 PM Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com> wrote:
> Hi Alex / Priya,
> 
> Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed...
>
> (see attached MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx, security_questionnaire.pdf)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Thanks Alex — happy to own the evaluation plan from CS side. Quick notes on canary & success criteria:

Canary plan (7 days):
- Day 0–1: integration (API keys, test dataset upload — limited to synthetic/hashed PHI)
- Day 2–3: baseline latency and throughput tests (our harness + Redwood streaming)
- Day 4–5: end-to-end functional checks with redaction metrics and downstream consumer validation
- Day 6–7: stakeholder review and go/no-go decision

Success criteria (minimum):
- Latency: P95 < 200ms for typical prompt mix (prefer <150ms)
- Availability: >= 99% during canary window
- Redaction/PII handling: automated redaction passes 95% of vetted prompts
- Cost threshold: pilot projected cost + 20% buffer <= $12k/month (per MAP numbers)

If Hosted path is chosen we can be live faster; for Dedicated we should start PO conversations immediately (Rishi — can you provide a ballpark reserved capacity estimate for 3 months by 2026-07-01?).

Thanks —
Priya Natarajan
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

On Tue, Jun 29, 2026 at 9:12 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now...
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: quick estimate for MedTech pilot (dedicated vs hosted)

Rishi / Marcus —

Following MedTech's MAP request (see thread), we need a short internal runbook and two cost points by EOD 2026-07-01:
- Hosted API estimate (3-month pilot): monthly token profile is in the MAP. Provide expected monthly spend and expected p95 latency in us-east-1.
- Dedicated reservation estimate (3-month pilot): upfront reservation delta, monthly burn, and time-to-provision (weeks). Include any special KMS/HSM integration overhead if customer-managed keys are requested.

Constraints: customer will accept Hosted if we can supply SOC 2 + questionnaire and meet latency P95 <150–200ms. If keys are customer-managed, they prefer Dedicated or Private.

Please paste short bullets here; I will fold into the MAP and send to Brenda.

Thanks,
Alex

On Tue, Jun 29, 2026 at 9:12 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments...
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot  (updated MAP attached)

Brenda — attaching the updated MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) and the Redwood security questionnaire responses. Quick summary and ask:

Executive summary / recommended path:
- Recommendation for pilot: Hosted API in us-east-1 to meet your mid-July timeline and minimize procurement friction. We will provide SOC 2 artifacts and completed questionnaire.
- If MedTech requires customer-managed KMS or hardware isolation, the alternative is Dedicated capacity in a single-tenant pool (provisioning time: ~3–5 weeks) or Private deployment (6–10+ weeks).

Updated MAP milestones & dates:
- 2026-07-01: Redwood provides completed security questionnaire + SOC 2 artifacts (DONE — attached)
- 2026-07-06: Security review complete (MedTech confirms)
- 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13: Technical canary (see Priya's plan)
- 2026-07-13: Performance gate & stakeholder review
- 2026-07-15: Contract & PO signoff (target)

Action items for MedTech:
- Confirm whether customer-managed KMS is required for pilot (reply to this thread by 2026-07-01).
- Approve the MAP milestones above or propose adjust by 2026-07-01 so we can align procurement.

Action items for Redwood:
- Rishi: dedicated vs hosted cost delta (see internal note) — deliver by 2026-07-01.
- Priya: run canary plan and engineering handoff — ready to start on 2026-07-07.

If those dates look good I will schedule a 30-minute alignment call on 2026-07-02 to walk the steering committee through the MAP and the hosting tradeoffs. Please confirm availability.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

Attachments: MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx (workbook), redwood_security_responses.pdf (PDF)

On Wed, Jun 30, 2026 at 8:10 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Rishi / Marcus — Following MedTech's MAP request (see thread)...

text:
From: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medtech.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Hi Alex / Priya,

Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed, the steering committee needs a short mutual action plan so we can lock a pilot decision by mid-July. Two things are blocking the contract: (1) clear hosting recommendation (Hosted API vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment) for our pilot dataset (PHI subset, US-only), and (2) a validated timeline with security gating (SOC 2 evidence + our questionnaire).

Attached is our draft MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx) and our security questionnaire (security_questionnaire.pdf). High level asks from our side:
- Deployment mode recommendation with tradeoffs (latency, cost, data residency, SLA)
- Estimated run costs for a 3-month pilot (token volumes attached in MAP)
- Evaluation gates and minimum success criteria (latency P95, throughput, false-positive rate on redaction)
- Region options — we prefer us-east for latency but need clarification on data residency and encryption in transit/at rest.

Our steering committee wants a firm commit or a clear go/no-go path by 2026-07-15 so procurement can start PO paperwork. Can you send a proposed MAP with milestones + dates and a recommended hosting mode by EOD 2026-06-30?

Thanks —
Brenda
Brenda Long
Head of Platform Partnerships, MedTech Inc.
brenda.long@medtech.com
+1 (415) 555-0129

Attachments: MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), security_questionnaire.pdf (PDF, 12 pages)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now. High-level response below; I will attach an updated MAP workbook (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) by EOD Tuesday.

Short recommendation (pilot scope):
- Recommended mode: Hosted API (us-east-1) for fastest time-to-value and to avoid initial procurement friction.
  - Pros: no infra procurement, Redwood manages SLAs and autoscaling, fastest onboarding (1–2 weeks).
  - Cons: shared tenancy (we provide strict data isolation controls), possibly higher unit costs vs Dedicated.
- Dedicated capacity: attractive if you need predictable SLOs + cost controls for sustained high QPS; requires reserved capacity contract and ~4–6 week lead time.
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): recommended only if legal requires customer-controlled keys or air-gap. Longer setup (6–10+ weeks) and heavier Ops.

Security gating: we can provide SOC 2 artifacts and a completed questionnaire. We will also propose an encrypted-in-transit and encrypted-at-rest default for the pilot, and offer customer-managed KMS if you need key separation (this pulls the thread toward Dedicated/Private).

Proposed MAP milestones (short):
1) Security review complete (Redwood provides SOC 2 + questionnaire answers) — target: 2026-07-06
2) Technical eval (integration + smoke tests, 7 days) — target window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13
3) Performance gate (latency P95 < 150ms for typical prompt mix OR documented fallback to smaller model) — by 2026-07-13
4) Contract & PO signoff — target: 2026-07-15

To hit these dates we need:
- Rishi / infra team: quick costed estimate for Dedicated reserved pool (3-month pilot profile) and delta vs hosted (units).
- CS (Priya): success criteria & canary plan for integration and data loading.
- MedTech: confirmation on whether customer-managed KMS or standard Redwood keys are acceptable for the pilot.

Looping in Rishi for the cost numbers. Priya — can you draft the canary plan? I will prepare the updated MAP and call out the decisions that shift us between Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Thanks Alex — happy to own the evaluation plan from CS side. Quick notes on canary & success criteria:

Canary plan (7 days):
- Day 0–1: integration (API keys, test dataset upload — limited to synthetic/hashed PHI)
- Day 2–3: baseline latency and throughput tests (our harness + Redwood streaming)
- Day 4–5: end-to-end functional checks with redaction metrics and downstream consumer validation
- Day 6–7: stakeholder review and go/no-go decision

Success criteria (minimum):
- Latency: P95 < 200ms for typical prompt mix (prefer <150ms)
- Availability: >= 99% during canary window
- Redaction/PII handling: automated redaction passes 95% of vetted prompts
- Cost threshold: pilot projected cost + 20% buffer <= $12k/month (per MAP numbers)

If Hosted path is chosen we can be live faster; for Dedicated we should start PO conversations immediately (Rishi — can you provide a ballpark reserved capacity estimate for 3 months by 2026-07-01?).

Thanks —
Priya Natarajan
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

source.subject: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing, BAA milestones & evidence request
messages:
From: Jamie Li <j.li@carebridge.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing and evidence request

Hi Alex,

Thanks for the quick onboarding so far. As part of our procurement packet for CareBridge’s telehealth platform evaluation, our security & compliance team has a few clarifying questions around PHI handling and the BAA timeline before we can proceed to contract review: 

- Primary question: Can you confirm the available deployment modes that will see PHI? We need details about Hosted (shared infra), Dedicated, and Private (VPC/on-prem) options and the default isolation guarantees for each.
- Key management: Does Redwood support customer-managed keys (CMK) for encryption at rest? If so, what KMS integrations and key rotation controls are supported?
- Audit logs & exports: What audit events are captured (API calls, user access, model inference trace) and can we forward logs to our Splunk instance?
- Data residency & retention: Can you support configurable retention policies for PHI (we expect up to 7 years for some records)?
- Evidence: Please attach SOC 2 type II report (redacted if necessary), dataflow diagram for PHI, and a list of controls used to de-identify or minimize PHI in inference workloads.

We’ve attached the RFP (CareBridge_RFP_Q1.pdf) with these items enumerated. We’d also like an anticipated timeline for a draft BAA from Redwood legal — ideally within five business days if feasible.

Can we schedule a 30-min clarifying call this week? Our preferred times are 6/22 morning PST.

Thanks,
Jamie Li
Senior Procurement Manager
CareBridge Health

---
[Attachment: CareBridge_RFP_Q1.pdf]

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>
To: Jamie Li <j.li@carebridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing and evidence request

Hi Jamie — appreciate the clear list. Short answers up front, details below and attachments included.

- Deployment modes: Redwood supports Hosted API (multi-tenant), Dedicated capacity (single-tenant GPU pools), and Private (VPC-hosted control plane or on-prem package). PHI can be processed in any of these modes; for strongest isolation we recommend Dedicated or Private.
- KMS/CMK: Customer-managed keys are supported for Dedicated and Private. Integrations: AWS KMS, GCP KMS, and Azure Key Vault. We also support BYOK workflows for the on-prem package.
- Audit logs: We capture inference request metadata, authentication events, administrative actions, model version rollouts, and key-access logs (where integrated). Logs can be forwarded to customer SIEMs (Splunk, Datadog) via our secure log-forwarding endpoint.
- Retention: Retention is configurable per-route; we can set PHI retention windows up to 7 years and configure automatic purge policies.
- Evidence: SOC 2 Type II (redacted) plus a PHI dataflow diagram and a summary of de-identification controls attached.

I’ve attached: redwood_security_summary.pdf and redwood_dataflow.svg.

Timeline: legal can provide an initial BAA draft within five business days after we confirm the CMK option and retention window; we can then iterate quickly.

I’m available for a call 6/22 9:00–11:00am PST. If that works, I’ll calendar-in with Marissa and Monica.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---
[Attachments: redwood_security_summary.pdf, redwood_dataflow.svg]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T12:08:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing and evidence request

Looping legal/ops for a quick alignment before the call.

Jamie asked about CMK, 7-year retention, Splunk forwarding, and evidence. Action items: 
- Monica: can you confirm the exact set of audit events we can forward and any PII scrubbing options on the forwarded logs? Also confirm default retention config and steps to enable 7-year retention for a dedicated customer.
- Marcus: legal will want to know if CareBridge requires customer key custody for all environments or just Dedicated/Private. Advise on any technical gaps for Hosted + CMK.
- Alex: when you schedule the call, please include the Security Architecture doc link and ask them to indicate if they need an on-prem deployment questionnaire.

Thanks — keep replies terse so we can get the BAA template out quickly.

Marissa
Director, Customer Security
Redwood Inference

> On 2026-06-21 at 11:03, Alex Martinez wrote:
> > Hi Jamie — appreciate the clear list. Short answers up front...

From: Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@carebridge.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>, Jamie Li <j.li@carebridge.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@carebridge.com>
Date: 2026-06-23T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing and evidence request

Alex / team — thanks for the fast responses. A few clarifications before the 6/24 call: 

1) Retention: We will require up to 7 years for a subset of PHI records; can Redwood provide a signed attestation that retention controls are configurable and that deletion is irreversible?
2) KMS: We prefer customer-managed keys for all PHI environments. Is the CMK model supported for your Hosted API, or limited to Dedicated/Private? If the Hosted API cannot support CMK, please propose compensating controls.
3) Logs: We plan to ingest audit logs into Splunk (SaaS). Please outline the log delivery mechanism and whether logs contain PHI by default or can be redacted.

Attached is our evidence matrix to help map items requested in the RFP (carebridge_evidence_matrix.xlsx).

Finally, please share an editable BAA for our counsel to review prior to the call (we want to start redlines in parallel).

Samuel Ortiz
Security Engineering
CareBridge Health

---
[Attachment: carebridge_evidence_matrix.xlsx]

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>
To: Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@carebridge.com>, Jamie Li <j.li@carebridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-25T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing, BAA milestones & evidence request

Hi Samuel / Jamie — summary and commitments below so counsel and engineering can proceed in parallel: 

1) Retention attestation: Redwood will provide a written attestation that retention windows are configurable per-route and that deletion follows irreversible purge workflows (we’ll include the purge operational runbook with the evidence package).
2) CMK support: CMK is supported for Dedicated and Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments. For Hosted API we do not support customer-controlled KMS today; compensating controls for Hosted include encryption-in-transit with TLS 1.2+, at-rest encryption with provider-managed KMS, strict tenant isolation policies, and optional request-level tokenization/prefix-caching to minimize persisted PHI. If CareBridge requires full customer key custody across all environments, we recommend Dedicated or Private.
3) Logs & Splunk: We can forward audit logs to your Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoint. Logs are configurable — by default they do not include raw request payloads (customer can opt-in to include them). We will provide a log schema and redaction controls.

BAA: Attached is a draft BAA (BAA_draft_redline_v1.pdf) in editable form; legal will accept redlines and aims to return a signed BAA within 2–3 business days after receiving your counsel’s changes.

Next steps: 
- We’ll share the full evidence package (SOC 2 redacted, purge runbook, log schema) in the Drive link before EOD 6/26.
- Proposed call confirmed for 6/24 10:30am PST — calendar invite sent; please add any technical folks who will review the dataflow.

If there are any missing items in the evidence matrix, flag them and we’ll prioritize.

Best regards,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---
[Attachments: BAA_draft_redline_v1.pdf]

source.subject: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the productive conversation yesterday. As discussed, we want to kick off the renewal conversation early so we can align on:

- Capacity uplift (targeting ~2x average throughput with burst headroom)
- Term options (12 vs 24 month pricing cadence and commit levels)
- Procurement timeline and PO milestones
- QBR timeline and attendees to surface technical runbooks and SLOs

Next steps I propose:
1) Quick QBR to review usage, latency/cost profile, and uplift plan (30–45m) — propose windows below.
2) Share a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and term amendment draft (attached).
3) Confirm procurement contact and any legal lead for redlines.

Possible QBR windows (PST):
- Tue Jun 22, 10:00–10:45
- Wed Jun 23, 14:00–14:45
- Thu Jun 24, 09:30–10:15

I've attached a MAP and a term amendment draft to get the ball rolling. Can you confirm who on your side should receive the MAP (procurement / legal)?

Attachments: [Orbital_Mutual_Action_Plan_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Term_Amendment_Draft.docx (docx)]

Thanks — looking forward to aligning.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0193

Redwood Inference — production-first LLM inference for developers
From: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim Ortega <tim.ortega@orbital-analytics.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the note and for the MAP. Quick answers:

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the checklist. Attached are the documents procurement asked for — W-9 and a bank letter. I also included an export of our preferred PO contact table (default route for small orders).

A couple quick notes:
- Legal name on our articles is "Helium Supplies, LLC" (EIN: 12-3456789).
- Our remittance address is: 412 Harbor Rd, Suite 2, Alameda, CA 94501.
- For PO routing, please use the column "DefaultPOEmail" in the spreadsheet; it maps by department.

Let me know if you need a Certificate of Insurance (we can provide) or vendor form fields you can't map.

Thanks,
Iris Gomez
Head of Procurement
Helium Supplies Inc.

To: Iris Gomez <iris.gomez@heliumsupplies.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Jun 2026 09:52:00 -0700
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — Helium Supplies (legal docs attached)

Iris — appreciate the quick turnaround and the spreadsheet. Looping in Marcus for AP and Karthik for vendor record fields we need to confirm.

Marcus / Karthik: can you confirm the minimal fields we need to create the vendor record so our AP system will accept POs from Helium? In particular:
- Confirmed legal name: "Helium Supplies, LLC"
- Remit address and whether ACH will be enabled (bank letter attached)
- Default PO email mapping — will your system accept a departmental default email per site?

Target: create vendor record and enable small-PO auto-routing by end of week if possible. Iris, if we need legal entity docs beyond what you sent, I'll flag it.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0168

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Jun 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — Helium Supplies (legal docs attached)

Thanks — quick read through of the attachments looks good. A few items for the vendor master record that we should capture explicitly:
- Legal name (as provided)
- DBA / trading name (if different) — Iris: please confirm if you also operate under any other names.
- EIN (required)
- Tax classification (LLC taxed as corp/partnership/sole prop) — if you can confirm the tax classification it helps downstream tax reporting.
- Remit address + remit method (ACH or check)
- Primary PO email (we can accept a department-level default per your spreadsheet)
- Primary AP contact name & phone

Process note:
- I will upload the attachments to our secure vendor repository and create the vendor record in the system (VNDR-20260617-093) once we have the tax classification and the AP contact.
- If ACH is enabled, we require a bank attestation (we have the bank letter you attached; I'll kick off micro-deposit verification after record creation).

Aditya — if you want to mark this as priority for the week, I can ring-fence time. Otherwise we'll queue for standard vendor intake (2-3 business days).

-Marcus
Marcus Lin
Finance Operations, Redwood Inference

source.subject: HavenRx production regressions after model v2.3 quantization
messages:
From: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:12:04 -0700
Subject: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Ava —

We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
- p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
- throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing

This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.

Please treat as high priority.

— Laura
Laura Mendez
Sr. Engineering Manager, Platform
HavenRx

Attachments: none in this message
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:20:17 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Hi Laura — thank you for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We're pulling the on-call and diagnostics right now.

Immediate next steps:
1) I've looped in our infra lead (David) and VP Eng (Marcus) — we'll triage logs and determine whether a rollback is needed.
2) Could you share the precise hit window + a sample request id? That will speed root cause.
3) If you prefer, let's jump on a 30-min call — I'm available 08:30 or 09:00 PT.

We'll prioritize the safety path (rollback to last known good) if it looks like QoS/latency regression rather than an edge case in your inputs.

— Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

On Mon, Jun 14, 2026 at 08:12 AM Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com> wrote:
> Ava —
>
> We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
> - p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
> - error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
> - throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing
>
> This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.
>
> Please treat as high priority.
>
> — Laura
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:45:33 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update (technical notes)

Folks — quick triage summary from the runtime team.

What we observed: the v2.3 model with the new quantization profile (q3->q4 mixed path) introduces a different decode kernel path that increases per-token CPU/GPU synchronization under the beam/ sampling config used by HavenRx. Under typical sequences the KV cache expands differently and memory paging between CUDA memory + host becomes more frequent at the tail latencies.

Measured from Canary -> Prod telemetry:
- baseline (v2.2, q3_profile): p99 ~420ms, error ~0.3%
- post-upgrade (v2.3, q4_mixed): p99 ~1.8s, 5xx errors to 4.8% concentrated on requests >512 tokens
- throughput per GPU decreased ~18-25% depending on batch mixing

Root cause hypothesis: the quantization selection pushed some attention kernels to a slower fallback that greatly impacts longer sequences. There are also memory fragmentation effects that cause transient OOMs->5xx.

Immediate remediation options (ranked by speed of mitigation):
A) Roll back to v2.2 (last known good) — fastest, <30m to flip and validate if we have hot spares and the model artifacts cached.
B) Adjust quantization profile to q4_light (tighter bias toward q3 path) — medium effort, ~2-4 hours to build/test and canary.
C) Apply request-side throttling / drop long-sequence sampling for the next 2 hours — quick but degrades quality for those inputs.

Attachments: p99_latency_report_20260614.csv (aggregated telemetry), error_trace_645.log (sample 5xx trace).

Recommendation: Start with rollback (A) to stabilize production, then implement B as the long-term fix with canary. I'll prepare a rollback playbook (rollback_playbook.md) and target a rollback window once we get sign-off.

— David
David Kim
Principal Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Jun 14, 2026 at 08:20 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Laura — thank you for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We're pulling the on-call and diagnostics right now.
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Leadership Distribution List (internal)
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: EXEC: HavenRx production regression — propose immediate rollback + customer credit discussion
