Attachment: threat-annotations-v1.pdf (pdf stub)
Attachment: security-evidence.zip (zip stub)

source.subject: Re: Catalyst Cloud — MSA / Order Form redlines (SLA & support)
messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>, legal@catalystcloud.com
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MSA redlines — SLA / support items to discuss

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal and procurement teams reviewed the MSA and order form and attached a redline with a few items we need clarity on before we can proceed: uptime measurement baseline, service credit formula, response times for Sev 1/2, and maintenance window carveouts.

Immediate asks:
- Can Redwood confirm how uptime will be measured (rolling windows, exclusion criteria)?
- We want a clear service credit schedule tied to percentage drops below the uptime target.
- Clarify notification requirements for scheduled maintenance and emergency security fixes.

I've attached our annotated MSA for reference (Catalyst_MSA_Redline_20260410.docx). Can we target a short call next week to walk through?

—Samir
Samir Patel
Director, Cloud Operations
Catalyst Cloud

Attachment: Catalyst_MSA_Redline_20260410.docx (docx, 56 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MSA redlines — SLA / support items to discuss

Samir — thanks for the redline and clear list. Looping in Laura (legal) and Marcus (ops) so we can cover contract and runbook implications. Quick notes before a call: 

- Uptime measurement: Redwood's default hosted SLA uses a rolling 30-day window measured against successful request rates at the API gateway level. We can provide daily availability reports and raw telemetry for auditing.
- SLO tiers: Standard (99.9% monthly) vs Dedicated (99.95% with uplift). We can discuss Dedicated for your production-critical routes.
- Service credits: typical cap is the lesser of 100% of monthly invoice or a fixed cap (we propose 50% of monthly invoice as an upper bound). Credits accrue on a per-incident/period basis.
- Response times: P1 (1 hour initial response, continuous engagement until mitigation), P2 (4 hours initial).
- Maintenance notification: standard is 72-hour notice for scheduled maintenance; emergency security patches can be invoked with as little as 24-hour notice but include a rollback plan and post-mortem.

Propose a 30-min working session on Tue, Apr 13 at 10am PT to walk the redline and record agreed edits live. Attaching our initial order-form SLA proposal (RDW_OrderForm_SLA_Proposal_20260412.pdf) and will bring a mutual action plan.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Account Exec — Strategic Customers
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW_OrderForm_SLA_Proposal_20260412.pdf (pdf, 84 KB)

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MSA redlines — proposed legal positions (SLA & support)

Hi Samir / Ben,

I've reviewed Catalyst's redline and noted the key legal asks. High-level positions from Redwood Legal below — we can kill or accept specific language on the call but want to be explicit: 

1) Measurement window and exclusions
- We accept a rolling 30-day measurement window.
- Exclusions: agreed scheduled maintenance (with notice), force majeure, customer-caused incidents (e.g., misconfiguration), and third-party dependency outages we cannot reasonably control. We will provide telemetry to demonstrate exclusions.

2) Service credit formula
- We propose a stepped credit schedule (example: 0.5% credit for 99.5–99.74, 1.5% for 99.0–99.49, etc.) with a monthly cap equal to the monthly invoice for the affected services. We cannot accept an unlimited cumulative credit that exceeds the monthly invoice.

3) Severity and response times
- P1: 1-hour acknowledgement, 4-hour mitigation target (continuous communication).
- P2: 4-hour acknowledgement, 24-hour mitigation target.
- We can tighten for Dedicated if Catalyst commits to committed capacity.

4) Maintenance windows / emergency patches
- Scheduled: 72-hour notice, communicated via support portal + email.
- Emergency security patch: Redwood will aim for 24-hour notice where possible, but language needs to allow immediate deploys if an active exploitation is observed; we will provide a post-deploy report within 48 hours.

I've attached a redlined Order Form that marks suggested legal language. If Catalyst insists on a higher uptime band without Dedicated capacity and price uplift, that will be a commercial ask.

—Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Privacy
Redwood Inference

Attachment: RDW_OrderForm_SLA_Proposal_20260412.pdf (annotated PDF, 92 KB)

On Mon, Apr 12, 2026 at 08:22 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Samir — thanks for the redline... (see above)

From: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>, legal@catalystcloud.com
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2026 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MSA redlines — SLA / support items to discuss

Thanks Laura and Ben — appreciate the clarity. A few follow-ups and counterpoints from our side after internal review: 

- Measurement & credits: Catalyst is aiming for 99.95% across our production tenancy. Our procurement team is prepared to accept a stepped credit schedule, but the credit quantum needs to be material: 5% of monthly fees per 0.1% below target up to 50% as a cap. We view this as a business continuity guarantee.
- Emergency patches: we understand the need for immediate remediation for active exploits, but we need a written escalation and canary plan for any urgent deploys into production. Unannounced disruptive changes are a showstopper.
- Evidence: we will require daily availability reports + access to a defined set of telemetry endpoints for a 30-day lookback during negotiation and a read-only data export thereafter for audit.

We can discuss on the call, but if Redwood is unwilling to move on the credit quantums, we will escalate procurement approvals.

—Samir

On Tue, Apr 10, 2026 at 11:03 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Samir — thanks for the redline and clear list...

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Catalyst Cloud — MSA / Order Form redlines (SLA & support)

Samir — thanks for the direct feedback and for laying out Catalyst's internal threshold. After conferring with Legal and Ops, here's a proposed compromise that should be pathable without restructuring our commercial model: 

Summary of compromise proposal:
- Uptime: 99.90% standard for hosted plans; 99.95% available for Dedicated (requires 1.25x committed capacity uplift).
- Service credit schedule: stepped credits tied to availability bands; example (monthly): 99.90–99.74 = 5% credit; 99.69–99.49 = 15%; <99.49 = 30%, with a hard cap at 50% of the monthly invoice for the affected services. We will provide a worked example on the call.
- Measurement & evidence: rolling 30-day window; Redwood will deliver daily availability reports and grant a read-only telemetry export for a 30-day rolling period to facilitate audits.
- Emergency maintenance: 24-hour notice for security patches where feasible; immediate deploys permitted only if active exploitation is detected and the only reasonable mitigation is deploy. In such events Redwood will: (a) notify within 4 hours, (b) run a canary deployment for 30 minutes, (c) provide a post-deploy incident report within 48 hours.
- Response targets: P1 acknowledgement 1 hour; target mitigation within 4 hours with continuous updates. P2 acknowledgement 4 hours, mitigation target 24 hours.

Next steps
- If this framework is acceptable in principle, propose a 60-minute redline session Fri, Apr 16 at 9am PT (Ben/Laura/Marcus + Samir/Hannah + Catalyst legal).
- I'll circulate a Mutual Action Plan with dates and responsibility owners and attach a DocuSign link for the Order Form once redlines are agreed.

Attaching: Catalyst-MutualActionPlan-20260415.xlsx (mutual action plan with milestones). DocuSign sample link: https://docusign.com/XYZ-Agreement-1938

If we can agree on the compromise, we should be able to get legal sign-off + procurement approval within 2–3 business days after the redline session.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Exec — Strategic Customers
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Catalyst-MutualActionPlan-20260415.xlsx (xlsx, 16 KB)

source.subject: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off
source.thread:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 9 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off

Team,

Quick background: Zenwell (healthcare analytics) is moving from a pilot (hosted embeddings + chat) to a full expansion that includes Redwood Dedicated + Private for their regulated workloads. Procurement has indicated a tight procurement window — they want a fully executed SOW by 4/15 to hit their Q2 deployment target.

Key commercial summary (attached: Zenwell_SOW_v3.pdf, zenwell_pricing_breakdown.xlsx):
- Term: 36 months; committed spend (total EV): $1.8M
- Year 1 ARR-equivalent: ~$700k (first 12 months skewed toward onboarding & Dedicated consumption)
- Requested pricing: a tiered discount schedule to hit an average ~42% off list across line items (hosted tokens, dedicated device hours, premium observability). Zenwell is also requesting we waive the standard onboarding fee and include the observability add-on free for the first 6 months.
- Current AE delegation: max approval 25% off list for this segment. This request exceeds delegation and needs CFO sign-off for the deviation.

Why I recommend approving (high level):
- Strategic reference value: Zenwell is a nationally recognized health analytics provider; successful on-prem + dedicated deployment in regulated healthcare would be a strong reference for other prospects in our healthcare GTM (estimated pipeline motion: ~$3.5M ARR over 24–36 months).
- Revenue lock-in: Bundling Dedicated + Private increases stickiness and reduces near-term churn risk.
- Operational: Zenwell will commit to a usage minimum (baseline device-hours) and provide a short case-study + reference callback as part of the agreement.

Request: Marissa — can you review and sign the attached internal approval memo if you’re comfortable with the proposed exception? We need a decision by EOD 4/12 to keep procurement on schedule. If you prefer, I can amend the proposal to stage the discount across years (Year 1 larger, then step-down), and add a clawback if they miss usage commitments.

Notes: I included Rafael to flag GL/credit implications and Dylan for SRE/ops to ensure the Dedicated capacity planning is acceptable. Marcus — please advise if you want different commercial guardrails.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off

Ava — thanks for the summary. I’m supportive of giving Zenwell preferential pricing but want tighter guardrails before endorsing a 42% average discount. Proposed adjustments:

- Staged discount schedule we can present to Zenwell: Year 1 = 38% off list, Year 2 = 25% off list, Year 3 = 15% off list. This keeps headline pricing attractive while protecting long-term unit economics.
- Mandatory minimums: require a baseline committed device-hour minimum (50% of estimated Year1 usage) and a minimum monthly hosted token volume with a clawback for sustained shortfall after month 6.
- Remove the permanent onboarding waiver; convert to a one-time 50% credit in month 1 tied to early signature.
- Require a case study + product reference to be delivered within 9 months of go-live; if not delivered, we recoup 25% of the initial onboarding credit.

If those are acceptable, I would ask Marissa to sign off on up to 38% (Yr1) with the conditions above. Rafael — can you confirm this structure keeps FY26 economics intact (I'll need the modeled margin impact).

—Marcus
VP Sales, Redwood Inference

---

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Internal memo for CFO approval — Zenwell tiered discount (D-8421-ZEN)

Marissa — forwarding a concise internal memo I prepared that outlines the ask and conditions Marcus suggested. Doc includes: deal economics, staged discount schedule, required legal clauses (clawback + usage SLAs), and finance GL entries.

Internal approval memo (for signature):
- Customer: Zenwell Health (deal D-8421-ZEN)
- Ask: Approve a customer-specific pricing exception to enable expansion into Dedicated + Private; authorization to sign SOW with the following commercial terms: 36-month term, staged discount (Yr1 38% / Yr2 25% / Yr3 15%), onboarding credit (50% one-time), minimum committed device-hours and hosted token volumes, and a product reference requirement.
- Rationale: Strategic healthcare reference, revenue lock-in via Dedicated/Private, high expansion potential (estimated $3.5M ARR pipeline).
- Financial impact: EV $1.8M; modelled FY26 margin hit ~7% (see attached spreadsheet). Rafael, please confirm classification of the onboarding credit (credit memo vs. cost of sale).
- Approvals requested: CFO signoff for pricing deviation beyond AE delegation (>25%); confirmation of payment terms (Net30 suggested) and billing schedule.

Attachments: Zenwell_SOW_v3.pdf; zenwell_pricing_breakdown.xlsx

If you’re comfortable, please sign and return the internal memo as a signed_discount_memo.pdf; I’ll attach to DocuSign SOW and route to Zenwell procurement.

Thanks — Ava

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 15:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal memo for CFO approval — Zenwell tiered discount (D-8421-ZEN)

Ava / team —

After reviewing the memo and Rafael’s preliminary economics, I will approve a conditional pricing exception with the following firm conditions:

1) Final Year 1 discount cap: 38% off list (as Marcus suggested). Year 2 = 25%, Year 3 = 15%.
2) Onboarding: convert the onboarding waiver to a 50% one-time credit; credit will be subject to a clawback of 25% if the customer fails to deliver the case study/reference within 9 months of go-live.
3) Minimums & clawback: include the committed device-hour minimum and hosted token minimum in Schedule A; add explicit clawback language for sustained shortfall after month 6.
4) Payment & collections: Net30. Because of the size, require a first-quarter payment holdback of 10% in escrow to be released after go-live acceptance criteria (Finance to set up; Rafael lead).
5) Legal: require DocuSign with the updated Schedule A and clawback clauses; Legal must sign off prior to execution.

Please proceed with updating the SOW and DocuSign packet. Rafael — once the final pricing file is attached, please post the approval memo to the deal record (HubSpot link above) and generate the necessary GL approval code (chargeback/credit memo code).

I’m attaching my signed approval memo (signed_discount_memo.pdf). Ava — move this to DocuSign and loop procurement.

Marissa Cole
CFO, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

---

[Attachments referenced in thread: Zenwell_SOW_v3.pdf (application/pdf), zenwell_pricing_breakdown.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet), signed_discount_memo.pdf (application/pdf)]

source.subject: Request: Launchpad Spring 2026 cohort credits — verification
messages:
From: Sarah Wallace <sarah@launchpad.vc>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-12T09:12:00-04:00
Subject: Launchpad Spring cohort — request for partner credits

Hi Ben,

Hope you're well — we're kicking off our Spring 2026 Launchpad cohort (20 early-stage startups) and would like to include Redwood Inference as our official inference credits partner. Historically we give new cohort startups $10–20k vendor credits so they can integrate during the 12-week program.

Can you confirm eligibility and whether we can provision $15,000 in platform credits per company (redeemable over 12 months)? If you need verification docs, we've got the signed accelerator intake and the cohort roster.

Quick details:
- Program: Launchpad Spring 2026 (Apr 6 start)
- Number of startups: 20
- Typical ask per startup: $15k credits, promo code per startup

Attached: roster + standard accelerator terms. Happy to jump on a quick 20-min call if helpful.

Thanks,
Sarah Wallace
Head of Partnerships
Launchpad Capital Accelerator
attachments: launchpad_roster_spring2026.csv (text/csv), accelerator_terms.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Wallace <sarah@launchpad.vc>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-12T11:06:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Launchpad Spring cohort — request for partner credits

Hi Sarah — great to hear from you and congrats on the upcoming cohort.

Thanks for sending the roster and terms — I reviewed them. To move forward with a self-serve issuance of promo credits we need:
1) A confirmation of accelerator eligibility (signed intake or confirmation email) — I see the terms attached which is helpful.
2) A primary contact list with company names + lead founder email for each startup (CSV is perfect).
3) Any co-marketing commitments (optional) so we can sync on joint comms.

Monica (cc'd) is our Partnerships manager — can you confirm whether we should issue one promo code per startup or a single blanket code per cohort? Also, do discounts need to be tracked to a single HubSpot deal?

If everything looks good I can provision promo codes with these parameters: $15,000 USD credit, valid 12 months from issuance, one-time allocation per startup, usage tracked to deal DL-2026-0451. I can also set an onboarding window in the Console to auto-expire unused credits after 12 months.

If you want me to issue codes automatically once you confirm the CSV, say the word and I'll queue issuance.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mobile: (415) 555-0183

From: Launchpad Operations <accelerator@launchpad.vc>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Wallace <sarah@launchpad.vc>
Date: 2026-03-12T14:40:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Launchpad Spring cohort — request for partner credits

On Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 11:06 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sarah — great to hear from you and congrats on the upcoming cohort.
> 
> Thanks for sending the roster and terms — I reviewed them. To move forward with a self-serve issuance of promo credits we need:
> 1) A confirmation of accelerator eligibility (signed intake or confirmation email) — I see the terms attached which is helpful.
> 2) A primary contact list with company names + lead founder email for each startup (CSV is perfect).
> 3) Any co-marketing commitments (optional) so we can sync on joint comms.
> 
> Monica (cc'd) is our Partnerships manager — can you confirm whether we should issue one promo code per startup or a single blanket code per cohort? Also, do discounts need to be tracked to a single HubSpot deal?

Hi Ben — attached is the completed intake + a cleaned CSV with lead emails and company domains. We prefer individual codes per startup to make onboarding easier and to track usage separately.

Co-marketing: we are open to a joint blog post and a demo day mention (no formal MOU required).

Attachments: launchpad_roster_spring2026.csv, accelerator_terms.pdf

Let us know if you need anything else.

— Launchpad Ops

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Wallace <sarah@launchpad.vc>
Date: 2026-03-13T08:15:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Launchpad Spring cohort — request for partner credits

Monica / Marcus — forwarding the Launchpad request. Summary: they want $15k credits per startup for 20 startups (individual codes). Attached roster and intake (see attachments).

Proposed to issue: 20 promo codes, $15k credit each, 12-month validity, tracked to HubSpot deal DL-2026-0451. They have a small co-marketing ask (demo day + blog mention).

Questions for approval:
- Confirm $300k total exposure (20 x $15k) is acceptable under our accelerator partner credit policy (we have an internal guideline but can approve up to $350k for strategic partners).
- Do we require an NDA signed by each startup or is the accelerator intake sufficient?

If approved I will queue issuance and send redemption links to each founder.

Thanks,
Ben

> -----Original Message-----
> From: Launchpad Operations <accelerator@launchpad.vc>
> Sent: Thursday, March 12, 2026 2:40 PM
> To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-13T09:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Launchpad Spring cohort — request for partner credits

Ben — thanks for the context and for flagging. I reviewed the intake and the attached roster.

Approval: I give the green light to proceed with issuance for the full $15k per company (20 startups) — total face value $300,000 — as this is a high-quality accelerator with alumni traction and strong co-marketing signal.

Conditions:
- Issue individual promo codes tied to each founder email in the CSV.
- Credits expire 12 months from issuance; unused balance is forfeited.
- Track usage to HubSpot deal DL-2026-0451 and assign to Partnerships pipeline.
- No additional NDA required beyond the intake; legal is OK with the intake terms for this program.

Marcus — please prepare the batch issuance and redemption links. Ben — once Marcus confirms, draft a short email with redemption instructions to send to founders.

Monica Patel
Head of Partnerships | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Wallace <sarah@launchpad.vc>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-13T11:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Launchpad Spring cohort — credits issued (next steps)

Hi Sarah — great news: Monica approved the cohort credits. We're issuing 20 individual promo codes worth $15,000 each, valid for 12 months.

What I did:
- Created HubSpot deal DL-2026-0451 and attached the intake.
- Queued batch issuance in the Console; Marcus confirmed codes generated.
- Attached: redemption_instructions.txt (short how-to) and the CSV with code mapping (redwood_codes_launchpad_spring2026.csv).

Redemption flow for founders: they click the unique redemption link (one per startup) which creates a self-serve account or links to an existing account and applies the credit. Credits are applied at the account level and show on the billing page. Unused credits expire after 12 months and are removed automatically.

Links and references:
- HubSpot deal: https://hubspot.redwood.com/deals/DL-2026-0451
- Drive folder with mapping: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1LaunchpadProofs
- DocuSign (agreement): https://docusign.com/agreements/LAUNCHPAD-2026-ALPHA

Attachments referenced: redwood_codes_launchpad_spring2026.csv (text/csv), redemption_instructions.txt (text/plain)

Next steps for Launchpad: please confirm you want the founder emails cc'd on the redemption email or if you'd rather we send links directly to founders without Ops being copied. Also let us know if you want Redwood to prepare a short joint blog post after demo day.

Thanks — excited to get the cohort onboarded.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

source.subject: POC plan & risk log — success criteria, timeline, owners
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@brightai.com>, Peter Holt <p.holt@brightai.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 1 Mar 2026 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: POC kickoff — proposed success criteria & 2-week timeline

Hi Samantha & Peter,

Thanks again for the call today — good alignment on the evaluation goals. Per our discussion, I put together a compact POC plan so BrightAI's engineering and product teams can validate whether Redwood meets your production latency and cost goals. Please review and flag anything missing.

Top-level proposal (attached: poc-success-criteria.xlsx):
- Duration: 2 weeks active evaluation (Mon 2026-03-08 to Fri 2026-03-19) with light steady-state week following.
- Scope: 1 critical flow (customer-facing chat), dataset subset (approx. 10k prompts), model candidate: redwood-7B-quant (we can swap to 13B if you want quality-first).
- Success criteria (quantified):
  - 95th pct tail latency <= 220ms for inference (under target concurrency)
  - Generation cost <= $0.XX per 1k tokens (TBD based on token mix)
  - Functional parity on 6 OEM prompt categories (pass/fail automated tests)
  - No data exfil risk; audit logs enabled and retained for 30 days
- Evaluation plan: end-to-end test harness + synthetic load + subset of live traffic (can be toggled). We'll provide a small harness and observability dashboard.

Risks & initial mitigations (full risk log attached as redwood-risk-log-v1.csv):
- Environment access delays: need IAM roles for your VPC or Dev account; action: Laura to coordinate SRE checklist.
- Model mismatch: we'll do a quick quality sweep during week 0 and propose model/quant configs.
- Billing/quotas: need to confirm Dedicated vs Hosted billing for trial — Marcus to confirm procurement steps.

Ownership recommended:
- Redwood lead (technical): Laura Bennett — infra onboarding, debugging, observability
- Redwood commercial: Aditya Rao — timelines, escalation, commercial terms
- BrightAI technical lead: Peter Holt — dataset and routing for live traffic slice

Next steps (action items):
1) Confirm kickoff date and who will be the BrightAI engineers assigned (reply by Wed 2026-03-03).
2) Share IAM/connection details or confirm if we should run in Hosted mode.
3) Marcus will confirm billing approach and any procurement constraints.

Attachments: poc-success-criteria.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood-risk-log-v1.csv (risk tracker).

Thanks — I’ll schedule a 30-min kickoff once we have owners confirmed.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

---
Note: Attachment stubs included above.

From: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@brightai.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Peter Holt <p.holt@brightai.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — proposed success criteria & 2-week timeline

Hi Aditya — thanks for the clear write-up. This looks good overall. A few quick items from our side: 

1) Team & kickoff: We can start on Mon 2026-03-08. I'll assign two engineers: Peter (cc'd) and Nina Park (nina.park@brightai.com). Can you add Nina to future threads?

2) Security: Our security team requires SOC2 evidence and the answers to a short questionnaire before we allow any VPC peering or connection. I'll send the questionnaire to Marcus today.

3) Blocking issue observed: when we attempted to spin up the sample harness in our staging account yesterday we hit an immediate dependency failure in your runtime image (glibc version mismatch). That prevented us from running the quick smoke tests. Can you confirm a remediation plan?

4) Success criteria: we're ok with the 95th pct latency target, but would like to add "no functional regressions on our regression suite (0 critical failures)" as a hard stop.

For now, please loop Nina and Laura on the onboarding and let us know who will own the runtime patch / hotfix.

Thanks,
Samantha
Director, Product (BrightAI)
samantha.cole@brightai.com

---
Quoted message follows below.

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@brightai.com>, Nina Park <nina.park@brightai.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Peter Holt <p.holt@brightai.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — proposed success criteria & 2-week timeline

Hi Samantha & team — appreciate the fast turnaround and the added regression requirement (we agree on 0 critical failures).

On the dependency failure: Laura — can you confirm the root cause and quick mitigation? If glibc mismatch is accurate we can push a patched runtime image to a sandbox repo within 24 hours. Marcus — I'll need a quick confirmation on whether BrightAI wants the Hosted fallback (we can run the POC on Redwood Hosted to avoid the peering step) or if you prefer us to remediate in your staging account.

Immediate plan:
- Laura to reproduce dependency failure and publish a patched image (target: 2026-03-03 18:00 PST).
- If patch requires longer work, Redwood will provide a Hosted sandbox with identical config (availability: within 4 hours of notification).
- Marcus to pick up procurement/billing clarification and SOC2 artifacts.

I've attached an updated risk log (redwood-risk-log-v1.csv) with the glibc issue marked Critical and an initial mitigation column filled. We'll keep the log updated.

Laura / Marcus — can one of you confirm ownership on this thread and update the risk log with estimated ETA?

Aditya
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood-risk-log-v1.csv (csv)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@brightai.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Mar 2026 08:44:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — proposed success criteria & 2-week timeline

Thanks all — I reproduced the staging failure this morning. Root cause: base runtime image we documented still references glibc 2.28; BrightAI staging uses a glibc 2.34-based host. The binary we ship was linked against the older ABI.

Mitigation plan (short/medium term):
- Short term (24 hours): build and publish a compatibility image (tag: rinf-compat-glibc-2.34) that bundles the correct runtime libs. I'll push to our sandbox registry and share pull URL. I expect this to be usable by your engineers within ~6 hours after I push.
- Medium term (by 2026-03-12): produce multi-arch / multi-glibc images and add integration tests in our CI to avoid regressions.

Escalation path:
- If the compatibility image fails, escalate to Marcus (commercial) and Aditya (AE) for priority support; we will open a Sev with the infra team and commit a hotfix within the business day.

Updated risk log entry: 
- risk_id: RL-003
- title: Runtime glibc mismatch
- severity: Critical
- owner: Laura Bennett
- eta: 2026-03-03T14:00:00-07:00 (publish)
- mitigation: publish compat image, provide Hosted sandbox fallback

Marcus — I'm marking this as assigned to me for technical ownership. Marcus, please confirm procurement path for Hosted fallback.

Laura Bennett
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
laura@redwood.com

[Attachment stub: registry-pull-url.txt]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@brightai.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Mar 2026 10:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — proposed success criteria & 2-week timeline

Hi Samantha — quick update on procurement & SOC2: 

1) SOC2 artifacts: I uploaded the SOC2 summary and evidence bundle to the HubSpot record and shared the Drive link here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a2b3c. Samantha, please confirm your security team can accept the bundle for the trial onboarding.

2) Procurement / billing: For a short 2-week POC we can run on Hosted with a temporary trial billing profile (no contract required). If you want Dedicated reserved capacity for the test, we will need a short order form (estimate 2–3 business days). For speed, I recommend Hosted sandbox to avoid procurement friction — we'll apply a credit cap and monthly invoicing terms if you convert after the POC.

3) On the runtime issue: Laura confirmed the compat image is published and available. Laura: can you paste the pull command and a one-liner here so I can forward it to Nina & Peter?

If the image doesn't resolve the smoke tests, we will open a Sev and escalate to infra immediately. Aditya — happy to take point on commercial fallback and run the Hosted sandbox onboarding if BrightAI opts for the quicker path.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Senior Customer Ops | Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com

---
Attached: poc-success-criteria.xlsx (referenced earlier)

source.subject: Telemetry add-ons pricing and discount options for DataOps
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Turner <sophie@dataops.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Telemetry add-ons pricing and retention options

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. As promised, I pulled together a concise summary of the observability add-ons we discussed (retention, tracing, advanced dashboards) and an initial pricing envelope so your finance team can assess the impact. See the attached spreadsheet (DataOps_Retention_PRICING_v2.pdf) for numbers. High level: 

- Retention tiers: 30d / 90d / 365d (per-GB-month incremental)
- Tracing: sampled traces add-on (per-1M spans) + higher sampling for enterprise
- Advanced dashboards: seat-based or account flat for unlimited dashboards

I know you mentioned price sensitivity and a potential 12-month commitment if we can get predictable unit economics. If you're open, I can loop in our pricing lead to propose formal discount tiers (12/24 month commitments).

Next steps: let me know if the attached level looks roughly in line and whether you want a short call (30m) with pricing + CS on Monday.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Sr. Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: DataOps_Retention_PRICING_v2.pdf (pdf, 78KB)
From: Sophie Turner <sophie@dataops.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Feb 2026 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons pricing and retention options

Thanks Ben — this is helpful. A few quick clarifying questions from our side before we align with finance: 

1) For retention — can you confirm the per-GB-month price listed for 365d includes cold storage assumptions? Any tiered discounts if our ingest grows > 20 TB/month?
2) Tracing — we rely on higher-fidelity traces for our production services. Is there a way to get a higher sampling SL with a throttled cost model?
3) Dashboards — your proposal lists seat-based pricing. We prefer a single account-level bundle for our whole org. Can you provide an example P&L for both approaches?

Also — Mike (cc) will be reviewing security/compliance. He asked whether the advanced dashboards option has SOC2 audit trails and an export for retention obligations.

If you can get pricing + a recommended packaging (incl. discount tiers) that would be great — happy to do a 30m sync next week.

— Sophie
Head of Platform | DataOps Inc
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Turner <sophie@dataops.com>, Mike Jenkins <mike.jenkins@dataops.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Mar 2026 08:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons pricing and retention options

source.subject: Clarify subword/token differences & hosted ratecard for pilot
messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena@brightretail.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightRetail Purchasing <procurement@brightretail.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 14:12:00 -0800
Subject: Question on token counting across models + request for hosted ratecard

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team liked Redwood's performance. Before we move to a pilot, procurement has two questions I hoped you could clarify:

1) Our engineering team noticed that different open models seem to tokenize similarly sized texts into different token counts (some use SentencePiece, others BPE). Can you confirm how Redwood bills tokens for the hosted API — is it based on the model's tokenizer, or a normalized token metric?

2) For the pilot (targeting ~5M tokens/day), could you share a baseline hosted ratecard and any standard pilot discounts for an initial 3-month trial? Procurement also asked whether you have an approach to predict billing variability given tokenizer differences — they want a conservative buffer estimate for the P.O.

I've attached a short sample of our anonymized corpus and a couple of sentences that highlighted the variance (attached as tokenization_sample_counts.csv). Procurement is looped in.

Appreciate any clarity so we can get a P.O. underway.

Best,
Elena
Head of Data Science
BrightRetail

Attachments: tokenization_sample_counts.csv (csv, 12KB)

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@brightretail.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, BrightRetail Purchasing <procurement@brightretail.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 17:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Question on token counting across models + request for hosted ratecard

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear ask and for the sample. Short answers up front: billed tokens are counted against the model's tokenizer (so different tokenizers produce different counts), and we provide both model-level token prices and a recommended normalization approach for budgeting.

Details and attachments: I've attached our standard hosted ratecard (BrightRetail_Ratecard_v1.pdf) and a short note that explains how tokenization differences show up in billing and a simple method to estimate a conservative daily buffer (Redwood_tokenization-notes.pdf). Key points:
- Redwood bills "input + output" tokens according to the model variant you route to (e.g., models based on SentencePiece vs BPE will yield different counts).
- For predictable budgeting we recommend deriving a per-model token multiplier using a representative sample (we ran this on your sample; see tokenization_sample_counts.csv). Use the 95th percentile token multiplier to provision a conservative buffer.
- For pilot pricing: standard hosted list is included in the ratecard. For a 3-month pilot at ~5M tokens/day, we typically offer a 20% pilot discount plus an optional monthly usage credit to offset onboarding evals.

Next steps: Vivek included here to add a couple of technical notes (tokenization edge cases, sequence length effects). If that all looks fine, I can share a draft SOW and a PO-ready summary for procurement.

Cheers,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: BrightRetail_Ratecard_v1.pdf (pdf, 95KB); Redwood_tokenization-notes.pdf (pdf, 18KB)

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@brightretail.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, BrightRetail Purchasing <procurement@brightretail.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Feb 2026 09:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Question on token counting across models + request for hosted ratecard

Hi all — quick technical addendum from infra/SE side:

1) Why counts differ: SentencePiece (unigram) and BPE split tokens differently; languages with many short subwords (e.g., compound words) will blow up counts on some tokenizers. This is entirely expected and not altered by Redwood — we bill actual tokenizer tokens the model consumes.

2) Practical recommendation: run a 10k-sample through the exact model variant you plan to use (we can host an eval run). Compute tokens_per_char and tokens_per-message distributions. For billing safety, provision at the 95th percentile tokens_per_message * expected messages per day.

3) Smoothing option to reduce billing volatility: route a portion of low-sensitivity traffic to a cheaper, compatible model variant (we can set routing policy by endpoint). That reduces average cost while maintaining quality for critical traffic.

4) For the pilot discount: we can commit to a 20% discount on hosted token pricing for 3 months and an additional one-time onboarding credit of $4,000 if you share a PO within 10 business days.

I'll run your sample through a comparable model we host and post results (I expect to share a short CSV with per-document token deltas).

— Vivek

From: Elena Rossi <elena@brightretail.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightRetail Purchasing <procurement@brightretail.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 14:12:00 -0800
Subject: Question on token counting across models + request for hosted ratecard

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team liked Redwood's performance. Before we move to a pilot, procurement has two questions I hoped you could clarify:

1) Our engineering team noticed that different open models seem to tokenize similarly sized texts into different token counts (some use SentencePiece, others BPE). Can you confirm how Redwood bills tokens for the hosted API — is it based on the model's tokenizer, or a normalized token metric?

2) For the pilot (targeting ~5M tokens/day), could you share a baseline hosted ratecard and any standard pilot discounts for an initial 3-month trial? Procurement also asked whether you have an approach to predict billing variability given tokenizer differences — they want a conservative buffer estimate for the P.O.

I've attached a short sample of our anonymized corpus and a couple of sentences that highlighted the variance (attached as tokenization_sample_counts.csv). Procurement is looped in.

Appreciate any clarity so we can get a P.O. underway.

Best,
Elena
Head of Data Science
BrightRetail

Attachments: tokenization_sample_counts.csv (csv, 12KB)

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@brightretail.com>

Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 17:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Question on token counting across models + request for hosted ratecard

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear ask and for the sample. Short answers up front: billed tokens are counted against the model's tokenizer (so different tokenizers produce different counts), and we provide both model-level token prices and a recommended normalization approach for budgeting.

Details and attachments: I've attached our standard hosted ratecard (BrightRetail_Ratecard_v1.pdf) and a short note that explains how tokenization differences show up in billing and a simple method to estimate a conservative daily buffer (Redwood_tokenization-notes.pdf). Key points:
- Redwood bills \"input + output\" tokens according to the model variant you route to (e.g., models based on SentencePiece vs BPE will yield different counts).
- For predictable budgeting we recommend deriving a per-model token multiplier using a representative sample (we ran this on your sample; see tokenization_sample_counts.csv). Use the 95th percentile token multiplier to provision a conservative buffer.
- For pilot pricing: standard hosted list is included in the ratecard. For a 3-month pilot at ~5M tokens/day, we typically offer a 20% pilot discount plus an optional monthly usage credit to offset onboarding evals.

Next steps: Vivek included here to add a couple of technical notes (tokenization edge cases, sequence length effects). If that all looks fine, I can share a draft SOW and a PO-ready summary for procurement.

Cheers,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: BrightRetail_Ratecard_v1.pdf (pdf, 95KB); Redwood_tokenization-notes.pdf (pdf, 18KB)

source.subject: Renewal + Private deployment expansion — proposed amendment and timeline
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Renewal window + Private deployment expansion conversation

Hi Sarah —

Quick note as we approach your contract renewal (Mar 15): thanks again for the collaborative pilots these past six months. Per our last call you were weighing a Private (VPC) deployment with an additional on-prem cluster to satisfy inter-regional isolation and latency requirements. 

We’d love to draft a short amendment so Procurement can review in advance. High-level asks from your side that I captured:
- Add a second dedicated cluster for on-prem inference in EWR (NY)
- VPC peering with the Atlas production network + stricter egress controls
- 12-month renewal with a 10% committed capacity increase (to ensure reserved throughput)

I’m looping in Monica (account lead) and Marcus (solutions/infra) to scope the work and produce an amendment and rough MOP (mutual action plan). Can you confirm the target cutover window (you mentioned late April) and whether Procurement needs a signed SoW or if a contract amendment is sufficient?

If it helps, we can have a short technical sync next week to run the network/ops checklist.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachments: Atlas_Security_Questions_2026.pdf (received from you in last call)
From: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Alan Cho <alan.cho@atlashealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@atlashealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal window + Private deployment expansion conversation

Thanks Ben —

Confirming a few things from our side:
- Cutover target: week of April 19–23 (we need the new on-prem cluster live before our Q2 campaign).
- Procurement: prefers a contract amendment rather than a separate SoW for this expansion; we will still require a short timeline and acceptance criteria.
- Security: we’re compiling the SOC2 artefacts and the network diagram for the VPC peering. I attached an updated network diagram (Network_Diagram_Atlas_VPC.drawio) to this reply.

A couple of asks for your team:
1) Can Marcus share a short checklist of firewall rules and ports we should allow for the peering and data plane?
2) We need explicit language around data residency and layered isolation in the amendment (Atlas is regulated — we must show technical controls).

Looping Procurement; Alan will coordinate redlines on the amendment.

Best,
Sarah
Sarah Connor | Head of Data Platform
AtlasHealth
sarah.connor@atlashealth.com

Attachments: Network_Diagram_Atlas_VPC.drawio
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Feb 2026 14:26:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal window + Private deployment expansion conversation

Hi Sarah — thanks for the diagram and timeline. Quick technical notes to feed into the amendment and the MOP: 

- VPC peering & networking:
  - Primary control plane will remain in Redwood-managed control plane (leased control plane for Private); inference data plane nodes will be inside your VPC (customer-managed subnet).
  - Required ports: 443 (TLS) for control/management, 50051 (gRPC) optional for high-throughput internal calls; we recommend NAT for outbound telemetry only.
  - CIDR ranges: we’ll need a /26 for the initial cluster and an additional /26 for the new on-prem cluster peering block.

- Isolation model:
  - Multi-cluster topology: Hosted control plane + two isolated inference clusters (EWR on-prem, primary in IAD). Traffic routing can be pinned by policy (latency first for NY customers).
  - KV cache separation: each cluster gets dedicated KV cache and disk-backed checkpointing per retention policy.

- Capacity & SLA
  - Proposed reserved capacity: +10% token throughput over current commit (we’ll propose concrete vGPU counts after you confirm expected QPS and concurrency).
  - Cutover playbook: staged canary on a mirror traffic slice, promote after 48h stability and perf acceptance.

Can you confirm expected peak QPS and typical request size (avg tokens per request)? That’ll let us size GPUs and IOPS. I’ll assemble a short MOP and a checklist we can hand to your infra team.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin | Solutions Architect, Infra
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>, Alan Cho <alan.cho@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Draft amendment + MOP (attached) — request for Procurement review

Team —

Attached is a first-pass amendment (RDW_Private_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx) and a one-page MOP. Highlights:
- Term: 12 months from Mar 16, 2026 (aligned to your renewal)
- Capacity: +10% reserved token throughput; detailed vGPU sizing table pending your QPS numbers
- Isolation: explicit clause for multi-cluster deployment, data residency, and KV separation
- Acceptance: 48-hour canary with defined performance acceptance (latency percentiles and error rate thresholds)
- Pricing: pro-rated pricing for the additional cluster for first 3 months; we gave you a promotional 5% discount on the new cluster for signing at renewal

Next steps we propose:
1) Procurement to review the amendment and return redlines by March 5
2) Technical pre-cutover call the week of March 15 to finalize firewall/cidr and monitoring hooks
3) Target cutover week April 19–23

Alan/Procurement — can you confirm if the 5% promotional discount is acceptable or if I should route for an approval for a steeper concession?

Thanks,
Ben

Attachments: RDW_Private_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx
From: Alan Cho <alan.cho@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@atlashealth.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Mar 2026 15:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Draft amendment + MOP (attached) — request for Procurement review

Ben / Monica / Marcus —

We reviewed the draft and have two immediate redlines:
1) Need explicit language that Redwood will provide SOC2 evidence for the deployed Private clusters within 10 business days of request (we cannot accept an open timeline).
2) Pricing: Procurement will accept the 5% promo for the first quarter but requires a cap on overage charges for burst traffic (specify a per-token max).

We’ll send annotated redlines via DocuSign for the amendment by EOD Thu (see link below). Also attaching a brief procurement checklist with required PO and billing contacts. 

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/en/links/abcd-efgh-2026
Drive folder with redlines: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cAtlasOnPrem

Alan Cho | Procurement Lead
AtlasHealth
alan.cho@atlashealth.com

Attachments referenced: Procurement_Checklist_Atlas.pdf

On Wed, 25 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -0800, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Attached is a first-pass amendment (RDW_Private_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx) and a one-page MOP. Highlights:

> - Capacity: +10% reserved token throughput; detailed vGPU sizing table pending your QPS numbers
> - Isolation: explicit clause for multi-cluster deployment, data residency, and KV separation
> - Acceptance: 48-hour canary with defined performance acceptance (latency percentiles and error rate thresholds)
> - Pricing: pro-rated pricing for the additional cluster for first 3 months; we gave you a promotional 5% discount on the new cluster for signing at renewal
> 
> Next steps we propose:
> 1) Procurement to review the amendment and return redlines by March 5
> 2) Technical pre-cutover call the week of March 15 to finalize firewall/cidr and monitoring hooks
> 3) Target cutover week April 19–23
> 
> Thanks,
> Ben

From: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Alan Cho <alan.cho@atlashealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@atlashealth.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal window + Private deployment expansion conversation

Thanks Ben —

Confirming a few things from our side:
- Cutover target: week of April 19–23 (we need the new on-prem cluster live before our Q2 campaign).
- Procurement: prefers a contract amendment rather than a separate SoW for this expansion; we will still require a short timeline and acceptance criteria.
- Security: we’re compiling the SOC2 artefacts and the network diagram for the VPC peering. I attached an updated network diagram (Network_Diagram_Atlas_VPC.drawio) to this reply.

A couple of asks for your team:
1) Can Marcus share a short checklist of firewall rules and ports we should allow for the peering and data plane?
2) We need explicit language around data residency and layered isolation in the amendment (Atlas is regulated — we must show technical controls).

Looping Procurement; Alan will coordinate redlines on the amendment.

Best,

sarah.connor@atlashealth.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Feb 2026 14:26:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal window + Private deployment expansion conversation

Hi Sarah — thanks for the diagram and timeline. Quick technical notes to feed into the amendment and the MOP: 

- VPC peering & networking:
  - Primary control plane will remain in Redwood-managed control plane (leased control plane for Private); inference data plane nodes will be inside your VPC (customer-managed subnet).
  - Required ports: 443 (TLS) for control/management, 50051 (gRPC) optional for high-throughput internal calls; we recommend NAT for outbound telemetry only.
  - CIDR ranges: we’ll need a /26 for the initial cluster and an additional /26 for the new on-prem cluster peering block.

- Isolation model:
  - Multi-cluster topology: Hosted control plane + two isolated inference clusters (EWR on-prem, primary in IAD). Traffic routing can be pinned by policy (latency first for NY customers).
  - KV cache separation: each cluster gets dedicated KV cache and disk-backed checkpointing per retention policy.

- Capacity & SLA
  - Proposed reserved capacity: +10% token throughput over current commit (we’ll propose concrete vGPU counts after you confirm expected QPS and concurrency).
  - Cutover playbook: staged canary on a mirror traffic slice, promote after 48h stability and perf acceptance.

Can you confirm expected peak QPS and typical request size (avg tokens per request)? That’ll let us size GPUs and IOPS. I’ll assemble a short MOP and a checklist we can hand to your infra team.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin | Solutions Architect, Infra
marcus.lin@redwood.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@atlashealth.com>, Alan Cho <alan.cho@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Draft amendment + MOP (attached) — request for Procurement review

Team —

Attached is a first-pass amendment (RDW_Private_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx) and a one-page MOP. Highlights:
- Term: 12 months from Mar 16, 2026 (aligned to your renewal)
- Capacity: +10% reserved token throughput; detailed vGPU sizing table pending your QPS numbers
- Isolation: explicit clause for multi-cluster deployment, data residency, and KV separation
- Acceptance: 48-hour canary with defined performance acceptance (latency percentiles and error rate thresholds)
- Pricing: pro-rated pricing for the additional cluster for first 3 months; we gave you a promotional 5% discount on the new cluster for signing at renewal

Next steps we propose:
1) Procurement to review the amendment and return redlines by March 5
2) Technical pre-cutover call the week of March 15 to finalize firewall/cidr and monitoring hooks
3) Target cutover week April 19–23

Alan/Procurement — can you confirm if the 5% promotional discount is acceptable or if I should route for an approval for a steeper concession?

Thanks,
Ben

Attachments: RDW_Private_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx


From: Alan Cho <alan.cho@atlashealth.com>

We’ll send annotated redlines via DocuSign for the amendment by EOD Thu (see link below). Also attaching a brief procurement checklist with required PO and billing contacts. 

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/en/links/abcd-efgh-2026

Alan Cho | Procurement Lead

source.subject: Offer band guidance + HC approval request — Ishaan Patel (Backend)
messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena@zenrecruit.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 25, 2026 at 09:12 AM -0800
Subject: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Hi Ava / Lauren,

Great chat yesterday — I wanted to follow up with Ishaan's background and the band we've been recommending based on market comps. Attached: Ishaan_Patel_Resume.pdf.

Quick summary:
- Senior backend engineer, ~6.5 years exp, strong infra + scale systems background.
- Current comp: $185k base + RSU tranche, open to manager-track conversations but prefers IC ladder.
- Recruiter market comp suggests L5 (Mid-Senior) at Redwood, with a narrow L4/L5 ambiguity depending on scope.

Can you confirm the target band and whether HC approval is required for a manager-track title at offer? We can move fast — candidate is talking to two other finalists.

Best,
Elena
ZenRecruit

Attachment: Ishaan_Patel_Resume.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 25, 2026 at 10:05 AM -0800
Subject: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Forwarding Elena's note — candidate looks strong. Quick context and asks:
- Hiring manager (Marcus) and I aligned that the role's scope currently maps to L5 expectations (ownership across subsystems, cross-team projects, mentoring).
- Candidate has manager-track interest; we need to decide whether to offer "Senior Engineer, Manager track" title (still individual contributor compensation band) or push for a people-manager hire.
- Headcount: this is a new hire on the Platform team (Requisition 9874). We currently have 2 open IC HC and 1 manager HC on the org plan; this req is approved for 1 IC.

Requests:
1) Marcus — confirm level (L5 vs L6) and any suggested title that helps future mobility.
2) Monica/People Ops — do we need a formal HC exception to target manager-track title or is the IC HC sufficient?
3) Tessa/Comp — if we want to make a manager-track offer without manager HC, what band/comp exceptions are allowable?

I've attached the comp band reference and our offer matrix for 9874 (offer-matrix-9874.xlsx, comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf). Candidate timeline: hiring loop + final with Marcus this week; Elena can convert to verbal offer as soon as we align.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Director, Recruiting Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf, offer-matrix-9874.xlsx

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 26, 2026 at 08:22 AM -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Ava / team —

I interviewed Ishaan yesterday — he demonstrates L5 scope today. Key points: owns cross-service performance initiatives, designed an infra migration, and has some experience running small feature teams but hasn't been a direct manager. Given that, I recommend offering at L5 with a title that signals manager-track growth: "Senior Software Engineer (Manager track)".

Rationale:
- Keeps hiring within approved IC HC (no immediate manager HC needed).
- Signals career path for candidate; we can convert to an EM role later with a promotion/HC change.

Comp ask: if we offer L5, target base $195k (midpoint). If candidate pushes for manager-level comp, we should consider a small sign-on or elevated equity grant rather than shift to L6 band.

I don't see a reason to go to L6 at hire based on interviews.

Marcus
Staff Engineer, Platform
Redwood Inference

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 27, 2026 at 11:03 AM -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Agree with Marcus on level = L5. From a comp perspective:
- L5 base midpoint (Q1 2026 band): $190k–$200k.
- For manager-track signaling without manager HC, standard practice is: keep L5 base, add 5–10% sign-on OR 10–25% more in RSU grant depending on internal parity.
- We should avoid creating a special L5+ band; keep offer within banding guidance to preserve internal equity.

HC note: People Ops (Monica) — please confirm we don't need an HC exception to use a manager-tracking title (we've done this twice in the past year). If it requires a formal headcount change, candidate timing might slip.

Lauren
VP, Talent Acquisition
Redwood Inference

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 02:45 PM -0800
Subject: RE: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Thanks team — quick operational confirmation:
- No formal HC exception required to use a manager-track title so long as the hire remains on IC headcount. Title is an internal designation; HC type (IC vs Manager) is what matters for budget.
- Approve offering L5 base up to $200,000 + discretionary sign-on up to 7% (one-time cash) OR up to 20% additional RSUs depending on equity budget for the role.
- Please route the final offer package through People Ops (offers@redwoodinference.com) and include the signed comp approval worksheet.

Ava — once you get candidate acceptance, loop me and we'll schedule the onboarding + initial 90-day success plan touchpoints.

Monica Patel
Head of People Operations (Interim)
Redwood Inference

--
On Wed, Feb 25, 2026 at 10:05 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Forwarding Elena's note — candidate looks strong. Quick context and asks:
> ...

Attachments referenced: comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf, offer-matrix-9874.xlsx

Great chat yesterday — I wanted to follow up with Ishaan's background and the band we've been recommending based on market comps. Attached: Ishaan_Patel_Resume.pdf.

Quick summary:
- Senior backend engineer, ~6.5 years exp, strong infra + scale systems background.
- Current comp: $185k base + RSU tranche, open to manager-track conversations but prefers IC ladder.
- Recruiter market comp suggests L5 (Mid-Senior) at Redwood, with a narrow L4/L5 ambiguity depending on scope.

Can you confirm the target band and whether HC approval is required for a manager-track title at offer? We can move fast — candidate is talking to two other finalists.

Best,
Elena
ZenRecruit

Attachment: Ishaan_Patel_Resume.pdf


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 25, 2026 at 10:05 AM -0800
Subject: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Forwarding Elena's note — candidate looks strong. Quick context and asks:
- Hiring manager (Marcus) and I aligned that the role's scope currently maps to L5 expectations (ownership across subsystems, cross-team projects, mentoring).
- Candidate has manager-track interest; we need to decide whether to offer \"Senior Engineer, Manager track\" title (still individual contributor compensation band) or push for a people-manager hire.
- Headcount: this is a new hire on the Platform team (Requisition 9874). We currently have 2 open IC HC and 1 manager HC on the org plan; this req is approved for 1 IC.

Requests:
1) Marcus — confirm level (L5 vs L6) and any suggested title that helps future mobility.
2) Monica/People Ops — do we need a formal HC exception to target manager-track title or is the IC HC sufficient?
3) Tessa/Comp — if we want to make a manager-track offer without manager HC, what band/comp exceptions are allowable?

I've attached the comp band reference and our offer matrix for 9874 (offer-matrix-9874.xlsx, comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf). Candidate timeline: hiring loop + final with Marcus this week; Elena can convert to verbal offer as soon as we align.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Director, Recruiting Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf, offer-matrix-9874.xlsx


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 26, 2026 at 08:22 AM -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Ava / team —

I interviewed Ishaan yesterday — he demonstrates L5 scope today. Key points: owns cross-service performance initiatives, designed an infra migration, and has some experience running small feature teams but hasn't been a direct manager. Given that, I recommend offering at L5 with a title that signals manager-track growth: \"Senior Software Engineer (Manager track)\".

Rationale:
- Keeps hiring within approved IC HC (no immediate manager HC needed).
- Signals career path for candidate; we can convert to an EM role later with a promotion/HC change.

Comp ask: if we offer L5, target base $195k (midpoint). If candidate pushes for manager-level comp, we should consider a small sign-on or elevated equity grant rather than shift to L6 band.

I don't see a reason to go to L6 at hire based on interviews.

Marcus
Staff Engineer, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 27, 2026 at 11:03 AM -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Agree with Marcus on level = L5. From a comp perspective:
- L5 base midpoint (Q1 2026 band): $190k–$200k.
- For manager-track signaling without manager HC, standard practice is: keep L5 base, add 5–10% sign-on OR 10–25% more in RSU grant depending on internal parity.
- We should avoid creating a special L5+ band; keep offer within banding guidance to preserve internal equity.

HC note: People Ops (Monica) — please confirm we don't need an HC exception to use a manager-tracking title (we've done this twice in the past year). If it requires a formal headcount change, candidate timing might slip.

Lauren

Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 02:45 PM -0800
Subject: RE: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Thanks team — quick operational confirmation:
- No formal HC exception required to use a manager-track title so long as the hire remains on IC headcount. Title is an internal designation; HC type (IC vs Manager) is what matters for budget.
- Approve offering L5 base up to $200,000 + discretionary sign-on up to 7% (one-time cash) OR up to 20% additional RSUs depending on equity budget for the role.
- Please route the final offer package through People Ops (offers@redwoodinference.com) and include the signed comp approval worksheet.

Ava — once you get candidate acceptance, loop me and we'll schedule the onboarding + initial 90-day success plan touchpoints.

Monica Patel

source.subject: Request: Temporary SLA carve-out for filter/toolchain incident + mitigation proposal
messages:
From: Kristen Holmes <kristen.holmes@acmecorp.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@acmecorp.com
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — request for temporary SLA carve-out

Hi Aisha,

We observed a production incident yesterday (Feb 17) where our calls to Redwood for an internal customer workflow encountered two concurrent issues: (1) the content filter triggered on ~0.8% of the requests and stripped structured tool-calls in the assistant response, and (2) several downstream tool-calls timed out and returned 504s causing our orchestrator to re-run the prompt leading to elevated latency and duplicate actions. The combined effect pushed our end-to-end P95 latency from ~420ms to ~1.8s and produced missing actions for some users.

We need two things from Redwood:
- A formal confirmation if this class of failure is covered by the standard SLA or if it requires a carve-out (we assume the moderation/filtering pipeline may be considered a runtime dependency).
- A temporary exception or SLA adjustment while we and you implement mitigations, plus a proposed mitigation plan and timeline.

I've attached logs and a short sample trace. Happy to jump on a call — Tom (cc'd) will be the technical lead on our side.

Thanks,
Kristen Holmes
Sr. Security & Trust Engineering
Acme Corp

[Attachment: evidence-filter-logs-20260218.csv (CSV, 204 KB)]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Kristen Holmes <kristen.holmes@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 17:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — request for temporary SLA carve-out

Hi Kristen — thanks for the detailed report and the logs. We take incidents that impact action correctness very seriously. High level:

- Short answer on coverage: The filtering and safety pipeline is considered a runtime safeguard; per our standard hosted SLA we do not guarantee output parity when content safeguards actively modify or suppress tokens. That means tool-call truncation/structured-output removal caused by the filter can fall into a carve-out.

- That said, we want to minimize customer impact. Attached is a proposed mitigation checklist and a timeline to close the gaps. We can provision a temporary narrow carve-out for the period required to implement a fix, provided Acme agrees to the mitigation milestones and a joint testing window.

Proposed next steps (quick):
1) Redwood technical deep-dive: Marcus and team to analyze the sample traces and confirm exact filter signatures that led to structured output removal (ETA 24 hours).
2) Short-term: enable conservative fallback mode for your project that will prefer returning a controlled failure token rather than silently stripping tool-call payloads (ETA 72 hours).
3) Medium-term: whitelist/heuristics tuning and a safety-verified bypass path for authenticated tool-calls (ETA 2–3 weeks).
4) Post-implementation: joint acceptance testing window + rollback plan.

I'll get Marcus and Olga looped for the deep-dive today. If acceptable, we can also put a provisional carve-out in writing that limits the exception to the specific filter signatures and date range and includes a requirement for joint testing.

Best,
Aisha
Head of Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference

[Attachment: proposed-mitigation-checklist.docx (DOCX, 48 KB)]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 17:15:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — deep-dive request

Team — forwarding Acme's logs and Kristen's note. TL;DR: structured-output/tool-call payloads are being suppressed by our filter in a small fraction of requests and downstream tool-call timing is amplifying the user-visible impact. Need a scoped root cause and short-term mitigation we can deploy to Acme's project.

Please prioritize:
- Marcus: can you run the attached CSV through the filter-trace analyzer and confirm which rule IDs triggered and whether the suppression was pre/post tokenization?
- Olga: outline the safest conservative fallback (controlled failure token) and the telemetry we'll emit to validate behavior.
- Karthik/Jonas: estimate effort to implement an authenticated bypass path for tool-calls and the safety review steps.

Goal: have a clear recommendation for Acme by EOD tomorrow (Feb 19). Ping me if blocked.

Thanks,
Aisha

> ---------- Forwarded message ----------
> From: Kristen Holmes <kristen.holmes@acmecorp.com>
> To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
> Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 15:03:00 -0800
> Subject: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — request for temporary SLA carve-out
>
> [See original message and attachment]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Feb 2026 09:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — deep-dive results

Aisha/Team — quick findings from the sample traces and logs:

- Root cause summary: The structured tool-call signatures were flagged by rule set FS-12 and FS-17. In several cases the filter removed the tokens that encoded the tool-call JSON before the tool-call serializer step executed. Separately, a subset of downstream invocations experienced an intermittent 500/504 due to a transient GC spike on the partner's runtime, which caused our client to retry and hit higher latency windows.

- Impact scope: ~0.8% of requests for Acme's project during the window Feb 17 02:00–06:00 UTC. Most failures resulted in graceful fallback; ~0.03% led to missing actions in the orchestration layer.

- Short-term mitigation (can be shipped as a reversible config):
  * Conservative-fallback mode for project ID acme-prod-1: on detection of a filter signature that would remove structured-output, instead emit a fixed failure token "[TOOL_CALL_BLOCKED]" and add detailed telemetry fields (filter_rule_id, pre/post token hash). This avoids silent stripping and lets the orchestrator handle retries deterministically.
  * Disable automatic retry-on-504 at the client layer for 30s during joint testing so Acme can observe single-failure behavior.

- Estimated timeline:
  * Config change and rollout to Acme's project (staging + prod): ~36–48 hours with standard canary.
  * Whitelist/bypass path (authenticated tool-calls): ~14 business days including safety review and regression tests.

I've documented the exact rule firings in INF-2241 and attached a small CSV with match detail. If Acme agrees to the conservative-fallback path and the telemetry we add, we can request a provisional carve-out limited to Feb 17–Mar 4 while the whitelist is developed.

-Marcus
Principal Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: filter-match-detail-20260218.csv (CSV, 42 KB)]

From: Kristen Holmes <kristen.holmes@acmecorp.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <t.nguyen@acmecorp.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 15:33:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — carve-out confirmation

Aisha/Marcus — thank you for the rapid investigation and the clear mitigation path. Acme is prepared to accept a narrow, time-boxed carve-out under the following conditions:

1) Carve-out scope: only applies to the content-filter-induced structured-output suppression signatures FS-12 and FS-17 for project acme-prod-1 between Feb 17 00:00 UTC and Mar 4 23:59 UTC.
2) Acceptance criteria: Redwood enables the conservative-fallback mode and the additional telemetry for the project within 48 hours and shares a canary run report.
3) Accountability: Redwood will open a joint monitoring dashboard (shared read-only link) with the telemetry fields (filter_rule_id, pre/post token hash, action_taken) and weekly status updates until whitelist/bypass is in production.
4) No blanket SLA waiver: This carve-out does not apply to unrelated runtime outages or other service classes.

If you can confirm the rollout schedule and provide an initial canary report, we will accept the limited carve-out in writing and pause any escalation on our side.

Kristen
Sr. Security & Trust Engineering
Acme Corp

[In-reply-to: INF-2241]
