- Impact scope: ~0.8% of requests for Acme's project during the window Feb 17 02:00–06:00 UTC. Most failures resulted in graceful fallback; ~0.03% led to missing actions in the orchestration layer.

- Short-term mitigation (can be shipped as a reversible config):
  * Conservative-fallback mode for project ID acme-prod-1: on detection of a filter signature that would remove structured-output, instead emit a fixed failure token "[TOOL_CALL_BLOCKED]" and add detailed telemetry fields (filter_rule_id, pre/post token hash). This avoids silent stripping and lets the orchestrator handle retries deterministically.
  * Disable automatic retry-on-504 at the client layer for 30s during joint testing so Acme can observe single-failure behavior.

- Estimated timeline:
  * Config change and rollout to Acme's project (staging + prod): ~36–48 hours with standard canary.
  * Whitelist/bypass path (authenticated tool-calls): ~14 business days including safety review and regression tests.

I've documented the exact rule firings in INF-2241 and attached a small CSV with match detail. If Acme agrees to the conservative-fallback path and the telemetry we add, we can request a provisional carve-out limited to Feb 17–Mar 4 while the whitelist is developed.

-Marcus
Principal Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: filter-match-detail-20260218.csv (CSV, 42 KB)]

From: Kristen Holmes <kristen.holmes@acmecorp.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <t.nguyen@acmecorp.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 15:33:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unexpected omissions and tool-call failures — carve-out confirmation

Aisha/Marcus — thank you for the rapid investigation and the clear mitigation path. Acme is prepared to accept a narrow, time-boxed carve-out under the following conditions:

1) Carve-out scope: only applies to the content-filter-induced structured-output suppression signatures FS-12 and FS-17 for project acme-prod-1 between Feb 17 00:00 UTC and Mar 4 23:59 UTC.
2) Acceptance criteria: Redwood enables the conservative-fallback mode and the additional telemetry for the project within 48 hours and shares a canary run report.
3) Accountability: Redwood will open a joint monitoring dashboard (shared read-only link) with the telemetry fields (filter_rule_id, pre/post token hash, action_taken) and weekly status updates until whitelist/bypass is in production.
4) No blanket SLA waiver: This carve-out does not apply to unrelated runtime outages or other service classes.

If you can confirm the rollout schedule and provide an initial canary report, we will accept the limited carve-out in writing and pause any escalation on our side.

Kristen
Sr. Security & Trust Engineering
Acme Corp

[In-reply-to: INF-2241]

messages:
From: Samir Kahn <samir.k@procureplus.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: ProcurePlus Purchasing <procureplus-purchasing@procureplus.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: ProcurePlus RFP: initial security pack request

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the demo last week — the stakeholders were impressed with Redwood's latency profile. Procurement is pulling together the security package for the RFP and needs a few things to proceed to the next stage:

1) A completed SIG questionnaire (or a SIG-lite control matrix mapped to your SOC 2 / ISO controls)
2) SOC 2 attestation (most recent) and scope statement
3) Confirmation on where customer data is processed (regions) and data retention default settings

Can you confirm whether you can deliver the above by the close of business on Friday, 20 Feb? If there are gaps, please call them out with expected delivery dates.

Also include a contact for any technical follow-ups; our security engineer may reach out directly.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Kahn
Senior Sourcing Manager, ProcurePlus
samir.k@procureplus.com

[Attachment: ProcurePlus_RFP_Instructions.pdf (pdf)]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Kahn <samir.k@procureplus.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: ProcurePlus RFP: initial security pack request

Hi Samir — thanks, and glad the demo landed well.

Quick ack: we can meet the 20 Feb window for an initial submission. Proposed plan below:

- Today: we will return a SIG-to-SOC2 control mapping (spreadsheet) and draft answers to the SIG questions that are commonly requested.
- Mon-Tue (15–16 Feb): Stephanie will validate system-level evidence links and mark any items that require redaction/NDAs.
- Wed (17 Feb): Marcus will package the SOC 2 attestation and a short evidence index (PDF).
- Fri (20 Feb): Final package uploaded to your portal (or emailed) with a short cover note and contact list for technical follow-ups.

We have three deliverables planned for Friday:
- Redwood_Control_Mapping_v1.xlsx (maps SIG controls → SOC 2 controls, with evidence pointers)
- SOC2-Attestation-Statement.pdf (attestation + scope summary)
- ProcurePlus_SIG-answers-20260218.docx (signed-off answers to questionnaire where applicable)

One clarification: does ProcurePlus require a fully completed SIG spreadsheet, or is a SIG-lite control mapping acceptable at this stage? For larger vendors we typically send a mapping + evidence bundle first and complete very detailed SIG spreadsheets on contract award.

Contact for technical follow-ups: Stephanie Nguyen (stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai), Security Solutions Lead — she can join a short call if your engineer requests deeper telemetry or KMS details.

I'll upload the initial mapping as promised today.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0148

[Attachment: Redwood_Control_Mapping_v1.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Samir Kahn <samir.k@procureplus.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: ProcurePlus Purchasing <procureplus-purchasing@procureplus.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2026 08:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: ProcurePlus RFP: initial security pack request

Aditya — thanks for the timeline. A couple of notes from security & procurement after reviewing the mapping draft you uploaded:

- SIG completeness: the procurement team wants a completed SIG spreadsheet or explicit gaps with compensating controls called out. SIG-lite is acceptable for initial scoring but must be complete before contract signature.
- Data residency: we operate in US-only regions for this procurement. We need a clear statement of default processing locations and whether customers can request EU-only processing.
- Evidence access: for operational artifacts (audit logs, change control records), our security team will request access to an evidence portal or SFTP. Can you confirm the method and whether we need a formal NDA for some artifacts?

Also, can Stephanie or Marcus provide a single-page control owner table (control id → Redwood owner/email) to accelerate our internal review? If possible, include expected lead times for any items that will require engineering support to export.

Deadline: to keep the RFP timeline, can you provide the completed SIG or an explicit gaps spreadsheet by end of day Thursday, 19 Feb? We will escalate if not received.

Regards,
Samir

>
> Best,
> Aditya

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Kahn <samir.k@procureplus.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, ProcurePlus Purchasing <procureplus-purchasing@procureplus.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: ProcurePlus RFP: control evidence & delivery cadence

Samir — following up with the requested items for the RFP. Deliverables uploaded and summarized below.

What we uploaded (links and attached stubs):
- Redwood_Control_Mapping_v1.xlsx — completed SIG-to-SOC2 mapping with a 'GAP' column where we do not have a 1:1 control; each GAP row includes a compensating control note and estimated delivery time if remediation is required.
- SOC2-Attestation-Statement.pdf — latest SOC 2 Type II attestation, scope: Redwood Inference production inference and control plane (us-west-2 region).
- ProcurePlus_SIG-answers-20260218.docx — questionnaire answers; redactions applied where evidence contains customer-specific data.
- Control_Owners_onepager.pdf — single-page table: control id → owner → contact (Stephanie and Marcus are listed as primary owners for their domains).

Evidence delivery & access:
- Operational artifacts (audit logs, change-control exports): we will provide a scoped SFTP drop with timebound URLs. Certain artifacts (raw logs with customer identifiers) require an NDA; we can provision a limited-access bundle once the NDA is in place.
- Data residency: default processing is in US (us-west-2). EU-only processing is available via our Dedicated/Private deployment options; we can include a formal statement in the contract language.

Next steps I propose:
1) If you confirm NDA requirements, we can start the evidence SFTP provisioning immediately (Marcus will own).
2) If ProcurePlus requires a full SIG spreadsheet signed-off before contract signature, flag that now so we can prioritize any remaining manual evidence pulls (we estimate 3–5 business days for manual exports).

Attachments included: Redwood_Control_Mapping_v1.xlsx; SOC2-Attestation-Statement.pdf; ProcurePlus_SIG-answers-20260218.docx; Control_Owners_onepager.pdf

If your security engineer wants a short call, Stephanie is available Thurs 19 Feb 10–11am PT.

Thanks — let me know if you want these uploaded to your RFP portal or delivered via HubSpot secure link.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0148

[Attachment stubs uploaded to portal: Redwood_Control_Mapping_v1.xlsx, SOC2-Attestation-Statement.pdf, ProcurePlus_SIG-answers-20260218.docx, Control_Owners_onepager.pdf]

source.subject: RFI: Evaluation & regression monitoring for LLMs - NIST / EU AI Act details
source.summary: Customer requested detailed mapping to NIST AI RMF and EU AI Act position plus continuous evaluation/regression monitoring plan. Internal alignment required on which commitments we can make (logging retention, KMS, SOC2 evidence). Final RFI response and evaluation plan attached; proposed follow-up demo/call.
messages:
From: Lisa Gomez <lisa.gomez@aureliushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Feb 2026 13:05:00 -0500
Subject: RFI: Evaluation & regression monitoring for LLMs

Ben —

Thanks for taking this RFI. Aurelius Health is compiling a vendor questionnaire focused on model risk and governance for suppliers providing hosted inference services. We need the following by COB Mon 22 Feb: a mapping to NIST AI RMF (controls you implement / responsible team), a short statement on how Redwood positions against the EU AI Act (high-level classification + mitigations), and a concrete evaluation / regression monitoring plan (how you detect quality regressions, datasets you run, alerting thresholds, turnaround SLAs for remediation).

Key context: we plan to use the LM for clinical summarization (PHI-lite payloads, de-identified but considered high impact in our risk review). Data residency and auditability are required. Please flag any items that are out of scope or would require contractual terms (e.g., specific retention windows, on-prem options).

Attached: Aurelius_RFP_Initial.pdf

– Lisa
Lisa Gomez
Procurement Lead, Aurelius Health
lisa.gomez@aureliushealth.com
(415) 555-9210

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Gomez <lisa.gomez@aureliushealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Re: RFI: Evaluation & regression monitoring for LLMs

Lisa —

Thanks for the clear ask and the deadline. We can deliver a consolidated RFI response that includes: (1) a NIST AI RMF mapping (identify/protect/detect/respond/recover) with team ownership, (2) our EU AI Act position and recommended mitigations for high-impact uses, and (3) a practical evaluation & regression monitoring plan with sample metrics and alerting rules.

We're attaching an internal outline to show the structure we plan to deliver. Would you be available for a 45-minute call Wed 18 Feb to walk through assumptions (use-case detail, PHI handling, and the remediation SLAs) before we finalize?

Attached: (draft) RFI outline

source.subject: Retention & audit logs feasibility for pilot — BYO/KMS questions
messages:
From: Jules Martin <jules.martin@acmehealth.com>
To: aditya.rao@redwood.ai
Cc: compliance@acmehealth.com
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. A quick follow-up from our security and compliance team: for the pilot we need clarity on three items before we sign off for data ingress:

- Retention: can we set retention to 12 months for conversational logs and have an export for audit?
- Audit logs: we need an immutable, exportable audit trail (admin acts, API calls, tokenization events). Sample format would be helpful.
- BYOK / KMS: we prefer customer-managed keys for any PHI data. Is BYOK supported for your hosted product or only in Dedicated/Private?

I'm attaching our current internal retention policy so you can map requirements. We also need an estimate of the engineering effort and a high-level timeline.

Can we get a short write-up before EOD next Tuesday? Jules

[Attachment: current-retention-policy.pdf (PDF, 420KB)]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 09:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

Team — forwarding Acme's ask. Short triage: they want 12mo retention + exportable audit + BYOK. Can each of you answer the bullets below by EOD Mon so I can consolidate a single reply to the customer on Tue?

Neha: feasibility/controls and what evidence we can deliver (SOC2 sections, sample logs).
Marcus: product options (hosted vs Dedicated tradeoffs, UI/workflow for retention config, export UX).
Grace: rollout & ops (how this affects routing, caching, cost estimates for 12mo retention).
Laura: any legal/procurement notes (POC terms, minimal contract language for pilot).

Quick guidance — please keep replies concise (1-3 bullets), call out hard blockers only. I will assemble a decision summary + proposed MVP and timeline.

Thanks,
Aditya

--
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0142

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

On Wed, Feb 11, 2026 at 9:45 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — forwarding Acme's ask. Short triage...

Answers (security):

- BYOK: Hosted API does NOT support classic BYOK for customer key rotation today. BYOK is supported for Dedicated and Private deployments where the customer key material can be provisioned into a customer-controlled KMS or HSM. For the pilot, the pragmatic approach is: 1) use Dedicated trial capacity with customer KMS integration, or 2) if they insist on hosted, provide envelope encryption where customer supplies an encrypted key blob (limited guarantee).

- Audit logs: we can produce detailed API-level audit logs (timestamp, actor, API route, request id, resource id) and admin console events. We currently persist audit logs for 90 days in hosted; Dedicated can be configured for longer retention (custom S3 export). We can deliver a CSV export similar to the attached sample.

- Retention & immutability: For immutability/audit-grade retention we recommend exporting logs to customer-controlled storage (S3 with Object Lock or equivalent) for 12 months. We can add a retention config that flags production logs for export.

Risks/notes:
- HIPAA/HITRUST: Hosted short-retention + non-BYOK could be a blocker for regulated PHI customers.
- Evidence: we can provide a runbook + sample logs for compliance review within 3 business days once we have acceptance from Product.

Attaching a sanitized sample log extract.

Neha
Neha Kapoor
Head of Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.ai
[Attachment: audit-log-sample.csv (CSV, 120KB)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 13 Feb 2026 10:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

Summary from product/roadmap POV:

- MVP approach (minimal scope to unblock pilot):
  1) Add retention UI setting (12mo option) + server-side export job to push logs to a configured S3. No immediate change to hosted DB retention.
  2) Label this as "Pilot: Dedicated-only BYOK" — scope Dedicated deployment template + KMS integration docs.
  3) Provide audit log CSV export and a small script to rehydrate logs into a reporting format.

- Estimation: ~3 dev-weeks for retention UI + export pipeline (backend + job), ~2 dev-weeks for KMS integration baseline in Dedicated (we already have a foundation). End-to-end demo in 5 weeks if we start this sprint.

- UX: simplest flow is tenant-level toggle + target S3 + confirm. Avoid per-conversation retention in MVP.

I'll draft a short-fashioned scope document and a timeline; attaching a one-pager.

Marcus
Marcus Lin
Product Manager, Platform
marcus.lin@redwood.ai
[Attachment: proposed-mvp-scope.docx (DOCX, 45KB)]

From: Jules Martin <jules.martin@acmehealth.com>
To: aditya.rao@redwood.ai
Cc: compliance@acmehealth.com
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. A quick follow-up from our security and compliance team: for the pilot we need clarity on three items before we sign off for data ingress:

- Retention: can we set retention to 12 months for conversational logs and have an export for audit?
- Audit logs: we need an immutable, exportable audit trail (admin acts, API calls, tokenization events). Sample format would be helpful.
- BYOK / KMS: we prefer customer-managed keys for any PHI data. Is BYOK supported for your hosted product or only in Dedicated/Private?

I'm attaching our current internal retention policy so you can map requirements. We also need an estimate of the engineering effort and a high-level timeline.

Can we get a short write-up before EOD next Tuesday? Jules

[Attachment: current-retention-policy.pdf (PDF, 420KB)]

Team — forwarding Acme's ask. Short triage: they want 12mo retention + exportable audit + BYOK. Can each of you answer the bullets below by EOD Mon so I can consolidate a single reply to the customer on Tue?

Neha: feasibility/controls and what evidence we can deliver (SOC2 sections, sample logs).
Marcus: product options (hosted vs Dedicated tradeoffs, UI/workflow for retention config, export UX).
Grace: rollout & ops (how this affects routing, caching, cost estimates for 12mo retention).
Laura: any legal/procurement notes (POC terms, minimal contract language for pilot).

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

On Wed, Feb 11, 2026 at 9:45 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — forwarding Acme's ask. Short triage...

Answers (security):

- BYOK: Hosted API does NOT support classic BYOK for customer key rotation today. BYOK is supported for Dedicated and Private deployments where the customer key material can be provisioned into a customer-controlled KMS or HSM. For the pilot, the pragmatic approach is: 1) use Dedicated trial capacity with customer KMS integration, or 2) if they insist on hosted, provide envelope encryption where customer supplies an encrypted key blob (limited guarantee).

- Audit logs: we can produce detailed API-level audit logs (timestamp, actor, API route, request id, resource id) and admin console events. We currently persist audit logs for 90 days in hosted; Dedicated can be configured for longer retention (custom S3 export). We can deliver a CSV export similar to the attached sample.

- Retention & immutability: For immutability/audit-grade retention we recommend exporting logs to customer-controlled storage (S3 with Object Lock or equivalent) for 12 months. We can add a retention config that flags production logs for export.

Risks/notes:
- HIPAA/HITRUST: Hosted short-retention + non-BYOK could be a blocker for regulated PHI customers.
- Evidence: we can provide a runbook + sample logs for compliance review within 3 business days once we have acceptance from Product.

Attaching a sanitized sample log extract.

Neha
Neha Kapoor
Head of Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.ai
[Attachment: audit-log-sample.csv (CSV, 120KB)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 13 Feb 2026 10:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot requirements — retention window, BYOK for PHI audits

Summary from product/roadmap POV:

- MVP approach (minimal scope to unblock pilot):
  1) Add retention UI setting (12mo option) + server-side export job to push logs to a configured S3. No immediate change to hosted DB retention.
  2) Label this as \"Pilot: Dedicated-only BYOK\" — scope Dedicated deployment template + KMS integration docs.
  3) Provide audit log CSV export and a small script to rehydrate logs into a reporting format.

- Estimation: ~3 dev-weeks for retention UI + export pipeline (backend + job), ~2 dev-weeks for KMS integration baseline in Dedicated (we already have a foundation). End-to-end demo in 5 weeks if we start this sprint.

- UX: simplest flow is tenant-level toggle + target S3 + confirm. Avoid per-conversation retention in MVP.

I'll draft a short-fashioned scope document and a timeline; attaching a one-pager.

Marcus

source.subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava

Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.

Marcus

Redwood Inference

source.subject: Exception request (EX-2026-01-28-01): ship UNSIGNED Redwood Private hotfix bundle via break-glass path
source.body:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 28, 2026 at 8:12 AM
Subject: Exception request (EX-2026-01-28-01): ship UNSIGNED Redwood Private hotfix bundle via break-glass path

Aisha,

Requesting a time-bound exception to ship an UNSIGNED Redwood Private offline hotfix bundle using the break-glass process.

Summary
- Customer impact: Helios Defense pilot environment is blocked on a high-severity regression introduced in Private 2026.01.0 (air-gapped install completes but runtime crashes on start due to an init container args parsing issue).
- Urgency: Their internal change window closes Fri 1/30. If we miss it, next window is ~3 weeks; pilot success criteria slips and exec escalation already started.

Why normal signing is blocked
- Our signing stage is currently hard-blocked due to HSM-backed key access failures in the release pipeline.
- Root issue is being tracked under INT-11048; infra has rotated the CI secret but the HSM policy still rejects the signing service token (403). We do not have a safe workaround that preserves key custody controls without bypassing the HSM entirely.

Proposed exception
- Ship an unsigned hotfix bundle (bundle build ID: rpvt-2026.01.0-hotfix.1+build.4127) directly to Helios Defense via Support/Solutions, limited distribution.
- Include manifest + checksums (SHA256) and require customer-side checksum verification prior to install.
- Follow up with a properly signed bundle as soon as signing is restored (target <48h).

Risk acceptance request
- We acknowledge this weakens provenance/integrity guarantees and increases supply chain risk if the bundle is tampered in transit.

Date: Wed, Jan 28, 2026 at 9:23 AM
Subject: Re: Exception request (EX-2026-01-28-01): ship UNSIGNED Redwood Private hotfix bundle via break-glass path

Responding inline:

1) Distribution scope
- Internal handlers: Connor O’Brien (Release Eng), Colin O’Donnell (Solutions) as the delivery contact. No other internal forwarding.
- External recipients (Helios): two named platform ops contacts (will be listed on the distribution list doc).
- Transfer: Helios will pull the bundle from their existing secure file exchange with us (same channel used for prior offline bundles). We will not send the tarball over email.
- Distribution limitation: single customer only; no posting to general download locations.

2) Customer verification steps required
- We will provide a SHA256 checksum file for:
  a) the bundle tarball
  b) the included manifest file
  c) the runtime artifact tar(s)
  d) Helm chart tarballs
- Helios must run:
  - sha256sum -c hotfix-bundle-sha256.txt
  - and capture the output in their change record.
- Installer dependency: they are on installer v1.18.2. Break-glass install flag is supported; signature verification is normally enforced but in this case we will use the break-glass override to proceed with explicit warning.

3) Logging/audit events
- When break-glass is used, installer writes:
  - /var/log/redwood-private-installer/install.log (explicit “BREAK_GLASS_USED” marker)
  - /var/log/redwood-private-installer/audit.log (event: bundle_verification_override)
- This is present in v1.18.2.
- We will also require a support bundle capture post-install which includes the installer audit section (per our standard support bundle flow).

4) Time limit + follow-up
- Time-bound exception: 7 days max.
- Plan: Once signing is restored, we will issue a signed equivalent hotfix bundle for the same code change and request Helios to:
  - verify signature successfully in preflight
  - store the signed bundle alongside the unsigned one for audit continuity
  - we are NOT requiring immediate reinstall if their environment is stable, but we will require the signed artifact to be validated and retained.
- If signing is not restored within 48h, we will provide daily updates and re-request approval if distribution needs to expand (currently not planned).

Attachments:
- hotfix-bundle-sha256.txt
- distribution-list-helios-defense.txt

Connor

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Wed, Jan 28, 2026 at 8:12 AM
Subject: Exception request (EX-2026-01-28-01): ship UNSIGNED Redwood Private hotfix bundle via break-glass path

Aisha,

Requesting a time-bound exception to ship an UNSIGNED Redwood Private offline hotfix bundle using the break-glass process.

Summary
- Customer impact: Helios Defense pilot environment is blocked on a high-severity regression introduced in Private 2026.01.0 (air-gapped install completes but runtime crashes on start due to an init container args parsing issue).
- Urgency: Their internal change window closes Fri 1/30. If we miss it, next window is ~3 weeks; pilot success criteria slips and exec escalation already started.

Why normal signing is blocked
- Our signing stage is currently hard-blocked due to HSM-backed key access failures in the release pipeline.
- Root issue is being tracked under INT-11048; infra has rotated the CI secret but the HSM policy still rejects the signing service token (403). We do not have a safe workaround that preserves key custody controls without bypassing the HSM entirely.

Proposed exception
- Ship an unsigned hotfix bundle (bundle build ID: rpvt-2026.01.0-hotfix.1+build.4127) directly to Helios Defense via Support/Solutions, limited distribution.
- Include manifest + checksums (SHA256) and require customer-side checksum verification prior to install.
- Follow up with a properly signed bundle as soon as signing is restored (target <48h).

Risk acceptance request
- We acknowledge this weakens provenance/integrity guarantees and increases supply chain risk if the bundle is tampered in transit.
- We will limit exposure by controlling distribution, using out-of-band checksum verification, and collecting explicit customer acknowledgment.

Asks
1) Security approval for the exception (break-glass) with any required conditions.
2) Guidance on required audit logging/evidence for compliance (what we must retain internally and what we must ask the customer to retain).

Attachment: EX-2026-01-28-01_exception_request_form.pdf

Thanks,
Connor O'Brien
Release Engineering Lead

Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 28, 2026 at 12:46 PM
Subject: Re: Exception request (EX-2026-01-28-01): ship UNSIGNED Redwood Private hotfix bundle via break-glass path

Approved: EX-2026-01-28-01, with conditions.

Approval scope
- Single customer: Helios Defense only.
- Single bundle: rpvt-2026.01.0-hotfix.1+build.4127 only.
- Validity: 7 days from today. Any extension requires a new request.

Required conditions (must complete before customer installs)
1) Dual-control attestation
- Connor + Neha to reply-all confirming:
  a) tarball SHA256
  b) checksum file SHA256
  c) that the checksum file validates the tarball and included artifacts.

2) Customer acknowledgment
- Obtain written confirmation from Helios that they:
  - verified sha256sum output successfully
  - understand the bundle is unsigned and why
  - will retain verification output + installer audit logs in their change record.

3) Logging/evidence retention
- Internally retain:
  - this email thread
  - the uploaded tarball hash
  - hotfix-bundle-sha256.txt
  - distribution list
  - the customer acknowledgment
- Ensure the installer break-glass event is present in the support bundle collected post-install.

4) Follow-up
- Produce a signed equivalent bundle when the signing pipeline is restored and send to Helios with instructions to verify signature in preflight.
- Marcus to open/track postmortem action items for the signing outage as a release blocker.

Do not broaden distribution without coming back to me.

-Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
Security (Exec Sponsor)
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Evaluation roadmap & throughput commitments for Q1 MAP
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@finco.com>
Date: Thu, 08 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Evaluation roadmap & throughput commitments for Q1 MAP

Hi Emily,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — following up with the proposed evaluation roadmap and the throughput targets we discussed. Attached is a working MAP (finco_map_v1.xlsx) and a short summary of the initial performance expectations so procurement has a sense of unit economics. Quick highlights: 

- Target sustained throughput: 500 QPS (inference requests) for the canonical payments use case
- Tokens/sec: target 20k tokens/sec steady-state for a 256-token average response
- Concurrency target: 50 concurrent request slots with tail-latency SLOs
- TTFT target: <= 75ms median; 95th < 250ms for the committed config
- Unit economics (hosted list-price estimate): ~$220 per 1M tokens; dedicated/reserved pricing TBD

Action items I propose for next week:
1) Redwood to run a 6-hour sustained throughput run with the FinCo prompt set (Ben)
2) Provide 95th and 99th latency histograms + tokens/sec breakouts (Ben/Marcus)
3) Procurement to confirm target contract date and any network/VPC requirements (Emily/procurement)
4) Schedule a 30-min review for results (suggest Tue 13 Jan)

Can you confirm the meeting time and whether procurement needs the cost breakdown in a different format?

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
redwood.ai

Attachments: finco_map_v1.xlsx (MAP spreadsheet)
From: Emily Zhang <emily@finco.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jared Cole <jared@cloudops.io>
Date: Fri, 09 Jan 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evaluation roadmap & throughput commitments for Q1 MAP

Thanks Aditya — looks good. A couple clarifying asks from our side before the review: 

- Can you include 99th percentile latency numbers for the planned config (we care about tail for reconciliation flows)?
- Please add a line-item showing cost per 1M tokens at the proposed throughput (list price and with a reserved discount if available).
- Procurement will need the MAP and a simple SOW stub if things check out.

Tue 13 Jan at 11:00 PT works for me and Jared.

Emily
VP, Product 
FinCo

On Fri, 09 Jan 2026 at 09:12 Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks again for the time yesterday — following up with the proposed evaluation roadmap...
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 09 Jan 2026 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Evaluation roadmap & throughput commitments for Q1 MAP (test harness & costing)

Quick note on logistics — I can spin up the FinCo test harness on Dedicated Catalyst-8 (8xA100-style) tomorrow and run an initial 6-hour ramp to steady-state followed by a 2-hour steady run. Plan:

- Workload: FinCo prompt set (avg resp ~256 tokens; 60% short, 40% long)
- Ramp: 0 -> target QPS over 30 minutes to capture warm-up effects
- Metrics captured: tokens/sec, sustained QPS, median/95/99 latencies, TTFT, mem/KV cache hit rate
- Expected initial numbers (estimate based on similar workloads): tokens/sec ~18k-21k; sustained QPS 420-500; concurrency 35-50; 95th ~200-260ms; 99th could range 300-500ms depending on network tail.
- Rough costing estimate (on-demand): $0.22 per 1k tokens => ~$220 per 1M tokens (list). With a 30% reserved commitment, estimate drops to ~$154 / 1M tokens.

I'll attach the latency plots and the run plan after the first run (latency_plots_qps.pdf). If Procurement needs cost per million in a line item table I can export from the MAP spreadsheet.

Ben
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachments: latency_plots_qps.pdf (initial run plan)
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@finco.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 11 Jan 2026 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evaluation roadmap & throughput commitments for Q1 MAP

Emily — attaching an updated MAP (finco_map_v1.xlsx) with concrete commitments from our side and a short results summary from Ben's first run: 

Summary of run (6h warm+2h steady):
- Sustained QPS observed: 450 (steady window averaged over 2h)
- Average tokens/sec: 18,000
- Median TTFT: 60ms; 95th latency: 210ms; 99th latency: 360ms
- Concurrency: 40 active slots during steady window
- Observed unit cost (on-demand): ~$220 / 1M tokens (matches estimate); projected reserved pricing ~ $150-160 / 1M tokens after commit

Committed actions in MAP:
1) Redwood to run a full 24-hour sustained throughput validation (Ben) — target date 13 Jan
2) Redwood to export 95/99 latency histograms + per-route token breakdowns for FinCo prompts (Marcus)
3) FinCo procurement to review the SOW stub and confirm PO timeline (Emily/procurement)
4) Final acceptance criteria: sustain >= 420 QPS for 24h with 99th latency < 400ms and unit cost <= $200 / 1M tokens (reserved pricing applied)

If procurement confirms the window, we'll start the 24-hour run on Tue 13 Jan and share the consolidated report by EOD Thu 15 Jan. Meeting remains Tue 13 Jan 11:00 PT.

Best,
Aditya

Attachments: finco_map_v1.xlsx
From: FinCo Procurement <procurement@finco.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Zhang <emily@finco.com>, Jared Cole <jared@cloudops.io>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 11:00:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evaluation roadmap & throughput commitments for Q1 MAP

Aditya / Team — procurement has reviewed the MAP and the acceptance criteria. We can proceed with the 24-hour validation run subject to the following: 

- Please provide a one-page SOW stub and a draft PO by EOD Wed 14 Jan
- Network/VPC peering will be provisioned on our side on Mon 12 Jan; confirm Redwood needs any special IPSec config
- We require a cost table that shows list price and reserved discount for 1M/10M/50M token bands

Once we have the SOW and the cost table we will confirm the PO timeline.

Regards,
FinCo Procurement
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@finco.com>, FinCo Procurement <procurement@finco.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Final benchmark report and MAP update — sustained validation complete

Team,

We completed the 24-hour sustained validation window (24 Jan run started/ended as scheduled). Summary of final validated metrics and updated unit economics: 

Final validated performance (24h steady window):
- Sustained QPS (steady): 420
- Average tokens/sec: 16,800
- Median TTFT: 65ms; 95th latency: 245ms; 99th latency: 320ms
- Concurrency sustained: 38 active slots

Notes: tail latency improved vs initial noisy windows; the 99th settled around 320ms once KV cache warmed and request shaping was applied. We saw periodic spikes correlated with the FinCo long responses (top 10% of requests).

Updated unit economics (reserved capacity pricing applied):
- Effective cost (after 30% reserved discount + 10% volume rebate estimate): ~$190 per 1M tokens
- Projected monthly spend at target steady-state (420 QPS, avg 256 tokens, 30 days): ~ $77k/mo (reserve-adjusted) — details in attached MAP spreadsheet.

Attachments: finco_map_v1.xlsx, latency_plots_qps.pdf, redwood_nda_signed.pdf

Next steps / asks: 
1) Emily/procurement: please review the attached SOW stub and cost table and confirm PO send-by date (requested by 2026-01-22). DocuSign link: https://app.docusign.com/s/finco-sow-2026
2) Redwood: propose a technical onboarding week (2 days) after PO to finalize VPC peering and routing
3) We'll schedule a short wrap-up call for 2026-01-23 to review run artifacts and sign-off checklist.

If procurement is comfortable with the numbers we'll convert the MAP to the contract language and move to the dedicated reservation.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
redwood.ai

source.subject: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema + SIEM export questions
related_links: Reference: Azure Sentinel / Log Analytics HTTP Data Collector API (learn.microsoft.com)
source.body:
From: Dana Whitaker <dana.whitaker@heliobank.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@heliobank.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 14, 2026 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema + SIEM export questions

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the Private deployment deep dive yesterday.

As part of our security review, can you share:

1) Your audit log event schema (field list + required vs optional). We need to confirm actor attribution, tenant/project scoping, and correlation IDs.
2) Which events are guaranteed to be logged for "sensitive actions" (RBAC changes, API key lifecycle, export destination changes, policy/routing changes, retention/residency settings, and Private install/upgrade actions).
3) Export options (Splunk HEC and Azure Sentinel/Log Analytics preferred). Any notes on batching, retries, and failure behavior.
4) Delivery semantics: best-effort vs at-least-once; how duplicates are represented; whether there is an idempotency key.
5) Data handling: any PII in audit logs, redaction rules, and retention controls in Private.

If you have a sample event payload for a couple of actions (e.g., role change + API key rotation), that would help our SIEM team validate mappings.

Best,
Dana Whitaker
Director, Security Engineering
Helio Bank


---

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Whitaker <dana.whitaker@heliobank.com>
Cc: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@heliobank.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 14, 2026 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema + SIEM export questions
Attachments: redwood-audit-log-schema-v1.json; redwood-siem-export-guide-draft-v0.9.pdf

Dana,

Sharing two attachments:

- redwood-audit-log-schema-v1.json (canonical schema v1; includes required/optional fields and constraints)
- redwood-siem-export-guide-draft-v0.9.pdf (SIEM export guidance; Splunk HEC + Azure Sentinel notes, plus recommended de-dupe)

Answers inline below to match your questions.

1) Schema (required vs optional)

At a high level, every audit event includes:
- schema_version (e.g., "1.0")
- event_id (unique ID for de-duplication; globally unique within a deployment)
- event_type (enumerated string; consistent across Hosted/Dedicated/Private for equivalent actions)
- occurred_at (RFC3339 timestamp)
- outcome ("success" | "failure")
- actor.* (who performed the action)
- target.* (who/what was acted on)
- resource.* (the affected object/scope)
- tenant_id + optional project_id (scoping)
- source_ip (when available; see note below)
- correlation IDs: request_id and trace_id (when action is triggered via API/Console)

For actor attribution we support:
- actor.type: user | service_account | api_key | system
- actor.id: stable identifier
- actor.display_name: optional (we recommend using this for convenience only; do not depend on it)
- actor.impersonated_by.*: optional; present when an action was taken via delegated/impersonation flows (where applicable)

2) Sensitive actions coverage

Our current standard is to emit audit events for the following categories (not exhaustive list, but the major buckets you called out):
- RBAC: role create/update/delete, role assignment changes, permission policy updates
- Credentials: API key create/rotate/revoke; token issuance/config changes
- Policy/routing: routing policy updates, fallback policy changes, allow/deny list updates (where used)
- Data controls: retention setting changes, region/residency setting changes
- Audit pipeline: audit export destination create/update/delete; exporter enable/disable
- Private operations: install, upgrade, rollback, config apply, and credential rotation events from the Private installer

We’ll also include outcome="failure" for denied/failed attempts (e.g., unauthorized RBAC mutation), with error_class/error_code fields (no secrets).

3) SIEM export (Splunk + Sentinel)

- Splunk: HTTP Event Collector (HEC) target is supported. We batch events and retry on transient failures.
- Azure Sentinel: Log Analytics ingestion is supported (for Sentinel consumption). We batch and retry similarly.

Exporter behavior notes:
- Retries use exponential backoff; events are not dropped silently.
- If the exporter destination is down, the exporter will buffer up to a configured limit; once exceeded, it enters a "degraded" state and surfaces metrics/alerts (details in the guide).

4) Delivery semantics / duplicates / idempotency

We design delivery as at-least-once from producer to exporter, which can result in duplicates during retries.

How we recommend handling this:
- De-duplicate on event_id.
- event_id is a stable identifier for the logical event (i.e., retries preserve the same event_id).

We do not recommend attempting to de-dupe on timestamps or message contents.

5) Data handling (PII/redaction/retention)

We minimize PII in audit logs by design:
- We do not log secrets (API keys, tokens, raw credentials, request bodies containing prompts, etc.).
- Fields that could contain sensitive identifiers are either omitted or represented as stable IDs.
- Free-form strings are constrained; event_type and actor/target types are enumerated.

Retention in Private:
- Retention is configurable by the customer for the in-cluster audit store (where used).
- Exported events are governed by your SIEM retention policies.

Source IP note:
- source_ip is populated when present in the ingress context (API Gateway / Console). For certain installer-initiated actions in fully offline environments, source_ip may be unavailable; in those cases we still include operator identity where available and a local correlation id.

Sample events

We can share sample payloads for:
- rbac.role_assignment.updated
- credential.api_key.rotated

If you confirm your preferred format (JSON lines vs single JSON object per event) we’ll send a couple representative samples that match your target.

If it helps, I can also set up a 30-min working session with your SIEM engineer + our Security Engineering owner (Marcus, cc’d) to walk through field mapping.

Regards,
Aisha Rahman
Office of the CISO
Redwood Inference


---

From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@heliobank.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dana Whitaker <dana.whitaker@heliobank.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 14, 2026 at 11:21 AM
Subject: Re: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema + SIEM export questions

Thanks, this is helpful.

Two follow-ups:

1) You mentioned event_id is stable across retries. Is that true across service boundaries as well (e.g., Console -> control plane -> exporter), or only within the exporter?

2) For role changes, do you log the before/after diff? If yes, is it structured or a string? We want to avoid high-cardinality fields in Splunk.

Also, JSON lines (one event per line) is easiest for us.

-Raj


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@heliobank.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Dana Whitaker <dana.whitaker@heliobank.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 14, 2026 at 12:07 PM
Subject: Re: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema + SIEM export questions

Raj,

On (1): event_id is generated at event creation time by the producing service and preserved end-to-end through our pipeline (Console/control plane/installer -> audit ingestion -> exporter). Retries in the exporter do not mint a new ID.

We also include correlation fields to help tie together multi-hop actions:
- request_id (ingress request)
- trace_id (distributed trace, if enabled)
- admin_action_id (used for certain Console-admin workflows that fan out into multiple underlying mutations)

On (2): we include a constrained “change summary” for sensitive config changes.

For RBAC/role changes specifically:
- We include a structured summary when feasible (e.g., lists of permission identifiers added/removed), using enumerated permission IDs rather than free-form text.
- We avoid embedding full object snapshots in audit logs.
- We cap list sizes and truncate with an explicit flag when the change set is large (to protect cardinality and payload size).

If you’d like, we can provide a sample rbac.role_assignment.updated event with the change_summary fields populated so you can validate indexing in Splunk.

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering (Exec Owner)
Redwood Inference


---

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Whitaker <dana.whitaker@heliobank.com>
Cc: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@heliobank.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 14, 2026 at 2:26 PM
Subject: Re: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema + SIEM export questions

Dana/Raj,

Confirmed on JSON lines — that’s also our recommended format for SIEM pipelines.

Next steps:
- We’ll send two JSONL sample events (RBAC assignment update + API key rotation) aligned to schema v1 with change_summary populated.
- If you can share your Splunk sourcetype/index conventions, we’ll tailor the export guide section accordingly.

If you want to schedule the 30-min mapping session, please propose 2–3 times next week (PT/ET both fine).

Best,
Aisha

source.subject: Mutual Runbook & Closure Timeline: Launch Window Decision Path
messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sana Patel <sana.patel@orchidretail.com>, Tom Briggs <tom.briggs@orchidretail.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Mutual Runbook & Closure Timeline: Launch Window Decision Path

Hi Sana / Tom,

Thanks again for the productive call today — appreciated the clarity on your evaluation criteria. I attached the mutual action plan (MAP_Orchid_v3.xlsx) and an evaluation playbook that maps Redwood deliverables to your acceptance criteria. High-level proposal below so procurement and legal can align on the same timeline.

Key proposed milestones
- Evaluation window: Dec 8–Dec 19 (two-week hands-on trial with weekly checkpoints)
- Final decision (Go/No-Go): Week of Dec 22 (requested sign-off by Dec 23)
- DocuSign signature window: Dec 24–Dec 26 (48–72 hour signer window; DocuSign envelope to be sent after legal confirmation)
- Target production start (soft launch / canary): Jan 19, 2026; full cutover week of Feb 2 if onboarding goes smoothly

Dependencies / asks from Orchid Retail
- Confirm primary signers + availability window for Dec 24–26 (we can shift ±1 week if needed)
- Complete the short security checklist we discussed (SOC 2 scope + data handling note) by Dec 5
- Provide PO template if you require a pre-signed PO before DocuSign

Next steps (from our side)
- Camila will schedule the technical handoff for your evaluation team this week
- Marcus will share a canary runbook and acceptance checklist by Nov 27
- Naomi (legal) will prep the standard SOW + EULA for a quick redline

Attachments: MAP_Orchid_v3.xlsx; Evaluation_Playbook_v1.pdf

If those dates look off, tell me what works and we’ll adapt. Goal is to make the final decision window very predictable for your procurement team.

Best,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123


From: Sana Patel <sana.patel@orchidretail.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Briggs <tom.briggs@orchidretail.com>
Date: Wed, 26 Nov 2025 11:03:00 -08:00

source.subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
source.body:
From: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 8:42 AM
Subject: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
Attachments: AsterBank_Redwood_Security_Questionnaire_v3.7.xlsx

Hi Dr. Rahman,

As discussed in last week’s security review, attaching our questionnaire (v3.7). We’re specifically looking for detailed responses for the sections on:

- Supply chain / artifact integrity (signing and verification)
- Offline / air-gapped installation and upgrades
- Key custody, rotation, and revocation
- Evidence we can retain internally for auditors (who/what/when was signed)
- Audit logging produced by the product when verification succeeds/fails

We need responses by EOD Wednesday to stay on track for our December change window.

Thanks,
Priya Nair
Director, Security Assurance
AsterBank


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>
Cc: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
Attachments: Redwood_Private_Offline_Bundles_Signing_Overview_2025-11-10.pdf, Redwood_Artifact_Signing_Standard_v1.0.pdf, Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2025.pdf

Priya,

Thank you — we can meet your Wednesday deadline.

For speed, below are direct answers to the artifact signing / air-gap items while Connor and Marcus fill the spreadsheet sections (we’ll return the completed XLSX).

1) What is signed for offline/air-gapped installs?
- Our offline bundle includes the installer, deployment manifests/Helm charts, and a manifest enumerating bundle contents with cryptographic hashes.
- The signed object is the canonicalized bundle manifest (covers: versions/build identifiers, component list, SHA-256 digests for files included, and pointers to SBOM artifacts when provided).
- Verification is performed fully offline. The installer/preflight blocks install/upgrade if signature verification fails or if bundle contents do not match the signed manifest.

2) Signature format and algorithm
- We use a detached signature (e.g., manifest + manifest.sig) over a canonical representation of the manifest.
- Signing keys are based on modern asymmetric cryptography (Ed25519 in our current implementation) and signatures are verified by the on-prem installer.

3) Key custody / HSM / access controls
- Release signing keys are generated and stored in a dedicated key management environment with strict separation of duties.
- Access is limited to a small Release Engineering operator group with approvals and audited actions. Human access to private key material is restricted; key usage is mediated via controlled signing operations.
- The key custody process and approvals are auditable (we can provide a written description suitable for your vendor risk file; see attached overview).

4) Rotation and revocation
- We support a trust store model in the installer so multiple signing public keys can be trusted concurrently (to allow a smooth rotation window).
- We publish new public keys and provide a documented process for customers to update trusted roots in air-gapped environments.
- If a signing key is suspected compromised, our emergency response includes revocation, rotation, and a “fail closed” verification posture (new releases are signed only by the replacement key; customers update trust store prior to applying the update).

5) Downgrade/replay protection
- The signed manifest includes bundle version/build metadata. The installer maintains local install state to prevent accidental or malicious downgrade to older versions where policy requires it.

6) Audit logging / evidence
- The installer emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success/failure + reason codes). These can be retained locally and, where configured, exported to your SIEM pipeline.
- We can provide: (a) signing policy/standard document, (b) a summary of the key ceremony/controls, and (c) release metadata that correlates a running deployment to a specific signed bundle build ID.

Connor will send the completed questionnaire file back in a single response, and we can schedule a 30-minute follow-up to walk through any items your auditors flag.

Regards,
Aisha Rahman, PhD
VP Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


---

From: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 11:21 AM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)

Thanks Aisha — helpful.

Two follow-ups we need to answer internally:

1) Time validity: Do signatures require accurate time on the target host? Our air-gapped zones can have clock drift.
2) Customer-controlled trust root: Can AsterBank provide our own signing key/cert and have bundles signed by us instead of Redwood?

If the answer to #2 is “not today,” please confirm whether we can pin Redwood keys and get notified of rotations well in advance.

Tom


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>
Cc: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 1:07 PM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)

Tom,

On the two follow-ups:

1) Time validity / clock drift
- Our verification does not depend on contacting an online time source.
- The signature verification itself does not require accurate system time (cryptographic verification is time-independent).
- Where time can matter is if you choose to enforce certificate validity windows (e.g., X.509 chains). For the offline bundle signing path we’re using a key-based trust store rather than online PKI validation. Net: moderate clock drift in air-gapped environments is not expected to break verification.
- Practically, we still recommend a reliable internal time source for general operational hygiene; we’ll include a short “time skew” troubleshooting note in the install guide.

2) Customer-controlled trust root / customer-signed bundles
- Not in the initial phase. The current design is “Redwood-signed bundles, customer verifies offline.”
- We do support pinning to a set of Redwood public keys (trust store) and we support overlapping keys for rotations.
- If you have a hard policy requirement for customer-signed bundles, we can document it as a roadmap item and propose an interim control (e.g., you verify Redwood signatures + you separately hash/attest bundles after receipt in your internal artifact system).

Connor will also include the rotation notice expectations in the XLSX response.

Marcus
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>
Cc: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Nov 11, 2025 at 9:34 AM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
Attachments: AsterBank_Redwood_Security_Questionnaire_v3.7_COMPLETED_Redwood_2025-11-11.xlsx

Priya,

Attached is the completed questionnaire (v3.7) with the requested details in the “Supply Chain,” “Deployment Model,” and “Audit/Logging” tabs.

Highlights you may want to copy into your internal controls mapping:
- Bundles are verified offline; installer fails closed on signature failure or hash mismatch.
- Signing occurs in our release pipeline with restricted operator approvals; signing events are logged and retained per our security logging policy.
- Installer produces a local audit record for verification result + reason code, and includes bundle build ID to support change management evidence.
- Key rotation: we maintain overlapping trust roots during rotation windows; customers can pin trust to a published set of Redwood public keys. For air-gapped customers, we provide updated trust store artifacts via your approved secure transfer method.

If you have a specific notice period requirement (e.g., 60/90 days) for key rotation communications, please send it and we’ll align on an SLA for your account.

Regards,
Connor O’Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference
