title: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
preview:
From: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 8:42 AM
Subject: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
Attachments: AsterBank_Redwood_Security_Questionnaire_v3.7.xlsx

Hi Dr. Rahman,

As discussed in last week’s security review, attaching our questionnaire (v3.7). We’re specifically looking for detailed responses for the sections on:

- Supply chain / artifact integrity (signing and verification)
- Offline / air-gapped installation and upgrades
- Key custody, rotation, and revocation
- Evidence we can retain internally for auditors (who/what/when was signed)
- Audit logging produced by the product when verification succeeds/fails

We need responses by EOD Wednesday to stay on track for our December change window.

Thanks,
Priya Nair
Director, Security Assurance
AsterBank


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>
Cc: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)
Attachments: Redwood_Private_Offline_Bundles_Signing_Overview_2025-11-10.pdf, Redwood_Artifact_Signing_Standard_v1.0.pdf, Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2025.pdf

Priya,

Thank you — we can meet your Wednesday deadline.

For speed, below are direct answers to the artifact signing / air-gap items while Connor and Marcus fill the spreadsheet sections (we’ll return the completed XLSX).

1) What is signed for offline/air-gapped installs?
- Our offline bundle includes the installer, deployment manifests/Helm charts, and a manifest enumerating bundle contents with cryptographic hashes.
- The signed object is the canonicalized bundle manifest (covers: versions/build identifiers, component list, SHA-256 digests for files included, and pointers to SBOM artifacts when provided).
- Verification is performed fully offline. The installer/preflight blocks install/upgrade if signature verification fails or if bundle contents do not match the signed manifest.

2) Signature format and algorithm
- We use a detached signature (e.g., manifest + manifest.sig) over a canonical representation of the manifest.
- Signing keys are based on modern asymmetric cryptography (Ed25519 in our current implementation) and signatures are verified by the on-prem installer.

3) Key custody / HSM / access controls
- Release signing keys are generated and stored in a dedicated key management environment with strict separation of duties.
- Access is limited to a small Release Engineering operator group with approvals and audited actions. Human access to private key material is restricted; key usage is mediated via controlled signing operations.
- The key custody process and approvals are auditable (we can provide a written description suitable for your vendor risk file; see attached overview).

4) Rotation and revocation
- We support a trust store model in the installer so multiple signing public keys can be trusted concurrently (to allow a smooth rotation window).
- We publish new public keys and provide a documented process for customers to update trusted roots in air-gapped environments.
- If a signing key is suspected compromised, our emergency response includes revocation, rotation, and a “fail closed” verification posture (new releases are signed only by the replacement key; customers update trust store prior to applying the update).

5) Downgrade/replay protection
- The signed manifest includes bundle version/build metadata. The installer maintains local install state to prevent accidental or malicious downgrade to older versions where policy requires it.

6) Audit logging / evidence
- The installer emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success/failure + reason codes). These can be retained locally and, where configured, exported to your SIEM pipeline.
- We can provide: (a) signing policy/standard document, (b) a summary of the key ceremony/controls, and (c) release metadata that correlates a running deployment to a specific signed bundle build ID.

Connor will send the completed questionnaire file back in a single response, and we can schedule a 30-minute follow-up to walk through any items your auditors flag.

Regards,
Aisha Rahman, PhD
VP Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


---

From: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 11:21 AM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)

Thanks Aisha — helpful.

Two follow-ups we need to answer internally:

1) Time validity: Do signatures require accurate time on the target host? Our air-gapped zones can have clock drift.
2) Customer-controlled trust root: Can AsterBank provide our own signing key/cert and have bundles signed by us instead of Redwood?

If the answer to #2 is “not today,” please confirm whether we can pin Redwood keys and get notified of rotations well in advance.

Tom


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Jensen <tom.jensen@asterbank.com>
Cc: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@asterbank.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, security-questionnaires@asterbank.com
Date: Mon, Nov 10, 2025 at 1:07 PM
Subject: Re: AsterBank security questionnaire - artifact signing, offline installs, and audit evidence (Redwood Private)

Tom,

On the two follow-ups:

1) Time validity / clock drift
- Our verification does not depend on contacting an online time source.
- The signature verification itself does not require accurate system time (cryptographic verification is time-independent).
- Where time can matter is if you choose to enforce certificate validity windows (e.g., X.509 chains). For the offline bundle signing path we’re using a key-based trust store rather than online PKI validation. Net: moderate clock drift in air-gapped environments is not expected to break verification.
- Practically, we still recommend a reliable internal time source for general operational hygiene; we’ll include a short “time skew” troubleshooting note in the install guide.

2) Customer-controlled trust root / customer-signed bundles
- Not in the initial phase. The current design is “Redwood-signed bundles, customer verifies offline.”
- We do support pinning to a set of Redwood public keys (trust store) and we support overlapping keys for rotations.
- If you have a hard policy requirement for customer-signed bundles, we can document it as a roadmap item and propose an interim control (e.g., you verify Redwood signatures + you separately hash/attest bundles after receipt in your internal artifact system).

Connor will also include the rotation notice expectations in the XLSX response.

Marcus
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 25 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Week-of Close: Pipeline Sweep and Final Commit Actions

Team — quick sweep as we head into the final 48 hours of the quarter. Objective: lock down commits that are realistically closing this week and surface any risk that needs escalation to Revenue Ops/Finance.

Ask (short):
- Update the attached commit tracker with final commit amounts and expected sign dates by EOD tomorrow (Fri 9/26).
- Flag any deals where legal/finance redlines are blocking execution. Add short note in the "barrier" column.
- If you have a verbal yes but waiting for PO/DocuSign, mark as "verbal-commit" and estimate the sign date.

I'll be consolidating a rollup for the CEO and Finance on Monday morning; please be honest about pull-through. If you need me to call a champion, ping and I'll jump in.

Attachments: ACM-4732-commit-tracker.csv, pricing-summary-2025Q3.pdf

Thanks,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Head of Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0127

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Sep 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Week-of Close: Pipeline Sweep and Final Commit Actions

Aisha — updating now. Quick notes for ACM-4732 (Jordan's procurement lead):
- Sales Commit: $420k ARR (verbal yes after exec demo)
- Legal: sent SOW v3 with counter redlines (attached). Waiting on their legal team to confirm: expected 3 business days if no new changes.
- Billing: they asked for standard 60-day invoicing cadence (Procurement preference).

I'm attaching my marked SOW and a one-page summary I shared with the champion this morning. I'm planning a check-in call with Jordan tomorrow to push for DocuSign.

Attachments: Acme_SOW-v3-redlines.docx

-Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 (617) 555-0199

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Sep 2025 13:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Week-of Close: Pipeline Sweep and Final Commit Actions

Looping in the commit adjustments from my side. Quick changes to tracker:
- ACME-ENT upstream upsell: moved $75k from "pipeline" to "probable" (sales champion confirmed budget). New expected sign date: 2025-09-29.
- ACME POC: $50k (pilot) — low confidence, left as "pipeline" and put a note: "waiting on security questionnaire response".

Also: I pinged Tom at our CloudOps partner about a deployment window; he replied that we can provision dedicated infra by 9/30 but needs final region confirmation. I put Tom on Cc below when appropriate.

-Marcus
Marcus Lin
Senior AE | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com

From: Jordan Price <jordan.price@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Fri, 26 Sep 2025 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Week-of Close: Pipeline Sweep and Final Commit Actions

Ben / Aisha — thanks for the update. We reviewed SOW v3 and sent a few redline suggestions to your legal team (you should have received an email from procurement@). On timing: we can target a signature next week, but I need two things to lock internal approvals earlier:

1) Final pricing sheet showing the quarterly cadence and any available onboarding credit.
2) A short note confirming that the Dedicated deployment can be staged in us-west-2 and that there are no additional network egress costs beyond your published pricing.

If you can get those over by Monday morning, I can push procurement to expedite.

Jordan Price
VP IT Procurement | Acme Health
jordan.price@acmehealth.com
+1 (206) 555-0148

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 26 Sep 2025 14:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Acme SOW redlines / Finance checklist

Forwarding Jordan's procurement note and please note Finance asked for:
- Confirmed payment terms (Net 60) — see Ben's note above.
- Purchase order process: Acme uses PO before signature for >$250k
- Need a high-level billing contact on file.

I've attached the finance checklist and added a column in the commit tracker for PO status. Please update. I'm happy to triage with Finance if anyone hits a blocker.

Attachments: pricing-summary-2025Q3.pdf

Laura Bennett
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 27 Sep 2025 17:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Close-of-Day Rollup: ACM-4732 + All Final Reads (please act)

Thanks for the quick updates. Consolidated rollup below (pulled from the tracker) — please sanity-check live in the sheet tomorrow before I send to Finance/CEO: 

- ACM-4732 (Acme Health): sales commit $420k ARR (verbal); legal redlines outstanding; procurement requested final pricing and region confirmation. Likely sign: 2025-09-30 -> status: at-risk -> action: Ben to provide final pricing sheet & confirm region by Mon 9/29 10:00 PT.
- ACME-ENT upsell: $75k, probable, sign date 2025-09-29; action: Marcus to confirm PO workflow and internal approver.

source.subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness
messages:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned on procurement and billing ops: 

Summary from the call
- LedgerWave is excited about Redwood for the KYC/transaction enrichment pipeline.
- Procurement leans toward using a PO process for all 3rd-party SaaS purchases. They still evaluate marketplace options (AWS Marketplace) depending on discounts and SSO integration ease.

Open items for billing/procurement (we need confirmation from you):
1) Commercial path: PO (preferred) vs. card vs. marketplace — which will your procurement team route for this engagement?
2) Invoice requirements: billing address, VAT/tax ID, company legal name exactly as it should appear on invoices.
3) Net terms: do you require Net 30/45? Any internal approvals needed for Net terms?
4) Invoice format: PDF invoice (email), e-invoice/EDI, or custom fields required?
5) Remittance and purchase order workflow — do you need invoices to reference a PO number for payment to be processed?
6) Billing contact name + email for our Finance team to reach out.

Proposed next steps
- If you can attach your PO template and any billing-spec doc, I’ll loop in our Billing Ops (Karthik) to confirm we can produce invoices in the requested format.
- If procurement wants Marketplace, we can pull together a short comparison of expected differences (discounts, billing cadence, invoice fields).
- I’ll share a draft SOW/initial invoice example once we lock the path.

Would love your responses on the numbered items above — if easier, feel free to attach your PO template and billing requirements and I’ll coordinate internally.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: none in this message
From: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, John Hayes <john.hayes@ledgerwave.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 17:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Priya —

Thanks for the clear recap. Answers inline below and I attached the PO template + billing checklist for your team.

1) Commercial path: Procurement prefers PO-based purchases for anything over $10k. For smaller pilots they will accept company card, but for rollout we expect PO. They are open to AWS Marketplace if we can get the same billing detail and our legal team signs the marketplace terms.

2) Invoice requirements:
- Legal name: LedgerWave, Inc.
- Billing address: 2308 Harbor Drive, Suite 400, Boston, MA 02110
- VAT/Tax ID: US EIN 12-3456789 (we are US entity, no VAT).

3) Net terms: We prefer Net 30. For pilots they may pay on receipt if we provide PO + invoice with correct GL codes.

4) Invoice format: PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com is fine. They also accept a structured XLS with line-level GL codes (I attached a sample spreadsheet). No EDI required at this stage.

5) Yes — invoice must reference the PO number. Payments won't be scheduled without a PO on the invoice.

6) Billing ops contact: John Hayes, Billing Ops Manager — john.hayes@ledgerwave.com, +1 (617) 555-1010.

Attached: LedgerWave_PO_template.pdf, LedgerWave_billing_requirements.xlsx

Let me know if you need anything else to get the initial SOW/invoice example over to us. We are aiming to have procurement sign-off within 2–3 weeks.

Best,
Elena
Director, Strategic Partnerships | LedgerWave Inc.
elena@ledgerwave.com
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 09:38:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Folks — forwarding LedgerWave’s reply and the attached PO + billing checklist. Key asks for Billing Ops:
- Generate a sample invoice (PDF) that references a placeholder PO and uses LedgerWave’s legal name/address and GL line structure as per the XLS.
- Confirm Karthik whether we can produce an XLS invoice variant with line-level GL codes for AP.
- Confirm whether we can offer Net 30 in an initial pilot and what approvals are needed.

LedgerWave (cc’d procurement & John Hayes) wants PO on invoice and will accept PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com.

Can you please take point on producing the sample invoice and we’ll send an SOW draft tied to that invoice amount?

Thanks — Priya

> On Sep 15, 2025, at 17:02, Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com> wrote:
> [quoted original message and attachments]
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 11:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Thanks Priya — tagging this for SalesOps/Billing queue. Quick checklist from our side: 
- We can produce a PDF invoice that includes PO reference, company legal name and billing address.
- We can also attach an XLS with line-level GL codes; we will need the final GL mappings from LedgerWave (they provided a sample column, we can map to our chart of accounts).
- Net 30 is doable for this customer; standard credit review applies for the committed spend (if they convert beyond pilot there will be a quick credit check). For the pilot, billing can be set to invoice-on-delivery with Net 30.
- To enable Marketplace billing we’ll need to route through our AWS Marketplace listing — that impacts pricing and discounting but simplifies procurement for some customers.

Action items I’ll own:
1) Produce a sample PDF invoice and XLS variant (using placeholder PO: PO-RED-0001). Target: EOD Wed 17 Sep.
2) Coordinate with Finance to confirm any approval needed to offer Net 30 for the full contract.

Marcus — loop Finance if you want us to expedite credit review.

Karthik
Billing & Sales Ops | Redwood Inference
From: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Sep 2025 09:05:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Great — thanks everyone. Confirming:
- PO workflow is the route for production.
- John (cc’d) will accept the sample invoice and confirm GL mapping.
- Procurement timeline: sign-off target ~ end of September.

John Hayes (ap@ledgerwave.com) — I’m handing this to you for GL mapping confirmation and to validate the sample invoice once Redwood posts it.

We are available for a quick 15-min sync if that helps once the sample invoice is ready.

Thanks,
Elena

text:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned on procurement and billing ops: 

Summary from the call
- LedgerWave is excited about Redwood for the KYC/transaction enrichment pipeline.
- Procurement leans toward using a PO process for all 3rd-party SaaS purchases. They still evaluate marketplace options (AWS Marketplace) depending on discounts and SSO integration ease.

Open items for billing/procurement (we need confirmation from you):
1) Commercial path: PO (preferred) vs. card vs. marketplace — which will your procurement team route for this engagement?
2) Invoice requirements: billing address, VAT/tax ID, company legal name exactly as it should appear on invoices.
3) Net terms: do you require Net 30/45? Any internal approvals needed for Net terms?
4) Invoice format: PDF invoice (email), e-invoice/EDI, or custom fields required?
5) Remittance and purchase order workflow — do you need invoices to reference a PO number for payment to be processed?
6) Billing contact name + email for our Finance team to reach out.

Proposed next steps
- If you can attach your PO template and any billing-spec doc, I’ll loop in our Billing Ops (Karthik) to confirm we can produce invoices in the requested format.
- If procurement wants Marketplace, we can pull together a short comparison of expected differences (discounts, billing cadence, invoice fields).
- I’ll share a draft SOW/initial invoice example once we lock the path.

Would love your responses on the numbered items above — if easier, feel free to attach your PO template and billing requirements and I’ll coordinate internally.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: none in this message


From: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, John Hayes <john.hayes@ledgerwave.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 17:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Thanks for the clear recap. Answers inline below and I attached the PO template + billing checklist for your team.

1) Commercial path: Procurement prefers PO-based purchases for anything over $10k. For smaller pilots they will accept company card, but for rollout we expect PO. They are open to AWS Marketplace if we can get the same billing detail and our legal team signs the marketplace terms.

2) Invoice requirements:
- Legal name: LedgerWave, Inc.
- Billing address: 2308 Harbor Drive, Suite 400, Boston, MA 02110
- VAT/Tax ID: US EIN 12-3456789 (we are US entity, no VAT).

3) Net terms: We prefer Net 30. For pilots they may pay on receipt if we provide PO + invoice with correct GL codes.

4) Invoice format: PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com is fine. They also accept a structured XLS with line-level GL codes (I attached a sample spreadsheet). No EDI required at this stage.

5) Yes — invoice must reference the PO number. Payments won't be scheduled without a PO on the invoice.

6) Billing ops contact: John Hayes, Billing Ops Manager — john.hayes@ledgerwave.com, +1 (617) 555-1010.

Attached: LedgerWave_PO_template.pdf, LedgerWave_billing_requirements.xlsx

Let me know if you need anything else to get the initial SOW/invoice example over to us. We are aiming to have procurement sign-off within 2–3 weeks.

Best,
Elena

elena@ledgerwave.com

source.subject: Clarify press & reference carveouts for MSA + Order Form
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ellie Harper <e.harper@clearsky.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-12T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions

Hi Ellie,

Thanks for the call earlier — great to see alignment on scope and timing. I attached our latest order form (OrderForm_ClearSky_v3_redlined.pdf) with a couple of small edits your procurement team might expect. There are two outstanding items that legal flagged on their side and I wanted to surface them early:

1) Publicity & logo use: we have a standard clause that allows mutual press announcements and logo reference with prior written consent. Your Counsel mentioned wanting an embargo until your public product launch and a carveout for customer testimonials. Can you confirm the desired approach?

2) Confidentiality carveouts: your team asked for a narrow exception to share anonymized performance metrics in aggregate marketing materials. We want to ensure that any anonymized usage doesn't reveal PHI or PII.

Timeline: our finance team needs a signed order form by EOD Friday (2025-08-15) to hit the September onboarding window. If publicity/approval gating blocks that, we can propose an interim clause that preserves the launch embargo but allows limited references (company name + generic solution descriptor) for customer lists.

Attaching the redline. Would you loop in whoever owns your publicity approvals (legal/PR) and send their preferred language? Happy to work quickly on compromise language.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0194

Attachment: OrderForm_ClearSky_v3_redlined.pdf (application/pdf, 185 KB)

From: Ellie Harper <e.harper@clearsky.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Dana Lowe <dana.lowe@clearsky.io>
Date: 2025-08-12T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions

Ben — thanks for sending this over so quickly. Looping in our legal/business ops (Dana) per your ask. A couple quick notes from our side: 

• Embargo: we require a soft embargo until our product launch (targeting mid-October). We can agree to a mutual announcement clause with a firm "no public statements" window until we grant written sign-off.

• Logo & reference rights: our legal prefers prior written consent for logo usage and wants a narrow testimonial carveout that allows quotes from named contacts but restricts performance metrics unless previously agreed.

Attached is our suggested publicity language (ClearSky_preferred-publicity-language.docx). The language includes a short approval timeline (5 business days) for logo approvals — if redlines would push the signing deadline, we may accept a temporary marketing-only name/reference (no logo, no metrics) while final language is negotiated.

Dana — any legal pushback or clarifications?

Best,
Ellie

Attachment: ClearSky_preferred-publicity-language.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 12 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-12T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: RE: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions — ClearSky wants embargo + 5-day approval

Team — forwarding ClearSky's note. Deal timeline is tight (finance wants signature by Friday 8/15). Highlights: embargo until mid-Oct + prior written consent for logo use + 5 business day approval window. They said they'd accept a temporary "marketing-only name/reference" with no logo/metrics if language can't be finalized by signing.

Questions:
- Can we accept the 5 business day approval window?
- Is a temporary reference (company name only) acceptable from a risk perspective?
- If we insist on a narrower window, what language would we propose to get them to sign this week?

Context: this customer will be a marquee reference for our analytics integrations; marketing/product want to keep the option for a short case study live soon after launch. I don't want to overpromise public quotes. Appreciate an actionable set of options I can share with Ellie.

Thanks — Ben

On 2025-08-12 13:45, Ellie Harper wrote:
> Ben — thanks for sending this over so quickly. Looping in our legal/business ops (Dana) per your ask. A couple quick notes from our side: ...
>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-13T09:21:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: RE: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions — ClearSky wants embargo + 5-day approval

Ben — quick legal take and recommended compromise below. Key constraints: (1) we need the ability to reference customers for sales/marketing; (2) we cannot permit unbounded use of logos/metrics without prior review; (3) we should avoid operationally tight approval SLAs that could create silent holds on go-to-market activities.

Suggested approach (redline language you can send to Ellie):
"Mutual Publicity and References: Except as required by applicable law, neither party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence of the relationship without the other party's prior written consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties may include each other's company name in lists of customers and short-form marketing materials ("Customer List") without prior written consent, provided that no logos, customer-specific performance metrics, or testimonials shall be used without prior written approval. Where prior approval is required for logo or testimonial use, the approving party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to respond within five (5) business days of receipt of a written request. For clarity, approval shall not be unreasonably withheld; if no response is provided within ten (10) business days, approval shall be deemed denied and the parties shall revert to using a marketing-only name/reference until approval is granted."

Rationale: this gives marketing what they need (Customer List usage) while forcing a reasonable cadence for logo/testimonial approvals. We added an explicit fallback (deemed denied after ten days) to avoid open-ended hold-ups. If they accept, we can proceed to sign the order form this week and finalize longer-form testimonial language post-signature.

Other notes: Rachel — confirm we are OK with the "deemed denied after ten days" fallback operationally? Marcus — any procurement red flags on the 10-day clause? Olga — any privacy/data concerns with the anonymized metrics carveout they requested?

If everyone signs off, Ben, send this to Ellie and propose signing by 8/15 with the fallback language included in the order form.

— Marissa
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

On 2025-08-12 15:05 Ben Carter wrote:
> Team — forwarding ClearSky's note. Deal timeline is tight (finance wants signature by Friday 8/15). ...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ellie Harper <e.harper@clearsky.io>
Cc: Dana Lowe <dana.lowe@clearsky.io>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-14T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Final: Proposed compromise language + next steps for signature

Ellie / Dana — thanks for the quick turnaround earlier this week. Per Dana's guidance and our internal counsel, please find the compromise language below; this is what we propose to include in the MSA / Order Form prior to signature so we can keep to the onboarding timeline: 

"Mutual Publicity and References: Except as required by applicable law, neither party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence of the relationship without the other party's prior written consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties may include each other's company name in lists of customers and short-form marketing materials ("Customer List") without prior written consent, provided that no logos, customer-specific performance metrics, or testimonials shall be used without prior written approval. Where prior approval is required for logo or testimonial use, the approving party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to respond within five (5) business days of receipt of a written request. If no response is provided within ten (10) business days, the requesting party may use a marketing-only name/reference (no logo, no metrics) pending final approval."

To confirm next steps:
1) If ClearSky approves the language above, we will insert it into the OrderForm_ClearSky_v3_redlined.pdf and proceed to signature.
2) Finance has requested final counters by Friday 2025-08-15 to meet the onboarding milestone. If the final publicity language is acceptable, can Dana reply with a brief confirmation?

We appreciate your flexibility on the approval window. Once signed we will treat any testimonial or metric requests as a secondary ticket requiring PR/legal approval.

Best regards,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0194

Attachment: OrderForm_ClearSky_v3_redlined.pdf (application/pdf, 186 KB)

- If we insist on a narrower window, what language would we propose to get them to sign this week?

Context: this customer will be a marquee reference for our analytics integrations; marketing/product want to keep the option for a short case study live soon after launch. I don't want to overpromise public quotes. Appreciate an actionable set of options I can share with Ellie.

Thanks — Ben

On 2025-08-12 13:45, Ellie Harper wrote:
> Ben — thanks for sending this over so quickly. Looping in our legal/business ops (Dana) per your ask. A couple quick notes from our side: ...
>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-13T09:21:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: RE: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions — ClearSky wants embargo + 5-day approval

Ben — quick legal take and recommended compromise below. Key constraints: (1) we need the ability to reference customers for sales/marketing; (2) we cannot permit unbounded use of logos/metrics without prior review; (3) we should avoid operationally tight approval SLAs that could create silent holds on go-to-market activities.

Suggested approach (redline language you can send to Ellie):
"Mutual Publicity and References: Except as required by applicable law, neither party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence of the relationship without the other party's prior written consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties may include each other's company name in lists of customers and short-form marketing materials (\"Customer List\") without prior written consent, provided that no logos, customer-specific performance metrics, or testimonials shall be used without prior written approval. Where prior approval is required for logo or testimonial use, the approving party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to respond within five (5) business days of receipt of a written request. For clarity, approval shall not be unreasonably withheld; if no response is provided within ten (10) business days, approval shall be deemed denied and the parties shall revert to using a marketing-only name/reference until approval is granted."

Rationale: this gives marketing what they need (Customer List usage) while forcing a reasonable cadence for logo/testimonial approvals. We added an explicit fallback (deemed denied after ten days) to avoid open-ended hold-ups. If they accept, we can proceed to sign the order form this week and finalize longer-form testimonial language post-signature.

Other notes: Rachel — confirm we are OK with the "deemed denied after ten days" fallback operationally? Marcus — any procurement red flags on the 10-day clause? Olga — any privacy/data concerns with the anonymized metrics carveout they requested?

If everyone signs off, Ben, send this to Ellie and propose signing by 8/15 with the fallback language included in the order form.

— Marissa
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

On 2025-08-12 15:05 Ben Carter wrote:
> Team — forwarding ClearSky's note. Deal timeline is tight (finance wants signature by Friday 8/15). ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ellie Harper <e.harper@clearsky.io>
Cc: Dana Lowe <dana.lowe@clearsky.io>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-14T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Final: Proposed compromise language + next steps for signature

Ellie / Dana — thanks for the quick turnaround earlier this week. Per Dana's guidance and our internal counsel, please find the compromise language below; this is what we propose to include in the MSA / Order Form prior to signature so we can keep to the onboarding timeline: 

"Mutual Publicity and References: Except as required by applicable law, neither party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence of the relationship without the other party's prior written consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties may include each other's company name in lists of customers and short-form marketing materials (\"Customer List\") without prior written consent, provided that no logos, customer-specific performance metrics, or testimonials shall be used without prior written approval. Where prior approval is required for logo or testimonial use, the approving party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to respond within five (5) business days of receipt of a written request. If no response is provided within ten (10) business days, the requesting party may use a marketing-only name/reference (no logo, no metrics) pending final approval."

To confirm next steps:
1) If ClearSky approves the language above, we will insert it into the OrderForm_ClearSky_v3_redlined.pdf and proceed to signature.
2) Finance has requested final counters by Friday 2025-08-15 to meet the onboarding milestone. If the final publicity language is acceptable, can Dana reply with a brief confirmation?

We appreciate your flexibility on the approval window. Once signed we will treat any testimonial or metric requests as a secondary ticket requiring PR/legal approval.

Best regards,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0194

Attachment: OrderForm_ClearSky_v3_redlined.pdf (application/pdf, 186 KB)

source.subject: SOC2 applicability for deployment modes (Hosted / Dedicated / Private)
messages:
From: Elena Torres <security@acme-corp.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 12:18:00 -0700
Subject: SOC2 report + scope question for Dedicated/Private deployments

Hi Alex / Redwood team,

We're in the final stages of our vendor security review for Acme's product expansion. Two quick asks so procurement can close:

1) Can you confirm whether Redwood's SOC 2 Type II report covers Dedicated (reserved GPU pools) that we've contracted with you? Our security team assumes the SOC 2 report covers only multi-tenant hosted services unless otherwise stated.
2) For Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments, do you provide a bridge letter or any attestation we can present to auditors?

If helpful, please point us to the specific section/pages in your SOC2 where scope boundaries are described. We need answers by EOD Aug 3 for the procurement deadline.

Thanks,
Elena Torres
Head of Security Programs
Acme Corp

--
Elena Torres
Acme Corp | security@acme-corp.com
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Torres <security@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jul 2025 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SOC2 report + scope question for Dedicated/Private deployments

Hi Elena — thanks for the note and timeline. Attaching our latest redacted SOC 2 Type II report and a draft bridge-letter template for Private deployments (see attachments). A few quick points while Laura and Marcus confirm the nitty-gritty: 

- Hosted (Redwood-managed multi-tenant API): covered by the SOC 2 Type II report for the audit period (report attached).
- Dedicated ("Redwood Dedicated" reservations): typically covered when the customer purchases dedicated capacity that is provisioned and operated by Redwood within our control plane (same control environment as Hosted). If the customer provisions Dedicated but manages the underlying infrastructure (customer-managed on their cloud account), that configuration is out-of-scope for Redwood's SOC 2.
- Private / VPC / on-prem: out-of-scope for Redwood's SOC 2. We can provide a bridge letter and evidence bundle to support your audit.

Laura / Marcus — can you confirm the exact language we should send to customers and whether we can provide a one-page scope statement to include in procurement? Elena — I'll follow up with a formal note and SLA/contract implications by Aug 3.

Best,
Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0147

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2024-07-redacted.pdf, SOC2-bridge-letter-template.docx
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 31 Jul 2025 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: recommended scope language and evidence for Acme

Alex / Marcus — drafting recommended customer-facing language and evidence checklist below. Please use this as the reply to Acme; I also uploaded the detailed mapping workbook to our shared Drive (see link).

Recommended plain-language summary for customers:
- Redwood's SOC 2 Type II (report attached) covers Redwood-managed inference services and the control environment operated by Redwood during the audit period (controls around change management, logical access, encryption, incident response, etc.).
- For Redwood Dedicated where capacity is reserved in Redwood's tenancy and Redwood operates the GPU pools, those instances are included within the scope of the SOC 2 report.
- Customer-managed Dedicated (where the customer runs Redwood software in their own cloud account or on-prem infrastructure) and Private / VPC / on-prem deployments are NOT covered by Redwood's SOC 2. For those deployment modes we provide:
  * A bridge letter describing which Redwood controls remain applicable and which are the customer's responsibility.
  * A configuration checklist and evidence bundle showing how Redwood secures the control plane components we still operate.
  * Assistance during customer audits (evidence collection, control descriptions) under an NDA.

Evidence checklist to offer Acme (we can provide redacted items where needed):
- Redacted SOC2 Type II report (attached)
- Management's description of controls (one-pager scope statement)
- Bridge letter (template attached)
- Control mapping workbook (scope-mapping-dedicated-hosted.xlsx)
- Sample logs/traces for control-plane events (time-limited access)

Timelines / process notes:
- Bridge letter: 3–5 business days for a templated version; fully customized bridge letter (legal review) may take 2–3 weeks.
- Evidence bundle: usually 5–10 business days depending on redaction requests.

Marcus — please confirm the canonical wording for the contract clause that indicates when Dedicated is Redwood-managed vs customer-managed, so we don't create procurement back-and-forth.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Director, Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Torres <security@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 3 Aug 2025 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Final: SOC2 scope boundaries and next steps (Hosted / Dedicated / Private)

Elena — quick follow-up with the clarified, customer-ready summary and next steps (per Laura's recommendations). I've also attached the redacted SOC 2 report and the bridge-letter template.

Customer-ready scope summary (please feel free to copy into your procurement docs):
- Redwood SOC 2 Type II covers Redwood-managed Hosted inference and any Dedicated capacity that is provisioned and operated by Redwood in Redwood's tenancy.
- Deployments where the customer provisions or manages the infrastructure (customer-managed Dedicated, VPC-connected self-hosted instances, or fully on-prem installations) are not covered by Redwood's SOC 2. These deployments require a bridge letter or customer attestation for audit purposes.

Offered deliverables and timeline for Acme: 
- Deliverable: templated bridge letter + one-page scope statement. ETA: 3 business days.
- Deliverable: redacted evidence bundle (SOC2 report, control descriptions, mapping workbook). ETA: 7 business days after confirmation of redaction scope.
- Request from Acme: please confirm which Dedicated instance(s) you have with Redwood and whether they are operated by Redwood (we need tenancy/account IDs). That will determine whether we can include them in the SOC 2 evidence package.

Next steps:
1) Reply with the Dedicated instance identifiers (or confirm none are customer-managed).
2) Tell us any special redaction requirements for PII or commercial terms.
3) We'll issue the templated bridge letter and scope one-pager once we have the Dedicated IDs.

If useful, happy to set a quick 20-minute call with Laura and Marcus to walk your audit team through the mapping.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0147

Attachments: Redacted SOC2 (Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2024-07-redacted.pdf), SOC2-bridge-letter-template.docx, scope-mapping-dedicated-hosted.xlsx

source.subject: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)
messages:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-14T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Hi Alicia — thanks again for your time this morning. Quick recap from the demo and the action items we discussed: 

- Summary: You walked us through your ingestion flow (EHR exports -> Medexa ETL -> anonymization step -> Redwood inference). Primary ask is minimizing retention of raw PII and providing fine-grained controls for audit logs.
- Questions you raised we promised to follow up on: 0-retention options for PII, what we log for debugging vs audit, and whether we can shorten logs for non-audit events.

Next steps we propose: 
1) I attached a short proposal (redwood-data-retention-proposal.pdf) showing two options: (A) transient-only (0 retention of input payloads after inference except for immediate KV cache lifetime), and (B) 7-day ephemeral retention with encrypted-at-rest fast-delete.
2) If option A still leaves you unable to meet compliance, we'll surface a formal exception process (medexa-retention-exception-form.docx) and loop in Rishi from Security to provide a written sign-off path.
3) Marcus will draft commercial guardrails for a pilot (SLA, price, and rollback).

Can you confirm which option you want to evaluate in a 30-day pilot? If you prefer, we can prepare a DocuSign-ready exception request for your security team.

Thanks — Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0112
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Medexa Procurement <procurement@medexahealth.com>
Date: 2025-07-14T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Hi Marissa — appreciate the follow-up and the concise options. A couple clarifying questions before we pick Option A or B: 

1) 0-retention for raw PII: when you say "transient-only", does that include all debug logs and request metadata? Our compliance team insists that any logs that could be reconstructed with external tooling must be excluded from long-term storage.
2) Audit & eDiscovery: we need to retain evidence for certain events for 90 days. Can you separate audit logs (retained 90 days) from operational/debug logs (shortened retention)?
3) Technical proof: can your team provide a short-runbook of deletion guarantees (e.g., delete job cadence, truncated KV cache behavior, and any background replication windows)? We may need this for our internal audit.

If you can confirm separation of audit vs non-audit logs and provide that runbook, Procurement is willing to move to a pilot. We will likely request the exception form if Option A is chosen (0 retention for payloads).

Thanks — Alicia

[original message quoted above]
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Marissa / Marcus — quick technical and compliance note to support the reply to Medexa. High level answers below; I also attached a one-page runbook to this thread (internal doc) — happy to convert to customer-friendly language if you want to send it out.

- 0-retention (Option A): We can offer a transient-only mode where payloads are not persisted beyond the inference operation. Constraints: we still produce ephemeral debug traces that exist only in-memory and are flushed on process termination; any background replication windows (for HA) are <=30 minutes and covered by encryption-in-transit policies. This mode requires customers to forgo server-side logging for full request payloads.
- Audit vs operational logs: We can implement a dual-retention model. Audit logs (authentication events, model version used, high-level request IDs) can be retained for 90 days. Operational/debug logs (stack traces, full request payloads) can be set to a configurable TTL — options: 0 days (never persisted), 7 days, or 30 days.
- Deletion runbook (summary): delete jobs run on a cron every hour for operational logs > TTL; a background purger processes KV cache truncation and zeroes memory-mapped pages before freeing; for encrypted snapshots we rotate keys and mark blobs for fast-delete. I'll add exact commands and retention cron samples if Medexa needs them for their audit packet.

Proposal for pilot: 
- Pilot period: 30 days
- Retention configuration: audit logs = 90 days; operational logs = 7 days (customer can request 0 days for specific routes via exception form)
- Exceptions: We can accept a signed exception request for route-level 0-retention on payloads, with an internal security review and an SLA amendment (limited support entitlement during exception window).

Marcus: if you can draft the pilot commercial note (30 days, reduced support SLA, and rollback plan), I'll prepare a customer-ready runbook and exception template.

Rishi

- Retention: audit logs retained 90 days; operational logs set to 7 days by default
- Route exceptions: Medexa will submit exception forms for three high-risk routes requesting 0-retention for payloads during the pilot; exceptions require signed acknowledgement and reduced support SLA (as Rishi outlined)
- Deliverables: Redwood to provide the deletion runbook and the exception template in DocuSign format; procurement will sign on receipt.

source.subject: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Diana —

Thanks again for the time last week. As we talked through during the demo, the fastest path for Medicore to get a short pilot live (1–2 weeks of ingest + 2 weeks evaluation) is if we align on a light MAP that sequences technical acceptance, security evidence, and signature gates.

I pulled together a first-pass MAP (attached) that maps out the:
- Evaluation milestones (data ingest, baseline eval, accuracy check, UAT)
- Contract gating (MSA signature, DPA completion, order form sign-off)
- Procurement items (SOW/PO, billing contact, payment terms)

Can you confirm these constraints from your side:
1) Legal needs a full DPA review by Medicore legal before MSA countersign — true/false?
2) Procurement requires an internal PO number before vendor activation?
3) Any absolute blackout dates for June/July that would block your security team?

If that lines up, Karthik (cc) and I will propose a minimal sequencing that keeps the evaluation independent of final order-form language until you hit the UAT pass. Attached: medicore_map_v1.xlsx (MAP draft).

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0126
Redwood Inference

Attachments: medicore_map_v1.xlsx (MAP draft)

From: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@medicore.com>, Elaine Wu <elaine.wu@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the MAP. Quick answers from procurement/legal side:
- DPA: Legal prefers to agree DPA terms before any provisioning for data containing PHI. For a limited pilot we can consider a scoped data subset and a short-form DPA addendum, but Legal (Sanjay) needs to review first.
- PO: Procurement typically issues PO before we process payment, but IT sometimes allows a temporary vendor onboarding form that lets us stand up test accounts for pilots. I'll check if we can do a temporary onboarding to start ingest without full PO.
- Blackouts: Our security reviewers are away Jul 3–10 (conference travel). Any gating work in that window will slow us down.

Sanjay, Elaine — looping you in to confirm DPA path.

Best,
Diana Park
Director, Product Operations | Medicore Health
D: 212-555-0444

---
On Mon, Jun 16, 2025 at 09:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Diana —
> 
> Thanks again for the time last week...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 08:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Diana / Ava —

Thanks for the clarity. Based on your constraints I recommend a two-track sequencing that minimizes legal blocking for the pilot provisioning:

Track A (Technical onboarding — low legal friction) — timeline: Day 0–10
- Day 0: Temporary vendor onboarding form signed by Medicore IT (no PO) -> Redwood provisions sandbox tenant (no PHI)
- Day 1–4: Data subset ingest (de-identified) + baseline eval run
- Day 5–8: Joint review of metrics; iterate prompts/configs
- Day 9–10: UAT pass for feature set we agreed as pilot success

Track B (Contracting & procurement) — runs in parallel, aim to finish by Day 14
- Day 0–7: Medicore legal reviews short-form DPA addendum + Redwood sends MSA summary (redwood_msa_summary.pdf attached)
- Day 8–12: Procurement issues PO / order form (or provisional PO)
- Day 12–14: Signatures complete, move pilot to production tenant if desired

Key assumptions / asks from Medicore:
- If Legal insists on full DPA prior to any provisioning we will adjust and keep initial runs strictly on synthetic/de-identified data.
- Provide a single procurement contact and billing email to speed PO issuance.

I'll attach a checklist CSV for our side (procurement_checklist.csv) to track items. Marcus — can you review the acceptance gating bullets (below) and confirm they look OK to ship as the MAP success criteria?

Acceptance gating (proposal):
- Data ingest complete (ingested rows match sample schema)
- Top-line latency and throughput within target (95thp < 350ms)
- Baseline quality: precision/recall thresholds met on test set
- No P0 security flags during vetting

Thanks,
Karthik
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_msa_summary.pdf, procurement_checklist.csv

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jun 2025 14:06:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

All —

I reviewed the acceptance gating and have two small edits to keep the pilot focused and unblock signature sequencing:
1) Split the "quality" gate into two: baseline metric pass for the instrumented test set (used for internal validation) and a lightweight business-signoff milestone (Medicore product owner confirms results are "fit for purpose"). This lets Legal/Procurement sign the order-form while business-side confirms fit.
2) Add a contingency: if Medicore legal requests changes to the DPA that materially affect data processing, we will cap the pilot to de-identified data and pause PHI-related evaluations until DPA is agreed.

If you (Diana/Sanjay) can confirm the temporary onboarding form option, I propose we target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 and run the technical Track A in parallel with contracting Track B through Jul 10 (noting your blackout Jul 3–10). That timing keeps the evaluation window clear of major holidays and aligns with our engineering squad availability.

Marcus Lin
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0899

On Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 08:27 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Diana / Ava —
> 
> Thanks for the clarity. Based on your constraints I recommend a two-track sequencing...

From: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>, Elaine Wu <elaine.wu@medicore.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Marcus / Ava / Karthik —

Thanks — confirmation from our side:
- Procurement can provide a temporary onboarding form this week to enable sandbox provisioning (no PO required up-front). I will route it to IT and copy you when complete.
- Legal (Sanjay) will start review of the short-form DPA addendum; if we need a full DPA we’ll flag it but will try to keep the pilot scoped to de-identified data until that is concluded.
- We agree to target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 for Track A. Given our review calendar, full contracting (Track B) will likely finish by Jul 14 if all gates pass.

I'll attach the onboarding checklist Medicore uses (internal) in a follow-up — for now, please go ahead and provision the sandbox tenant on your side on Jun 30.

Best,
Diana
Director, Product Operations | Medicore Health

Attachments: (will share Medicore onboarding checklist in follow-up)

Subject: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Diana —

Thanks again for the time last week. As we talked through during the demo, the fastest path for Medicore to get a short pilot live (1–2 weeks of ingest + 2 weeks evaluation) is if we align on a light MAP that sequences technical acceptance, security evidence, and signature gates.

I pulled together a first-pass MAP (attached) that maps out the:
- Evaluation milestones (data ingest, baseline eval, accuracy check, UAT)
- Contract gating (MSA signature, DPA completion, order form sign-off)
- Procurement items (SOW/PO, billing contact, payment terms)

Can you confirm these constraints from your side:
1) Legal needs a full DPA review by Medicore legal before MSA countersign — true/false?
2) Procurement requires an internal PO number before vendor activation?
3) Any absolute blackout dates for June/July that would block your security team?

- PO: Procurement typically issues PO before we process payment, but IT sometimes allows a temporary vendor onboarding form that lets us stand up test accounts for pilots. I'll check if we can do a temporary onboarding to start ingest without full PO.

source.subject: HealthCore: Tenant-scoped deletion & field-redaction options for PHI workloads
messages:
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Avery / Diego,

Thanks for taking this. HealthCore is evaluating Redwood for a pilot that will ingest clinical chat transcripts, structured vitals, and small document attachments that include PHI. Procurement asked me to capture a few specific requirements before we move to a security review: 

- Can Redwood support per-tenant retention policies (we need different windows per environment)?
- For PHI fields (patient name, MRN, SSN, clinical notes) we need a configurable maximum retention window and an automatic irreversible redaction option once the window expires.
- We require proof-of-deletion exports (auditable report) that we can store off-site for our auditors.
- Are there field-level redaction options (mask tokens / hashed pseudonymization) at ingest vs. deletion-time?
- Any limitations for attachments (PDFs/images) — can they also be redacted/removed on a per-tenant schedule?

We can share a filled questionnaire if that helps. Goal is to understand constraints (latency, costs, and whether this impacts availability SLAs).

Thanks — let me know if a quick call this week helps.

— Sarah
Sarah Kim
Director, Security & Compliance
HealthCore Clinics
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Sarah — appreciate the clarity here. High-level answers and proposed next steps below; attaching a short options brief (redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf) and a questionnaire template you can complete.

Short answers:
- Tenant-scoped retention: yes — per-tenant (org) policy with route-level overrides.
- Configurable windows: yes — arbitrary TTLs per field group (PHI, PII metadata) and per model/route.
- Irreversible redaction: we support irreversible field redaction (crypto-shredding of keys + deletion of token mappings) and a confirmable deletion workflow.
- Field-level options: both — we support ingest-time pseudonymization (hashing, reversible tokenization with KMS) and post-ingest deletion/redaction jobs. Ingest-time reduces footprint and risk but changes downstream analytics.
- Attachments: blobs are supported and can be deleted/zeroed out per schedule; we also offer OCR-based field extraction to allow selective redaction of text inside PDFs (additional processing step).

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Irreversible deletion for encrypted token mappings requires KMS key destruction or secure key-wrapping workflows; that has implications for legal holds and recoverability.
- Proof-of-deletion: we can export signed deletion manifests (timestamped, request-id, checksum-based) and delivery via secure S3 link or push to your SIEM.
- Availability: running aggressive background deletion/redaction jobs at scale can create IO spikes; we can schedule phased canary rollouts and rate-limit background work to preserve SLOs.

Next steps:
1) Can you share the high-level retention windows you expect for each data class (clinical notes, identifiers, attachments)?
2) If you prefer ingest-time pseudonymization vs. post-hoc deletion, tell us which workflows you want for the pilot.
3) We can schedule a 30-min tech call with Diego and Marcus (SRE/product) to walk process and show the deletion manifest format.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Notes on legal / compliance implications for HealthCore ask

Avery / Diego / Marcus — quick internal alignment before we reply with a formal proposal. Key points from legal/compliance perspective: 

1) Retention granularity: support per-tenant and per-route TTLs. We should propose default templates: 
   - PHI (identifiers, clinical notes): 90 days (pilot), configurable up to client-requested maximum;
   - PII-metadata (session IDs, usage logs w/o content): 365 days;
   - Attachments: configurable, default 180 days.

2) Redaction semantics: recommend offering two primary modes to customer: 
   - Ingest-time irreversible redaction: redact fields before they are stored; no keys retained. Use when client needs minimal retention risk.
   - Tokenize-with-reversible-keys: store tokens and KMS-wrapped mappings for cases where client requires later re-identification for support. Provide clear UI toggle and flag for legal_holds.

3) Proof-of-deletion: require support for signed manifests including: deletion_request_id, deleted_object_ids, checksums before/after, deletion_timestamp, operator_id, and canonical audit_hash. We can produce a PKI-signed manifest (service key) that HealthCore can verify. This satisfies auditors.

4) Audit / legal hold: note that destroying KMS keys is irreversible — if HealthCore needs retention for audit (some states require 6–7 years), advise tokenization instead of key destruction. We should surface the option to place objects into a restricted-hold bucket instead of destroying immediately.

5) SLA/Costs: background redaction at scale should be scoped as part of Dedicated / Private deployments. For hosted pilots, rate-limited deletion jobs and a small engineering change reduce risk. We should be transparent about CPU/GPU costs for OCR-based redaction of attachments.

Action: Marcus, can you draft the deletion-manifest schema and a sample export? Diego, please confirm S3/Syslog delivery options and any rate limits we should propose. Avery, I'll craft the compliance language for the proposal.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Thanks — that level of detail helps. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Some of our records (state-level requirements) require a minimum retention window of 7 years for certain records. Can the per-field policy be set to a minimum (e.g., 7 years) if we request that?
- We will likely want ingest-time tokenization for identifiers but keep clinical notes for 90 days then delete. Is Redwood able to support hybrid policies per-field in the same record?
- For proof-of-deletion, we prefer a push to our SIEM (Syslog/TLS) and an encrypted S3 drop for manifests — can you confirm both delivery methods exist?

I've attached the initial completed questionnaire (healthcore-data-retention-questionnaire.xlsx). If helpful, we can take the tech call Thursday morning PT.

— Sarah
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>, HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Sarah — great questions. Quick summary and proposed plan: 

- Minimum / Maximum windows: Yes — per-tenant fields can be configured to any minimum you require, including multi-year windows (we'll note the 7-year option in the proposal).
- Hybrid per-record policies: Supported. You can tokenize identifiers at ingest and set separate TTLs for clinical notes vs. token mappings. Token mappings can be set to retain for the required audit period or destroyed earlier depending on your preference.
- Delivery of deletion proofs: We support both Syslog/TLS push to a customer endpoint and encrypted S3 manifests. We'll include sample manifest payload and PKI signature during the tech call.

Proposed next steps:
1) Tech call Thursday 10:00 AM PT — Diego/Mike (SRE) to demo deletion manifest and show ingestion pipeline options.
2) We will produce a short appendix in the SOW laying out: template TTLs, redaction modes (ingest/post), manifest schema, and delivery options.

If you confirm the Thursday slot I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

On Tue, May 20, 2025 at 10:22 AM Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com> wrote:
> Thanks — Thursday works for us. Looking forward to the demo and the manifest sample.

Thanks everyone — will follow up with the invite.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference

4) Audit / legal hold: note that destroying KMS keys is irreversible — if HealthCore needs retention for audit (some states require 6–7 years), advise tokenization instead of key destruction. We should surface the option to place objects into a restricted-hold bucket instead of destroying immediately.

5) SLA/Costs: background redaction at scale should be scoped as part of Dedicated / Private deployments. For hosted pilots, rate-limited deletion jobs and a small engineering change reduce risk. We should be transparent about CPU/GPU costs for OCR-based redaction of attachments.

Action: Marcus, can you draft the deletion-manifest schema and a sample export? Diego, please confirm S3/Syslog delivery options and any rate limits we should propose. Avery, I'll craft the compliance language for the proposal.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Thanks — that level of detail helps. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Some of our records (state-level requirements) require a minimum retention window of 7 years for certain records. Can the per-field policy be set to a minimum (e.g., 7 years) if we request that?
- We will likely want ingest-time tokenization for identifiers but keep clinical notes for 90 days then delete. Is Redwood able to support hybrid policies per-field in the same record?
- For proof-of-deletion, we prefer a push to our SIEM (Syslog/TLS) and an encrypted S3 drop for manifests — can you confirm both delivery methods exist?

I've attached the initial completed questionnaire (healthcore-data-retention-questionnaire.xlsx). If helpful, we can take the tech call Thursday morning PT.

— Sarah


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>, HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Sarah — great questions. Quick summary and proposed plan: 

- Minimum / Maximum windows: Yes — per-tenant fields can be configured to any minimum you require, including multi-year windows (we'll note the 7-year option in the proposal).
- Hybrid per-record policies: Supported. You can tokenize identifiers at ingest and set separate TTLs for clinical notes vs. token mappings. Token mappings can be set to retain for the required audit period or destroyed earlier depending on your preference.
- Delivery of deletion proofs: We support both Syslog/TLS push to a customer endpoint and encrypted S3 manifests. We'll include sample manifest payload and PKI signature during the tech call.

Proposed next steps:
1) Tech call Thursday 10:00 AM PT — Diego/Mike (SRE) to demo deletion manifest and show ingestion pipeline options.
2) We will produce a short appendix in the SOW laying out: template TTLs, redaction modes (ingest/post), manifest schema, and delivery options.

If you confirm the Thursday slot I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

Avery Johnson

Redwood Inference
text:
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Avery / Diego,

Thanks for taking this. HealthCore is evaluating Redwood for a pilot that will ingest clinical chat transcripts, structured vitals, and small document attachments that include PHI. Procurement asked me to capture a few specific requirements before we move to a security review: 

- Can Redwood support per-tenant retention policies (we need different windows per environment)?
- For PHI fields (patient name, MRN, SSN, clinical notes) we need a configurable maximum retention window and an automatic irreversible redaction option once the window expires.
- We require proof-of-deletion exports (auditable report) that we can store off-site for our auditors.
- Are there field-level redaction options (mask tokens / hashed pseudonymization) at ingest vs. deletion-time?
- Any limitations for attachments (PDFs/images) — can they also be redacted/removed on a per-tenant schedule?

We can share a filled questionnaire if that helps. Goal is to understand constraints (latency, costs, and whether this impacts availability SLAs).

Thanks — let me know if a quick call this week helps.

— Sarah
Sarah Kim
Director, Security & Compliance
HealthCore Clinics


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@healthcore.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-17T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Data retention & deletion for PHI — requirements and ask

Hi Sarah — appreciate the clarity here. High-level answers and proposed next steps below; attaching a short options brief (redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf) and a questionnaire template you can complete.

Short answers:
- Tenant-scoped retention: yes — per-tenant (org) policy with route-level overrides.
- Configurable windows: yes — arbitrary TTLs per field group (PHI, PII metadata) and per model/route.
- Irreversible redaction: we support irreversible field redaction (crypto-shredding of keys + deletion of token mappings) and a confirmable deletion workflow.
- Field-level options: both — we support ingest-time pseudonymization (hashing, reversible tokenization with KMS) and post-ingest deletion/redaction jobs. Ingest-time reduces footprint and risk but changes downstream analytics.
- Attachments: blobs are supported and can be deleted/zeroed out per schedule; we also offer OCR-based field extraction to allow selective redaction of text inside PDFs (additional processing step).

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Irreversible deletion for encrypted token mappings requires KMS key destruction or secure key-wrapping workflows; that has implications for legal holds and recoverability.
- Proof-of-deletion: we can export signed deletion manifests (timestamped, request-id, checksum-based) and delivery via secure S3 link or push to your SIEM.
- Availability: running aggressive background deletion/redaction jobs at scale can create IO spikes; we can schedule phased canary rollouts and rate-limit background work to preserve SLOs.

Next steps:
1) Can you share the high-level retention windows you expect for each data class (clinical notes, identifiers, attachments)?
2) If you prefer ingest-time pseudonymization vs. post-hoc deletion, tell us which workflows you want for the pilot.
3) We can schedule a 30-min tech call with Diego and Marcus (SRE/product) to walk process and show the deletion manifest format.

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Regulated Industries
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-retention-options-v2.pdf


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Notes on legal / compliance implications for HealthCore ask

Avery / Diego / Marcus — quick internal alignment before we reply with a formal proposal. Key points from legal/compliance perspective: 

1) Retention granularity: support per-tenant and per-route TTLs. We should propose default templates: 
   - PHI (identifiers, clinical notes): 90 days (pilot), configurable up to client-requested maximum;
   - PII-metadata (session IDs, usage logs w/o content): 365 days;
   - Attachments: configurable, default 180 days.

2) Redaction semantics: recommend offering two primary modes to customer: 
   - Ingest-time irreversible redaction: redact fields before they are stored; no keys retained. Use when client needs minimal retention risk.
   - Tokenize-with-reversible-keys: store tokens and KMS-wrapped mappings for cases where client requires later re-identification for support. Provide clear UI toggle and flag for legal_holds.

3) Proof-of-deletion: require support for signed manifests including: deletion_request_id, deleted_object_ids, checksums before/after, deletion_timestamp, operator_id, and canonical audit_hash. We can produce a PKI-signed manifest (service key) that HealthCore can verify. This satisfies auditors.

4) Audit / legal hold: note that destroying KMS keys is irreversible — if HealthCore needs retention for audit (some states require 6–7 years), advise tokenization instead of key destruction. We should surface the option to place objects into a restricted-hold bucket instead of destroying immediately.

5) SLA/Costs: background redaction at scale should be scoped as part of Dedicated / Private deployments. For hosted pilots, rate-limited deletion jobs and a small engineering change reduce risk. We should be transparent about CPU/GPU costs for OCR-based redaction of attachments.

Action: Marcus, can you draft the deletion-manifest schema and a sample export? Diego, please confirm S3/Syslog delivery options and any rate limits we should propose. Avery, I'll craft the compliance language for the proposal.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

Cc: HealthCore Procurement <procurement@healthcore.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T10:22:00-07:00

— Sarah
