messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com>
To: Amy Chen <amy.chen@atlashealth.com>, security@atlashealth.com
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Sun, 4 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security exhibit onboarding & audit playbook

Hi Amy / Atlas Security team —

Thanks for the quick turn here. Attaching our initial security exhibit draft and a suggested pen-test window template to get the procurement/legal review started. High-level asks from Atlas that we want to confirm in this thread: SOC 2 report availability (last 12 months), preferred pen-test/inspection notice windows, and the cadence you expect for evidence deliveries (weekly snapshots vs. monthly bundles).

Proposed immediate next steps:
- 30-min kickoff (technical + legal) this week to align scope and evidence handoff.
- We’ll produce a redline to the MSA/order form that names the security exhibit appendix and enumerates data-flow boundaries.
- Draft a bounded pen-test protocol that restricts tests to non-production staging unless prior approval is given.

Attachments: redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v1.pdf; pen-test-windows-proposal.xlsx; evidence-upload-specs.md

Can you confirm which SOC 2 artifacts you need (Type II report + bridge letter?) and suggest a couple of kickoff times?

Thanks — happy to own scheduling.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood-inference.com | +1 415-555-0182

---
(attachment stubs: redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v1.pdf (11 pages), pen-test-windows-proposal.xlsx (spreadsheet), evidence-upload-specs.md (text))

From: Amy Chen <amy.chen@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com>, security@atlashealth.com
Cc: devops@atlashealth.com
Date: Sun, 4 May 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security exhibit onboarding & audit playbook

Ben —

Appreciate the docs. Quick reactions from Legal + Security:

- We will need Redwood to provide the latest SOC 2 Type II report and any SOC bridge letters covering the most recent changes. Please share via our secure upload link.
- For the security exhibit: Atlas typically requires an evidence cadence of weekly snapshots for the 30 days prior to a pen test plus a monthly bundle thereafter. If your platform cannot commit to weekly exports, propose an alternative and we’ll discuss.
- Pen test scope: we prefer tests to be performed against a production-equivalent but isolated environment; we cannot accept uncontrolled testing against customer data. Will your team confirm staging IP ranges and data segmentation controls?

Also copying DevOps to route the technical folks. Legal will share the MSA redline expectations separately (we normally ask for a limited inspection right with a 30-day notice and explicit scope).

Available kickoff slots: Tue 5/6 10:00-10:30 PT or Wed 5/7 13:00-13:30 PT.

Thanks,
Amy Chen
Senior Counsel, AtlasHealth
amy.chen@atlashealth.com

---
(secure upload requested; Atlas attached a pointer, not a file)

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Amy Chen <amy.chen@atlashealth.com>, security@atlashealth.com, devops@atlashealth.com
Date: Mon, 5 May 2025 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security exhibit onboarding & audit playbook

On Sun, May 4, 2025 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the quick turn here. Attaching our initial security exhibit draft ...

Amy / Atlas team — 

Thanks for the clarity. Internally I reviewed the draft and propose the following guardrails we should include in the redline before the kickoff: 

1) Inspection window: limit to single annual inspection per order unless additional inspections are mutually agreed and tied to explicit compliance triggers (e.g., material changes, data-scope expansion).
2) Pen test scoping: require tests against a production-equivalent isolated environment; explicitly exclude any testing that attempts to access customer data or persistent backups. Requests for broader scope require a documented change request, approved by Redwood engineering + security.
3) Evidence cadence: offer an automated monthly export (encrypted) by default plus the option for weekly snapshots for the 30-day pre-test window. Provide a secure upload path (we recommend SFTP with scoped keys or an Atlas secure collector).
4) Timeboxed access: any remote inspection/forensic access is for a defined window (days/hours) with named Atlas contacts and an agreed on-call list.
5) Data minimization & redaction: Redwood will redact customer PII from evidence bundles where required and provide an index of redactions.

I attached a short edit-list to the exhibit (see updated file: redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v1.pdf -> we will produce v2 after intake). Also: we should not accept unlimited, unbounded inspection language in the MSA; instead the exhibit will enumerate artifacts and acceptable evidence formats.

Ben/Marcus — can you confirm engineering can support an isolated staging endpoint with ephemeral credentials for the agreed pen-test window? Once we have that confirmation, I’ll prepare a redline to the MSA + a short mutual action plan for the kickoff.

Laura Bennett
Head, Privacy & Compliance | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com

(attachment: edit-list for exhibit, 2 pages)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
To: Amy Chen <amy.chen@atlashealth.com>, security@atlashealth.com, devops@atlashealth.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 May 2025 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security exhibit onboarding & audit playbook

Hi Amy / Atlas team —

Thanks — confirming the 5/6 10:00 PT kickoff works for Redwood (Ben will host the call). Quick technical confirmations ahead of the meeting: 

- Engineering can stand up a production-equivalent, isolated staging environment and publish the IP ranges and ephemeral credential process by EOD 5/7.
- We will provide the SOC 2 Type II bundle (redacted where appropriate) via the secure upload link you provided by EOD 5/5.
- Redwood accepts the proposed evidence cadence as a starting point: monthly exports by default, weekly snapshots available during the 30-day pre-test window. We will capture this in the exhibit and the MSA redline.

Attached is a draft v2 of the security exhibit that incorporates Laura’s guardrails and our engineering commitments (redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v2.pdf). Marcus will be on the kickoff to walk through the pen-test scaffolding and evidence upload flow.

Mutual action plan (short):
- By 5/5: Redwood uploads SOC2 bundle to Atlas secure collector.
- By 5/7 EOD: Redwood publishes staging IPs + ephemeral credential flow.
- 5/6 10:00 PT: kickoff call (30 mins) — agenda: final scope, notice windows, MSA redline owners.
- Post-kickoff: Redwood to deliver MSA redline with exhibit incorporated (target 5/13).

Looking forward to the call — please confirm the secure upload receipt once you have the SOC docs.

Marcus Lin
Security & Reliability Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com

(attachment stubs: redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v2.pdf (updated), pen-test-windows-proposal.xlsx)

amy.chen@atlashealth.com

---
(secure upload requested; Atlas attached a pointer, not a file)

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Amy Chen <amy.chen@atlashealth.com>, security@atlashealth.com, devops@atlashealth.com
Date: Mon, 5 May 2025 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security exhibit onboarding & audit playbook

On Sun, May 4, 2025 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the quick turn here. Attaching our initial security exhibit draft ...

Amy / Atlas team — 

Thanks for the clarity. Internally I reviewed the draft and propose the following guardrails we should include in the redline before the kickoff: 

1) Inspection window: limit to single annual inspection per order unless additional inspections are mutually agreed and tied to explicit compliance triggers (e.g., material changes, data-scope expansion).
2) Pen test scoping: require tests against a production-equivalent isolated environment; explicitly exclude any testing that attempts to access customer data or persistent backups. Requests for broader scope require a documented change request, approved by Redwood engineering + security.
3) Evidence cadence: offer an automated monthly export (encrypted) by default plus the option for weekly snapshots for the 30-day pre-test window. Provide a secure upload path (we recommend SFTP with scoped keys or an Atlas secure collector).
4) Timeboxed access: any remote inspection/forensic access is for a defined window (days/hours) with named Atlas contacts and an agreed on-call list.
5) Data minimization & redaction: Redwood will redact customer PII from evidence bundles where required and provide an index of redactions.

I attached a short edit-list to the exhibit (see updated file: redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v1.pdf -> we will produce v2 after intake). Also: we should not accept unlimited, unbounded inspection language in the MSA; instead the exhibit will enumerate artifacts and acceptable evidence formats.

Ben/Marcus — can you confirm engineering can support an isolated staging endpoint with ephemeral credentials for the agreed pen-test window? Once we have that confirmation, I’ll prepare a redline to the MSA + a short mutual action plan for the kickoff.

Laura Bennett
Head, Privacy & Compliance | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com

(attachment: edit-list for exhibit, 2 pages)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
To: Amy Chen <amy.chen@atlashealth.com>, security@atlashealth.com, devops@atlashealth.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood-inference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 May 2025 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security exhibit onboarding & audit playbook

Hi Amy / Atlas team —

Thanks — confirming the 5/6 10:00 PT kickoff works for Redwood (Ben will host the call). Quick technical confirmations ahead of the meeting: 

- Engineering can stand up a production-equivalent, isolated staging environment and publish the IP ranges and ephemeral credential process by EOD 5/7.
- We will provide the SOC 2 Type II bundle (redacted where appropriate) via the secure upload link you provided by EOD 5/5.
- Redwood accepts the proposed evidence cadence as a starting point: monthly exports by default, weekly snapshots available during the 30-day pre-test window. We will capture this in the exhibit and the MSA redline.

Attached is a draft v2 of the security exhibit that incorporates Laura’s guardrails and our engineering commitments (redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v2.pdf). Marcus will be on the kickoff to walk through the pen-test scaffolding and evidence upload flow.

Mutual action plan (short):
- By 5/5: Redwood uploads SOC2 bundle to Atlas secure collector.
- By 5/7 EOD: Redwood publishes staging IPs + ephemeral credential flow.
- 5/6 10:00 PT: kickoff call (30 mins) — agenda: final scope, notice windows, MSA redline owners.
- Post-kickoff: Redwood to deliver MSA redline with exhibit incorporated (target 5/13).

Looking forward to the call — please confirm the secure upload receipt once you have the SOC docs.

Marcus Lin
Security & Reliability Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com

(attachment stubs: redwood-security-exhibit-draft-v2.pdf (updated), pen-test-windows-proposal.xlsx)

Subject: New Dedicated demo signup — BrightBridge Analytics

A new self-serve signup has requested a Dedicated capacity demo for BrightBridge Analytics.

Lead details (attached): selfserve-signup.csv
- Name: Jules Parker
- Title: Head of ML Infrastructure
- Company: BrightBridge Analytics
- Email: jules@brightbridge.ai
- Notes: Interested in reserved capacity; target latency <= 80ms p95 at 1k concurrent requests; potential POC Q2.

---
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Jules Parker <jules@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 Mar 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity demo intake — BrightBridge (priority lead)

Hi Jules —

Thanks for signing up and for the detail in the form. I'm Ava from Redwood's SDR team; Ben (AE) is copied. We'd love to get a focused 45–60 minute walkthrough of our Dedicated offering and scope a potential POC.

Quick asks so we make the session useful:
- Can you confirm your target latency (p50/p95) and typical concurrent request profile? You mentioned ~1k concurrent requests in the form; is that sustained or bursty?
- Will you want a short technical deep-dive (kernel/quantization discussion) with one of our engineers?
- Time availability next week (Mon/Tues mornings PT are best for us): we can offer 30/45/60 min slots.

Proposed times (PT):
- Mon Mar 17, 9:00–9:45 or 10:30–11:15
- Tue Mar 18, 8:30–9:15 or 11:00–11:45

I've attached our dedicated-capacity-checklist (quick intake) and a Calendly link if you'd prefer to pick a slot: https://calendly.com/ava-chen/rr-dedicated-demo-60

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0137

---
From: Jules Parker <jules@brightbridge.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: BrightBridge Sales <sales@brightbridge.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 Mar 2025 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity demo intake — BrightBridge (priority lead)

Thanks Ava —

Mon 10:30 PT works for us. A few details up front:
- Target: p95 <= 80ms for text-gen endpoints; p50 typically ~20–30ms.
- Concurrency: %%% We expect 700–1,200 concurrent active requests during daytime peaks, with sustained bursts of ~10–20 min.
- Payload: average prompt + response ~600 tokens.
- Data must stay in our VPC for the POC — can you confirm Private deployment options?

Yes please include a technical engineer. Ben, is that doable for a short deep-dive?

Thanks,
Jules
Head of ML Infrastructure
BrightBridge Analytics
jules@brightbridge.ai

---
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>

Date: Mon, 17 Mar 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity demo intake — BrightBridge (priority lead)

Adding a quick technical checklist before the call so we can tailor the demo: 
- Can you share a sample QPS/arrival curve (CSV) or expected token/sec distribution?
- Any model families you plan to serve (e.g., Llama2-70B, Mistral) and whether you need FP16/INT8?
- Do you require KMS/HSM integration for key management during POC?

On the Private/VPC point: yes, we support Private (VPC) deployments and have a documented onboarding flow. We'll prepare a short slide on network diagrams and isolation controls.

Marcus Lin

marcus@redwood.ai

Calendar invite: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=RR-DEMO-BB-2025-0421
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/contact/BB-2025-0421

Attachments: dedicated-capacity-checklist.pdf (filled by customer during intake), selfserve-signup.csv (original lead).

If anything changes and you need to shift the slot, reply to this thread and we'll reschedule; otherwise looking forward to the discussion.

Thanks,
Ava Chen

source.subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)
source.body:
From: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Hi all — requesting final security/compliance approval on the permissions + audit approach for Incident Bot v2 before we merge the remaining PRs and enable beta for SRE rotation.

Summary of proposed model (details in attached PDF + scope matrix):

1) Service identity + scopes (least privilege)
- Bot runs as service identity: svc-incident-bot
- Two new internal scopes:
  - incident_bot:config_diff:read (customer-safe, redacted output only)
  - incident_bot:telemetry:read_privileged (bounded query templates; tenant-aware guardrails)
- No write scopes. No direct access to raw secret stores.

2) Config diff endpoint access
- Endpoint returns ONLY allowlisted fields by default; everything else is either omitted or redacted.
- Redaction layer runs server-side (not in Slack bot) to prevent leakage if Slack rendering changes.
- For Sev0/Sev1 only: break-glass path allows on-call to request an expanded diff view (still redacted for secrets, but includes more non-secret fields).
  - This requires incident_id + reason, plus elevated on-call role.

3) Privileged telemetry access
- Query service exposes template-based queries (no arbitrary PromQL/SQL).
- Hard limits:
  - time window max 30m (defaults to 15m)
  - max series/rows per query (bounded)
  - per-incident caching + coalescing
  - rate limits + circuit breaker during upstream slowness

4) Audit logging
- Audit event emitted for:
  - every config diff request (including tenant/service identifiers + incident_id)
  - every privileged telemetry request (including template id + bounds)
  - every break-glass elevation attempt (approved/denied)
- Audit events go to SIEM pipeline with retention aligned to current internal standard.

Open items we want sign-off on today:
- Is the break-glass flow acceptable as described (Sev0/Sev1 only, logged, time-limited)?
- Are the audit event fields sufficient for later review/investigation?

Attachments:
- incident-bot-v2-permissions-audit-approval-summary-v3.pdf
- incident-bot-scope-matrix-v2.xlsx

If approved, Ava will update ADR-013 with final language and we’ll proceed with merges for PR-29133 / PR-29140.

Thanks,
Kaitlyn
Authn/Authz & Tenant Isolation
Redwood Inference


---

From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Approved from Audit Logging / SIEM side with 2 conditions:

1) Ensure audit events include immutable identifiers
- actor_type=service
- actor_id=svc-incident-bot (and any delegated user for break-glass)
- incident_id (required)
- tenant_id OR “global”
- resource_type (config_diff | telemetry)
- resource_id (e.g., config namespace + env; telemetry template id)
- decision (allowed/denied)

2) Retention + review hooks
- Route these events into the existing audit stream (same retention policy as other privileged reads).
- Add a weekly automated report for break-glass usage (count + incident_id + approver), at least during beta.

Break-glass flow as written (Sev0/Sev1, time-limited, fully logged) is acceptable.

If you want, I can help review the event schema in PR-29133 before merge.

Kevin
Audit Logging / SIEM Integration
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

+1 to Kevin’s conditions.

Security approval granted for the permissioning approach with the following notes to capture in ADR-013:

- Redaction must be enforced server-side for config diffs (you already called this out) and covered by unit tests (negative tests for known secret patterns and a small allowlist test).
- For telemetry: keep the template list small for beta (avoid “footgun” templates that explode cardinality) and require explicit bounds.
- For break-glass: require an incident severity field (Sev0/Sev1) and make the time-limited role grant explicit (e.g., expires in 60 minutes).

No objections to moving forward.

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:03 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Thanks all — capturing this as final approval to proceed.

Decision:
- Approved to ship Incident Bot v2 with the proposed least-privilege scopes for config diff + privileged telemetry.
- Break-glass is approved for Sev0/Sev1 only, time-limited, and fully audited.

Conditions (will be tracked as merge blockers for beta):
1) Audit event schema includes the identifiers Kevin listed, plus decision=allowed/denied.
2) Add a weekly break-glass usage report during beta.
3) ADR-013 updated to explicitly state:
   - server-side redaction + test coverage requirements
   - telemetry template approach + bounded query constraints
   - break-glass grant expiry (60 minutes) and severity requirement

Next steps:
- Kaitlyn/Ethan: incorporate notes into ADR-013 today.
- Kevin: please take a pass on PR-29133 event fields before we merge.
- Rafael: once merged, we’ll greenlight SRE beta cohort behind the feature flag.

Related work:
- INT-3312 (bot missing scope) should be resolved by the scope rollout + audit events.

Ava
Platform Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

+1 to Kevin’s conditions.

Security approval granted for the permissioning approach with the following notes to capture in ADR-013:

- Redaction must be enforced server-side for config diffs (you already called this out) and covered by unit tests (negative tests for known secret patterns and a small allowlist test).
- For telemetry: keep the template list small for beta (avoid “footgun” templates that explode cardinality) and require explicit bounds.
- For break-glass: require an incident severity field (Sev0/Sev1) and make the time-limited role grant explicit (e.g., expires in 60 minutes).

No objections to moving forward.

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:03 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Thanks all — capturing this as final approval to proceed.

Decision:
- Approved to ship Incident Bot v2 with the proposed least-privilege scopes for config diff + privileged telemetry.
- Break-glass is approved for Sev0/Sev1 only, time-limited, and fully audited.

Conditions (will be tracked as merge blockers for beta):
1) Audit event schema includes the identifiers Kevin listed, plus decision=allowed/denied.
2) Add a weekly break-glass usage report during beta.
3) ADR-013 updated to explicitly state:
   - server-side redaction + test coverage requirements
   - telemetry template approach + bounded query constraints
   - break-glass grant expiry (60 minutes) and severity requirement

Next steps:
- Kaitlyn/Ethan: incorporate notes into ADR-013 today.
- Kevin: please take a pass on PR-29133 event fields before we merge.
- Rafael: once merged, we’ll greenlight SRE beta cohort behind the feature flag.

Related work:
- INT-3312 (bot missing scope) should be resolved by the scope rollout + audit events.

Ava
Platform Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

source.subject: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review
source.body:
From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Team,

I need an exec-level update on RBAC v2 today for the Q1 enterprise readiness review.

Please reply-all with:
1) Current timeline (discovery/design/implementation/rollout) and what is at risk.
2) Top migration risks (Hosted + Dedicated + Private), including likelihood/impact.
3) Compliance implications: what we can credibly claim for SOC 2 / enterprise questionnaires once v2 ships, and what remains “in progress.”
4) Concrete actions (owner + date) to reduce risk, especially deny-by-default rollout + break-glass controls + auditability.

Keep it crisp (bullet points are fine). If there’s something you need from me (resourcing, prioritization, sequencing), ask directly.

Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
CISO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 9:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Aisha + team — status + timeline as of this morning.

Timeline (current plan)
- Discovery & inventory (in progress; week 2/3)
  - Output: canonical permission inventory + endpoint coverage report + naming conventions
  - Tracking: Linear ENG-18241 (inventory) + shared coverage spreadsheet
- Design (starts next week; 3–5 weeks)
  - Output: RBAC v2 design overview + ADRs + audit log schema updates
  - Tracking: PM-642 (PRD + success metrics)
- Implementation (6–10 weeks, overlaps with design)
  - Shared authz library + gateway middleware + endpoint annotations + policy bundle + Console role mgmt MVP
- Rollout & validation (4–8 weeks)
  - Dogfood + pilot accounts (1 Private, 1 Dedicated) behind flags, then staged rollout

Key milestones (targets)
- Permission catalog v1 freeze for implementation: Feb 21
- RBAC v2 design sign-off: Mar 14
- First end-to-end (gateway -> control plane -> audit logging) in staging: Apr 11
- Pilot enablement start: late April / early May (dependent on inventory coverage)

What’s at risk
- Inventory completeness / coverage gates: we’re finding endpoints without consistent checks; needs quick remediation ownership.
- Console role management MVP scope: we need to keep MVP tight (templates + bindings + permission viewer/search) to avoid slipping.
- Private installer bootstrap details: scoping + initial admin bindings need alignment with customer IdP constraints.

Requested decisions from execs (Aisha/Priya)
- Confirm we will ship with deny-by-default on all annotated endpoints, with a staged flag plan (dual-read) to avoid outages.
- Confirm break-glass workflow requirement (time-bound elevation + alerting + post-use review) is non-negotiable for enterprise launch.

I can circulate a 1-pager with the above + owners by EOD.

Talia
Program Manager


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Adding migration risks + mitigations (security eng view). Top 6:

1) “Silent allow” gaps (missing annotations or bypass routes)
- Likelihood: Medium (based on early endpoint audit)
- Impact: High (least-privilege claims undermined)
- Mitigation:
  - Coverage gate in CI: fail if security-relevant endpoints are unannotated (owner: Ethan/Benji, target: Mar 7)
  - Regression tests for critical actions (owner: Leila, target: Mar 21)
  - Explicit hot-path review for streaming routes (we already saw one weak spot in prior work)

2) Deny spikes during migration (customer-facing 403s)
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High (outage-like symptoms + support load)
- Mitigation:
  - Dual-read mode + metrics comparison (RBAC v1 vs v2 decisions) (owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan, target: Mar 28)
  - “Why denied” admin guidance + denial reason codes (owner: Felix/Chloe + Naomi for copy, target: Apr 4)

3) Stale authz decisions due to caching
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: Medium/High (privilege persistence after role change)
- Mitigation:
  - Short TTL + scoped cache keys + invalidate-on-binding-change hooks (owner: Jordan, target: Apr 4)
  - Metrics: cache hit rate + decision age + deny/allow drift (owner: Logan, target: Apr 11)

4) Scope model confusion (org vs project vs env vs cluster)
- Likelihood: High (design still in flux)
- Impact: Medium (overprivileged defaults or blocking workflows)
- Mitigation:
  - Keep v2 scope primitives limited (org + project + environment; cluster only for Private admin) (owner: Naomi + Ines, target: Mar 14)
  - Publish a single scope matrix for top workflows (routing policy edits, audit export, key mgmt) (owner: Naomi/Talia, target: Mar 14)

5) Break-glass misuse / lack of monitoring
- Likelihood: Low/Medium
- Impact: High
- Mitigation:
  - Time-bound elevation, approvals, alerting to SecOps, and mandatory post-use review (owner: Fiona + Marcus, target: Mar 21)

6) Audit log “authz decision” fields incomplete/inconsistent across services
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High for enterprise evidence + investigations
- Mitigation:
  - Schema update + exporter mapping (owner: Kevin, target: Apr 11)
  - Require decision context on deny + allow for sensitive actions (owner: Allison, target: Apr 18)

If helpful, I can turn the above into a risk register format with likelihood/impact scoring + contingency plans.

Marcus
Security Engineering Lead


---

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 11:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Compliance/evidence implications (what we can say vs what’s still “in progress”):

What RBAC v2 enables us to credibly claim (once rolled out to Hosted + Dedicated + Private control plane)
- Least privilege: fine-grained permissions tied to concrete product actions (key mgmt, role mgmt, routing policy edits, audit export, retention settings, private deployment admin ops).
- Deny-by-default enforcement: consistent middleware/interceptor behavior; no implicit “admin can do everything” shortcuts beyond explicit break-glass.
- Separation of duties: built-in role templates (e.g., Security Admin vs Deployment Operator vs Billing Admin vs Read-only Auditor) with explicit permission matrices.
- Auditability of access decisions: audit log entries include subject, action, scope, decision allow/deny, denial reason code, and policy version.
- Evidence artifacts: versioned permission catalog + policy bundle release artifacts + CI coverage reports + integration tests demonstrating enforcement.

What will remain “in progress” / not claimed for v2
- Customer-defined ABAC/policy scripting (out of scope)
- Perfectly granular data-plane action scoping for all runtime inference requests (we’re focusing on control plane + admin actions first)
- Long-tail legacy endpoints not classified as security-relevant until inventory completes (we’ll be explicit about phased coverage)

SOC 2 / questionnaire packaging (Olga/Daniel, please sanity check)
- We should have a standard evidence packet: design overview + ADRs, permission inventory, role template matrix, audit log schema example (allow + deny), break-glass SOP, test coverage summary.
- We should pre-write answers for “how do you ensure authorization is enforced?” and “how do you detect/handle unauthorized access attempts?” including monitoring/alerts.

Open question for Aisha/Priya
- Do we want to require audit log export permissions be separate from “read audit logs” (to prevent bulk export by default)? I recommend yes for regulated accounts.

Naomi
PM, Security & Compliance


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 1:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Agree with the plan and the non-negotiables (deny-by-default + break-glass controls + auditable decisions).

Resourcing / sequencing commitments from Platform:
- Gateway enforcement + control plane endpoint annotations are prioritized over new feature work through March.
- We will staff a “coverage strike team” for the endpoint audit remediation (Benji + Brian + service owners); goal is to eliminate high-risk unannotated admin endpoints before pilots.
- We’ll treat Private bootstrap/admin binding flows as a first-class deliverable (not an afterthought). Vivek’s team will get weekly checkpoints.

On Naomi’s question: yes, we should split “read audit logs” from “export audit logs” and have export default to Security Admin + Auditor only (not Deployment Operator).

If we run into a timeline slip, I’d rather cut Console role editor polish than compromise on enforcement coverage + logging.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 2:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Thanks — this is the right framing. Capturing action items here so we can track them cleanly.

Decisions (confirmed)
- Deny-by-default is required for all security-relevant control plane endpoints (staged via feature flags/dual-read as needed).
- Break-glass must be time-bound + monitored + reviewed.
- Separate permission for audit log export vs view.

Action items
1) Executive 1-pager (timeline + risks + mitigations)
- Owner: Talia
- Due: Feb 7 EOD

2) CI coverage gate for endpoint annotations (no unannotated sensitive endpoints)
- Owner: Ethan/Benji (with Marcus reviewing the initial scope)
- Due: Mar 7

3) Dual-read + comparison metrics dashboard (RBAC v1 vs v2 decision drift)
- Owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan + Logan for metrics
- Due: Mar 28

4) Break-glass SOP + alerting wired to SecOps + post-use review checklist
- Owner: Fiona + Marcus
- Due: Mar 21

5) Audit log schema updates + exporter mapping + example events (allow + deny)
- Owner: Kevin (schema/export) + Allison (standards/consistency)
- Due: Apr 11 (schema/export), Apr 18 (standards requirement)

6) Evidence packet outline for SOC 2 / enterprise questionnaires (what we’ll ship + what’s phased)
- Owner: Olga + Daniel + Naomi
- Due: Feb 21

Connor — please ensure release planning includes explicit rollback steps for deny spikes and clear comms runbook for pilots.

I’ll use this thread to brief leadership. If anything materially changes (timeline slip, critical security gap), escalate same-day.

Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
CISO, Redwood Inference

Aisha + team — status + timeline as of this morning.

Timeline (current plan)
- Discovery & inventory (in progress; week 2/3)
  - Output: canonical permission inventory + endpoint coverage report + naming conventions
  - Tracking: Linear ENG-18241 (inventory) + shared coverage spreadsheet
- Design (starts next week; 3–5 weeks)
  - Output: RBAC v2 design overview + ADRs + audit log schema updates
  - Tracking: PM-642 (PRD + success metrics)
- Implementation (6–10 weeks, overlaps with design)
  - Shared authz library + gateway middleware + endpoint annotations + policy bundle + Console role mgmt MVP
- Rollout & validation (4–8 weeks)
  - Dogfood + pilot accounts (1 Private, 1 Dedicated) behind flags, then staged rollout

Key milestones (targets)
- Permission catalog v1 freeze for implementation: Feb 21
- RBAC v2 design sign-off: Mar 14
- First end-to-end (gateway -> control plane -> audit logging) in staging: Apr 11
- Pilot enablement start: late April / early May (dependent on inventory coverage)

What’s at risk
- Inventory completeness / coverage gates: we’re finding endpoints without consistent checks; needs quick remediation ownership.
- Console role management MVP scope: we need to keep MVP tight (templates + bindings + permission viewer/search) to avoid slipping.
- Private installer bootstrap details: scoping + initial admin bindings need alignment with customer IdP constraints.

Requested decisions from execs (Aisha/Priya)
- Confirm we will ship with deny-by-default on all annotated endpoints, with a staged flag plan (dual-read) to avoid outages.
- Confirm break-glass workflow requirement (time-bound elevation + alerting + post-use review) is non-negotiable for enterprise launch.

I can circulate a 1-pager with the above + owners by EOD.

Talia

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Adding migration risks + mitigations (security eng view). Top 6:

1) “Silent allow” gaps (missing annotations or bypass routes)
- Likelihood: Medium (based on early endpoint audit)
- Impact: High (least-privilege claims undermined)
- Mitigation:
  - Coverage gate in CI: fail if security-relevant endpoints are unannotated (owner: Ethan/Benji, target: Mar 7)
  - Regression tests for critical actions (owner: Leila, target: Mar 21)
  - Explicit hot-path review for streaming routes (we already saw one weak spot in prior work)

2) Deny spikes during migration (customer-facing 403s)
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High (outage-like symptoms + support load)
- Mitigation:
  - Dual-read mode + metrics comparison (RBAC v1 vs v2 decisions) (owner: Kaitlyn/Jordan, target: Mar 28)
  - “Why denied” admin guidance + denial reason codes (owner: Felix/Chloe + Naomi for copy, target: Apr 4)

3) Stale authz decisions due to caching
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: Medium/High (privilege persistence after role change)
- Mitigation:
  - Short TTL + scoped cache keys + invalidate-on-binding-change hooks (owner: Jordan, target: Apr 4)
  - Metrics: cache hit rate + decision age + deny/allow drift (owner: Logan, target: Apr 11)

4) Scope model confusion (org vs project vs env vs cluster)
- Likelihood: High (design still in flux)
- Impact: Medium (overprivileged defaults or blocking workflows)
- Mitigation:
  - Keep v2 scope primitives limited (org + project + environment; cluster only for Private admin) (owner: Naomi + Ines, target: Mar 14)
  - Publish a single scope matrix for top workflows (routing policy edits, audit export, key mgmt) (owner: Naomi/Talia, target: Mar 14)

5) Break-glass misuse / lack of monitoring
- Likelihood: Low/Medium
- Impact: High
- Mitigation:
  - Time-bound elevation, approvals, alerting to SecOps, and mandatory post-use review (owner: Fiona + Marcus, target: Mar 21)

6) Audit log “authz decision” fields incomplete/inconsistent across services
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High for enterprise evidence + investigations
- Mitigation:
  - Schema update + exporter mapping (owner: Kevin, target: Apr 11)
  - Require decision context on deny + allow for sensitive actions (owner: Allison, target: Apr 18)

If helpful, I can turn the above into a risk register format with likelihood/impact scoring + contingency plans.

Marcus
Security Engineering Lead

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Daniel Carter <daniel.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 6, 2025 at 11:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: RBAC v2: exec update + migration risk review

Compliance/evidence implications (what we can say vs what’s still “in progress”):

What RBAC v2 enables us to credibly claim (once rolled out to Hosted + Dedicated + Private control plane)
- Least privilege: fine-grained permissions tied to concrete product actions (key mgmt, role mgmt, routing policy edits, audit export, retention settings, private deployment admin ops).
- Deny-by-default enforcement: consistent middleware/interceptor behavior; no implicit “admin can do everything” shortcuts beyond explicit break-glass.
- Separation of duties: built-in role templates (e.g., Security Admin vs Deployment Operator vs Billing Admin vs Read-only Auditor) with explicit permission matrices.
- Auditability of access decisions: audit log entries include subject, action, scope, decision allow/deny, denial reason code, and policy version.
- Evidence artifacts: versioned permission catalog + policy bundle release artifacts + CI coverage reports + integration tests demonstrating enforcement.

What will remain “in progress” / not claimed for v2
- Customer-defined ABAC/policy scripting (out of scope)
- Perfectly granular data-plane action scoping for all runtime inference requests (we’re focusing on control plane + admin actions first)
- Long-tail legacy endpoints not classified as security-relevant until inventory completes (we’ll be explicit about phased coverage)

SOC 2 / questionnaire packaging (Olga/Daniel, please sanity check)
- We should have a standard evidence packet: design overview + ADRs, permission inventory, role template matrix, audit log schema example (allow + deny), break-glass SOP, test coverage summary.
- We should pre-write answers for “how do you ensure authorization is enforced?” and “how do you detect/handle unauthorized access attempts?” including monitoring/alerts.

Open question for Aisha/Priya
- Do we want to require audit log export permissions be separate from “read audit logs” (to prevent bulk export by default)? I recommend yes for regulated accounts.

Naomi
PM, Security & Compliance

Agree with the plan and the non-negotiables (deny-by-default + break-glass controls + auditable decisions).

Resourcing / sequencing commitments from Platform:
- Gateway enforcement + control plane endpoint annotations are prioritized over new feature work through March.
- We will staff a “coverage strike team” for the endpoint audit remediation (Benji + Brian + service owners); goal is to eliminate high-risk unannotated admin endpoints before pilots.
- We’ll treat Private bootstrap/admin binding flows as a first-class deliverable (not an afterthought). Vivek’s team will get weekly checkpoints.

On Naomi’s question: yes, we should split “read audit logs” from “export audit logs” and have export default to Security Admin + Auditor only (not Deployment Operator).

If we run into a timeline slip, I’d rather cut Console role editor polish than compromise on enforcement coverage + logging.

Priya
